(or Abbaye Sainte-Marie d'Aulps) is a former Cistercian abbey situated at 807 m altitude in the Upper Chablais valley. Founded around 1094 by monks from the abbey of Molesme in Burgundy, it affiliated with the Cistercian order in 1136 and became one of the most powerful monasteries in medieval Savoy. Partially destroyed in 1823 after 700 years of monastic life, its majestic remains of the abbatial church have been listed as a Historic Monument since 1902.

, XQV0Z appeals through its immediate proximity to the city (20 minutes by road) and the diversity of its activities: hiking and mountain biking in summer, alpine and Nordic skiing in winter, educational animal park, summer toboggan on rails, and numerous interpretation trails. It is also a privileged site for paragliding and alpine sunrises. The alpine pastures, where cows and goats graze between June and September, produce traditional mountain cheeses sold in the mountain chalets.

The venue offers a weekly programme rotating with national releases, art-house films in original version (OV) or dubbed (VF) depending on the showing, occasional previews and themed events (festivals, talks with directors, family screenings).

, XQV0Z. The venue is located in XQV1Z (74550), in the Lower Chablais, 10 minutes from XQV2Z and 15 minutes from Lake Geneva, on a large field of several hectares. Professional referees brief safety rules before each game.

, XQV0Z. The venue is set in the XQV1Z valley (74400), at the heart of France's alpinism capital. Ideal as a ski/hike alternative or rainy-day option for resort guests. The site offers games on specially designed fields, with varied scenarios and full equipment provided (full face mask, marker, jumpsuit or chest protector, paintballs). Professional referees brief safety rules before each game.

, XQV0Z. The venue offers 7 individual lanes, allowing up to 21 people to play simultaneously, in the warm atmosphere of a games bar. Each session starts with a safety briefing led by a qualified coach who teaches the proper technique (grip, stance, rotational or no-spin throw), before moving on to various games and challenges to make the experience accessible for beginners and seasoned throwers alike.

, XQV0Z. The venue sits in XQV1Z (74190), facing the XQV2Z range, 10 minutes from XQV3Z, XQV4Z and Saint-Gervais — perfect after a day in the mountains or as a rainy-day alternative. The site offers games on specially designed fields, with varied scenarios and full equipment provided (full face mask, marker, jumpsuit or chest protector, paintballs). Professional referees brief safety rules before each game.

, XQV0Z. XQV1Z is XQV2Z's historic cinema, an art-house/repertory venue nestled at the heart of the valley. It combines mainstream releases, art-house films, classics and mountain-themed programming. , XQV0Z. XQV1Z is XQV2Z's municipal cinema, digitally equipped and open year-round to the XQV3Z community. , XQV0Z. XQV1Z is a classified art-house cinema in central XQV2Z, with 4 screens dedicated to a curated programme: art-house features, documentaries, restored classics, family films in OV. , XQV0Z. XQV1Z is a multiplex in the centre of XQV2Z (Bonlieu), with around 8 auditoriums equipped with digital laser projection and Dolby sound (Atmos in some). , XQV0Z. XQV1Z is a repertory / art-house cinema in central XQV2Z, dedicated to a curated programme: art-house features, documentaries, restored classics and previews. , is a natural waterfall located in the Aravis massif, at approximately 1,164 meters altitude above the Grand-Bornand Chinaillon resort. Fed by the Nant du Chinaillon, a tributary of the Borne, it plunges some thirty meters down a narrow gorge carved out by water. The site is accessible in about twenty minutes' walk from the main Chinaillon road, on a marked trail crossing meadows and beech forest.

, is a natural waterfall located in the commune of XQV0Z, in the hamlet of Chedde, at approximately 660 m elevation. It is formed by the Ugine torrent (locally called the Nant de l'Ugine), which emerges from the slopes of Platé mountain. A rock at the summit divides the waters into two branches that rejoin at the base, creating a characteristic heart silhouette.

, is an artificial lake over one kilometre long covering approximately 50 hectares, located at 547 metres elevation in the Arve valley. Created in the 1970s following excavation of materials needed for construction of the A40 motorway (the White Road), it is today the largest developed lake in the XQV0Z region.

, the XQV0Z stands out for its regular and spectacular flow, particularly impressive during the spring snowmelt. The upper fall, measuring around one hundred metres with a mid-height drop, rebounds on a stone slab creating water sprays that make the surroundings slippery in high flow — accidents have occurred under these conditions. The site was listed in the inventory of natural sites as early as 1914 and is among the most photographed waterfalls in the region. A local legend attributes its water with virtues of virility and fertility, to the point that locals would recently still offer a flask of this water to newlyweds.

From January to March 1944, 465 guerrilla fighters regrouped here under the command of Lieutenant Tom Morel and then Captain Maurice Anjot to receive weapon airdrops intended for the XQV0Z resistance networks. On 26 March 1944, they faced a joint assault by the Wehrmacht and the French Militia: 129 guerrilla fighters and 20 residents lost their lives. The

: it has been inhabited by the same family since its foundation in the 11th century, spanning 23 generations. It is the birthplace of Saint Bernard of Menthon (1008), founder of the Grand-Saint-Bernard hospice and patron saint of alpinists. The castle retains its period furnishings, a library of over 12,000 rare books (including an Encyclopédie by Diderot and d'Alembert), a kitchen with a 15-metre serving hatch carved into the rock, and a chapel dedicated to Saint Bernard.

: it was at the foot of this waterfall that the first inhabitants of Grand-Bornand are said to have met, giving birth to the "Bornandins". This story passed down through generations adds a touch of poetry and enigma to this already impressive natural site. In spring, when snowmelt swells the torrent's flow, the sight is particularly striking. In autumn, the flamboyant colours of the surrounding beech forest add a different charm to the walk.
: its interpretive panels explain 20,000 years of landscape evolution, from the great Quaternary glaciers to today's slowly rising peat bogs.
: lake archaeology, ethnology, navigation on Lake Geneva, border smuggling, costumes, military collections and works by sculptor Marguerite Peltzer.

: seventeen columns of red marble topped with iron crosses and a statue of the Virgin Mary crown the summit. This monumental calvary, created in 1808 by Joseph-Marie Félisaz, a farmer from XQV0Z, marks the route of a Way of the Cross frequented during pilgrimages and solemn festivals for over two centuries. In 1774, an initial oak cross was erected here, destroyed during the Revolution and replaced by this remarkable ensemble.

A 1,100 m² wellness space near Geneva: Roman-Irish circuit, saunas, hammams, heated balneotherapy pool at 33°C, and included access to the aquatic area. Open from age 16.
A 1-hour session costs around €12-16 depending on the slot, plus non-slip socks (~€3, reusable). Duo, birthday, membership and group rates available. 2026 prices to confirm.
A 1-hour session is around €10-15 at full price. Multi-session passes, family/birthday/group deals and off-peak promos are available. 2026 prices to confirm on just-jump.fr.
A 10-minute lakeside walk links the park to the Pâquier, the Jardins de l'Europe, the Pont des Amours and the canals district.
A 14.5 km loop variant by taking the other bank of the Arve on the return. Possible on a mountain bike too, with a few more demanding sections.
A 1h session costs around €15 to €19 depending on slot and day. Non-slip socks (compulsory, ~€3) are a one-off purchase and reusable. Exact 2026 prices to confirm at elevation-indoor.com.
A 2,400 m² tropical water park perched at 1,800 m altitude in the heart of Avoriaz resort, featuring heated pools, lazy river, water slides and an outdoor hot tub with panoramic views of the peaks.
A 2-hectare, 6-metre-deep altitude lake accessible from the gondola in July and August. Pedalos and water play area.
A 20-30 min discovery flight costs around €80-120, a 1h flight around €200-280. 2026 prices to confirm.
A 20-metre waterfall located at 1,233 m altitude on the Dard torrent, which descends from the Pélerins Glacier. Panoramic views of the Aiguilles Rouges and XQV0Z.
A 20-minute walk from the upper station. Mont Blanc reflections on the lake, picnic tables and electric barbecue (plancha) on site.
A 24-hectare natural area on the shores of Lake XQV0Z: ancient forest, centuries-old chestnut trees, lakeside shores, and remarkable biodiversity. Free access year-round.

A 24-hectare sensitive natural area on the shores of Lake XQV0Z: ancient forest, centuries-old chestnut trees, lakeside shores and remarkable biodiversity. One of the rare public sites accessible on the French shore of Lake XQV1Z.
A 25 m sports pool (six lanes), a 17 m leisure pool, a heated paddling pool and a jacuzzi. The wellness area adds sauna, hammam and salt cave.
A 270-meter waterfall overlooking the hamlet of Luzier, ranked as the 3rd highest waterfall in France and the highest in the French Alps. Protected natural site since 1991.
A 3,000 m² courtyard classified as a Historic Monument since 1991, historic space around the keep, accessible during the visit. Historical documents provided at the entrance.
A 30 min to 1h ride costs around €50-80/person. Kennel visits ~€10-15/person. Longer outings on quote. 2026 prices to confirm.
A 30-meter waterfall cascading down a narrow gorge on the Nant du Chinaillon, accessible in 20 minutes' walk from the resort. Legend of the first couple of Bornandins.
A 30-metre waterfall carving turquoise pools, nestled in forest 30 minutes' walk from XQV0Z town centre. Technical trail, winter access prohibited.
A 360° view over Geneva and Lake Geneva, the Jura to the west, the XQV0Z basin to the south-east and, on a clear day, the Mont Blanc range.

A 45-minute tour through the main rooms: chapel, library, historic kitchen, Saint Bernard's oratory, drawing rooms and the Countess's bedroom. Theatrical visits by amateur actors on weekends and holidays in July–August.
A 60-meter waterfall in a spectacular canyon, accessible via a secure trail carved into the cliff face above Lake XQV0Z.
A Direct Drive wheel offers extremely realistic road force feedback, like in a real car. It's the top of sim racing, used by pro drivers to train.
A century later, in

A city-centre cinema, XQV0Z attracts both local and visiting audiences for national and international releases, in VF or OV with subtitles. Comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD/laser projection ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.
A coached session usually costs €20-30 per person depending on the package (single session, birthday, stag/hen, privatisation). Exact 2026 prices to confirm on lancerdehache-annecy.fr.
A dedicated poker room at XQV0Z Grand Casino welcomes Texas Hold'em fans — tournaments and cash games in the evening.
A descent generally costs €5 to €8, with 5/10/day passes at lower per-descent rates. Exact 2026 prices to confirm at the box office.
A descent lasts about 3 to 5 minutes depending on pace. Return to the start is usually by chairlift (~10 min ride up).
A dozen interpretation viewpoints punctuate the trail and explain relief, rocks, vegetation, water and human activity.
A family castle of the de Sales family for over 500 years, now a 4-star hotel and reception venue. Closed to public visits, accessible only for accommodation and events.
A family-friendly historical investigation route starting from Parc Biolley integrates the cascade into a themed tour of notable sites in XQV0Z-Chedde.
A few Gothic wall sections remain of the original 15th-century castle of the marquises of XQV0Z, modified until the 17th century.
A few alpine inns serve food and drinks in summer.
A flat 30-minute to 1-hour walk through flower beds, the perennial border and the rose garden, ideal on summer evenings and during the May-June blooms.
A flight follows a set pattern: very early-morning meet-up (often 5:30-7am depending on the season),
A flight follows the ballooning ritual: very early-morning meet-up (5:30-7am depending on season),
A former 1902 thermal establishment repurposed as an excellent exhibition center, facing Lake XQV0Z. Temporary exhibitions of painting, sculpture and photography.
A free car park is dedicated to the Vuache Nature Trail in XQV0Z. Access by car from XQV1Z or Frangy. The Nature Detective loop starts right next to the car park.
A friendly spot after the round: coffee, food, view over the course.
A full loop of the Jardins de l'Europe along the lake is about 500 m and takes 30 minutes at a leisurely pace. Allow 1 hr or more for a photo stop, a picnic or to reach the Pont des Amours.
A game generally costs €9-12. Bundles of 2 or 3 games at lower per-game rates are available, along with group rates, birthday packages and stag/hen rates. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
A game generally costs €9-12. Bundles of 2 or 3 games at reduced rate are available, along with specific rates for groups, birthdays and stag/hen parties. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
A genuine

A grassy beach bordered by trees and flowering hedges, it offers a spacious and sunny setting throughout the day. The lake bed is sandy with a gentle slope facilitating water access for young children. Three sanitary blocks, free outdoor showers, accessible water entry with free flotation chairs. Lifeguard supervision from noon to 7 p.m. in July–August.

A group half-day outing starts at around €60-100 per person. Private outings and high-mountain ascents are quoted individually (depending on commitment, duration, number of people). 2026 prices to confirm on the website.

A historic XQV0Z institution, XQV1Z focuses on subtitled OV, independent film, talks with directors, themed cycles and participation in the XQV2Z International Animation Film Festival. The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.

A jump generally costs €70-100 per person (2026 indicative prices, to confirm with the operator), with photo/video options often extra. Booking is very strongly recommended several days ahead in July and August, and for groups (stag/hen, seminars). The activity is seasonal (usually May/June to October) and subject to weather: in strong winds, heavy rain or storms, the jump may be postponed for safety. Bring sportswear, closed shoes and ID.

A lake at 790 m altitude at the heart of the Parc des Dronières, 400 m from the centre of XQV0Z. A 2 km lake loop, an animal park, fishing, a tree-top adventure course, an adjacent public pool. Swimming in the lake is not allowed.

A leading cinema in the French Chablais, XQV0Z offers both mainstream programming and an art-house label section with OV films and cinephile events. The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.
A limestone amphitheatre spanning 4 to 5 km, the largest mountain cirque in the Alps with walls 500 to 700 m high and over thirty waterfalls cascading in spring.

A listed arthouse cinema in central XQV0Z, XQV1Z brings together four auditoriums devoted to a sharp programme: arthouse films, documentaries, restored classics and young-audience films in subtitled OV. Regular talkback screenings for the cinephiles of the XQV2Z area.

A local cinema in the heart of the Pays du XQV0Z, the XQV1Z equips several auditoriums with digital projection. Mainstream releases, family films and event operations: a weekly programme for the families of the Arve valley.
A lovely walk through shaded mixed forest between Plan de la Chaux and the quarry, ideal in summer.
A maintained platform offers a clear view of the double heart-shaped waterfall and allows you to observe the play of light in the water mist, particularly at sunrise.

A meeting point for locals and visitors looking for an indoor activity on rainy days or evenings, XQV0Z also programmes the Mountain Film Festival and screenings linked to alpinism. The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.
A monument raised on the site recalls the tragedy of Christmas 1943 and the 32 victims of the massacre carried out by the SS in the castle.
A monumental work by Émile Gilioli (1973) standing 15.20 m high, symbol of hope. Free access. Interior visits with meditative artworks. Photo exhibition on the esplanade.
A mysterious boulder left by the glaciers, whose distant origin is explained on an interpretive panel.
A natural basin at the base of the waterfall forming a pool to cool off in summer. A small stream sheltered by the forest, ideal for children's play.
A natural belvedere over historic XQV0Z: its church, medieval centre, slate roofs and the Giffre valley.

A nature walk in the heart of XQV0Z along the Dranse, on the old way up to the Nyon alpine pastures: 2 hours of easy walking, with panels on the life of the fox, the river wildlife and the geology of the valley. Snowshoe-friendly in winter.
A neighbourhood cinema at the heart of the Pays du XQV0Z, XQV1Z runs several digital screens to offer families in the Arve valley a full programme:

A neighbourhood cinema in the heart of XQV0Z, with digital projection. Mainstream programme, family sessions and special operations (festivals, talkback screenings) shape an offering refreshed every Wednesday.

A next-generation multiplex ten minutes from Geneva. XQV0Z brings together a large IMAX screen, a 4DX auditorium (motion seats with effects), Dolby Atmos auditoriums and several 3D laser screens for fully immersive mainstream releases.
A plant maze inspired by medieval gardens, with over 1,500 varieties of plants spread across five sensory gardens dedicated to taste, smell, touch, sight, and hearing.
A popular spot for schools and holiday centres for educational outings about Alpine fauna. No booking required for free visits.
A prime spot to watch ibex and chamois at dawn. The col lies on an ecological corridor for the bearded vulture, reintroduced to the Aravis. Guided watching days organised in summer.
A quintessential family activity, accessible to accompanied children. Combines with hiking, mountain restaurant, swimming in XQV0Z lake on the way back.

A reference venue for Chablais cinephiles, XQV0Z focuses on subtitled OV, AFCAE-labelled films, talks with directors or specialists, and themed cycles. The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.
A remarkable 7000 m² garden in XQV0Z, near XQV1Z, made of 33 spaces inspired by Middle-Eastern patios. The work of a passionate family. Classified 'Remarkable Garden' by the Ministry of Culture.

A restaurant operates at the Lognan level (2,134 m) during the winter ski season. Facilities at the summit will be part of the new station opening in December 2026. Check the official site for current service availability.
A rock at the top of XQV0Z divides the waters of the Ugine torrent into two branches that form a heart when they rejoin at the base. This distinctive silhouette earned it the Heart Cascade nickname.
A roughly 1.5-hour route through forest, meadows and lakeside shores. Trails partly accessible to people with reduced mobility. Educational interpretation panels along the route.
A seasonal highlight is the
A second trail, visible from the main path, allows visitors comfortable on uneven terrain to get close to the churning waters and pool at the base.
A session costs €20-30 depending on duration and kart type. Multi-session packs, birthday, stag/hen and group packages are available. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
A session is around €30-50 depending on the format (duration, materials, firing, glazing). Multi-session passes and group/birthday rates are available. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
A session lasts 45 to 60 minutes immersed, plus ~10 minutes briefing before and debriefing after. Plan around 1h15 on site.
A setting often used by XQV0Z schools for outings: nature, heritage and local history.
A shaded trail at XQV0Z where each walker can create an ephemeral land-art work (stone, wood, soil) past wooden sculptures — ideal for a family stroll, with the Bougerie pond and picnic tables.

A simple stroll through the Parc de l'Imperial takes 30 to 45 minutes. Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 if you take time to see the aviary and the rose garden and enjoy a lakeside break — or longer if you extend the visit to the beach and the Pâquier.
A single session represents around 10 minutes of track time. A full grand prix (practice + qualifying + final + podium) lasts about 1h30. Allow 30 extra minutes for briefing and kit-up.
A single session represents around 10 minutes of track time. A full grand prix (practice + qualifying + final + podium) lasts around 1h30. Allow 30 extra minutes for briefing and kit-up.
A small shelter ("Erraticaborne") provides cover in case of rain. Free access year-round.
A specific AlpiSki AGM ticket for ski tourers: adult 36.80 €, reduced 31.30 € (desk purchase only). Covers 1 return Plan Joran + 1 one-way Bochard or Herse chairlift.
A standard session lasts about 1h to 1h30, including safety briefing and learning. Plan a little more for group packages with tournament.
A stone boundary marker dated 1579, at the foot of the steps near the church of XQV0Z: that is all that remains today of the seigneurial Château du Vuache, once perched above the village.
A stroll through the Herbularius garden
A succession of small natural pebble beaches connected by maintained trails, bordering the historic Ripaille Castle estate on the wild shores of Lake XQV0Z.
A summer sledge run is accessible from the top of the Pléney. Billed per descent, suitable for families.

A swimsuit (long shorts and burkinis not allowed), clean plastic sandals (or purchase on-site €6) and a coin for lockers. Bathrobes and towels are provided. Bring your own towel for changing rooms if desired.
A traditional 1815 farm turned ecomuseum in XQV0Z: 2,000 utensils, kitchen (cuzna), heated common room (pèle), cowshed, hayloft and bread oven — guided tour by the Guides du Patrimoine.

A trail called « Au fil de l'eau » connects the complex to Arpenaz waterfall (2 km, 30 minutes on foot) via the Luzier footbridge. The site is accessible by cycle path from XQV0Z. Summer shuttle service is available in July–August. Please note: refurbishment works are underway from September 2025 to June 2026 — paths around the fishing and swimming lakes are closed during this period; only the northern lake (windsurfing) and the Arve embankment remain accessible.
A true
A visit to the XQV0Z takes about 1 hour round trip, on a route slightly over 1 km long. The path is flat, with benches along the footbridges.
A40 then N205 Route Blanche towards Chamonix. Follow Montenvers/Mer de Glace then signs for Domaine des Planards. 200-space car park on site (paid).

Ablok is a 100% bouldering gym (ropeless climbing, 4-5 m). Climb Up in XQV0Z offers both bouldering and 18 m lead/top-rope routes. The two complement each other.
Ablok is an indoor bouldering gym in XQV0Z, near XQV1Z: 4-5 m walls over thick mats, regularly refreshed sectors, training board and friendly bar.
Ablok welcomes all levels, from the curious newcomer to the experienced climber chasing demanding circuits.

About 20 minutes' easy walk from the Chinaillon car park, on a marked trail through meadows and beech forest. Free access year-round except in snowy conditions.
About 20 minutes' walk from the trail start at Chinaillon. The path crosses meadows and beech forest on a marked trail.
About 250 km of way-marked trails cover the whole massif — hiking, mountain biking, trail running, from family routes to technical lines.
About 30 min on foot from XQV0Z town centre: climb on steep road (15 min) then forest trail (15 min). Return by same route or loop via Creux Saint-Jean.

Access is free, but a certified mountain guide is strongly recommended for beginners, families with children or anyone not fully self-sufficient. Bureau des Guides de XQV0Z: +33 4 50 63 35 99.
Access is free, but a guide is highly recommended if you are not autonomous, equipped and experienced. Several guide offices offer supervised outings with equipment provided.
Access is free, but a guide is strongly advised if you are not self-sufficient, equipped and experienced. The Bureau des Guides de Sixt offers supervised outings with equipment from €50/person.
Access is free, but a guide is strongly advised if you are not self-sufficient, experienced and fully equipped. The Compagnie des Guides de Saint-Gervais offers supervised outings.
Access is free, but a guide is strongly recommended if you are a beginner or inexperienced. The Bureau des Guides du Grand-Bornand offers group outings (€122/pers) with equipment provided.

Access is free, but a high-mountain guide is strongly advised if you are not self-sufficient, equipped and comfortable with exposure. Allow €80-120 per person equipment included from a Chamonix or Argentière guide office.

Access is free, but a high-mountain guide or climbing instructor is strongly recommended for beginners. Allow €70-100 per person for a half-day at the Saint-Gervais, Chamonix or XQV0Z guide offices, equipment usually included.

Access is from the Chemin de Curalla hamlet in XQV0Z, with a free shaded car park on the left. The approach walk is steep, lasts about 20 minutes and seriously warms up the legs; the return to the car park via the hiking trail takes ~40 minutes. The route is open year-round but is best enjoyed from April to late October, outside residual snow conditions. Mandatory equipment: harness, double energy-absorbing lanyard, helmet; supervision by a high-mountain guide or climbing instructor is strongly recommended for beginners. Minimum height 1.50 m.
Access is generally from age 5, with dedicated slots for ages 3-5 at certain times. Under-8s must be accompanied. Check conditions on site.
Access is generally possible from age 6-8 depending on areas, with adult or in supervised lessons (sometimes "baby climbing"). Specific conditions on atomeclimbing.com.
Access is generally possible from age 6-8 depending on games. Classic arcade cabinets suit all ages; some modern pinball machines may have more adult themes (assess based on your child).
Access is generally possible from age 7-8, provided the child is comfortable wearing the vest. Check the specific conditions on lasermaxx74.fr or by phone.
Access is generally possible from age 8-10 depending on games. The catalogue offers gentle experiences for the youngest and more intense challenges for teens/adults. Check conditions on ereel.fr.
Access is on a free-play pass:
Access is only by a small shuttle boat from Évian harbour. The crossing takes ~10 minutes and is part of the experience. The site is not directly accessible by road.

Access is open only from mid-June to early November, subject to weather conditions. The site is closed from November to March due to frozen trail (dangerous and slippery). Best period: June and September. In June, flow is still powerful and pools well-fed. In summer (July–August), the trail is busier but the site remains calm compared to major regional waterfalls. In September, autumn colours enhance the forest setting. Prefer weekday mornings to avoid canyoning groups.

Access is recommended from May to October depending on snow conditions. In winter, the route is accessible on snowshoes for experienced hikers, particularly from the Plaine-Joux cross-country ski center or the hamlet of La Lanche.
Access is via a marked path from the village of XQV0Z (allow 30-60 min approach walk depending on the start). The guide gives the meeting point.
Access is via a slightly uneven path; some sections are less suitable for strollers or wheelchairs. Contact the town hall for current conditions.

Access is via the Chemin du Maz, partly tarmacked then forest path. The final section is technical: a wooded descent with metal rungs fixed to wet, slippery rocks to reach the waterfall base. An equipped footbridge provides a more accessible elevated viewpoint. The site is closed from November to March for safety reasons (frozen trail).
Access only by a small shuttle boat from Évian harbour (~10 min). A maritime approach that is part of the experience. Boat times match tour slots.
Access possible by bike from XQV0Z via the Luzier road. Departure point for marked mountain biking circuits from the waterfall parking lot.
Access possible by bike or MTB from XQV0Z (5 km uphill, 300 m elevation gain). Marked MTB trails around the lake.

Access possible by bike or mountain bike from central XQV0Z via D338 towards Manche valley (2 km, uphill road). Bike parking at Nyon cable car parking. Marked MTB route 'Green trail 1: Nyon Waterfall Loop' from Pleyney (11 km, 180 m elevation gain).
Access possible by bike to the foot of the trails (Plan Sagran, parking prohibited to cars), then on-foot ascent mandatory. Cycle path along the lake from XQV0Z.
Access possible by bike to the parking lot via the D12 from Thonon or XQV0Z. Last section on foot only (uphill trail not suitable for bikes).
Access possible via the D1005 (shared path). Cycling route from XQV0Z along Lake XQV1Z via XQV2Z and XQV3Z (approximately 12 km). No dedicated cycle lane on the final D1005 section.

Access possible via the Giffre Green Way (family trail following the Giffre river from XQV0Z). Secure itinerary, accessible to bikes and mountain bikes. From XQV1Z town centre, 1.5 km by road or footpath.
Access the site via three hiking trails: from XQV0Z church (village centre), from Mâcheron hamlet (south), or from Le XQV1Z hamlet in XQV2Z. Marked trails with picnic tables.
Access time
Access to Lake Geneva with pebble beach and supervised swimming in summer.
Access to XQV0Z Grand Casino is restricted to adults aged 18 and over, on presentation of ID. Minors are not admitted.
Access to XQV0Z from XQV1Z (~30 min) or Geneva (~1h15) via D902. Snow equipment mandatory in winter. Parking in XQV2Z village; meeting point given at booking.
Access to XQV0Z is by the Super-XQV1Z gondola in XQV2Z village (74390). The few-minute ride is included in the tree-top ticket.
Access to XQV0Z is by the Super-XQV1Z gondola: a few minutes of panoramic ride, included in the park ticket.
Access to XQV0Z lake recreation base: supervised beach in summer, picnic areas, tables, water fountain, giant board games and walking trails.
Access to the Boucles du Coteau in XQV0Z is entirely free: open-access paths, waymarked and maintained by the commune. Checked on 10 June 2026.
Access to the Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne is entirely free. No ticketing. Verified on 10 June 2026.
Access to the Ruins of the St Michel castle is free year-round, but the trail can be impassable in winter depending on snow conditions. Prefer the spring to autumn period.
Access to the XQV0Z – XQV1Z ski area (Portes du Soleil). Blue and red runs, direct link to XQV2Z from the summit.
Access to the botanical trail is free. Only chalet exhibits or guided outings may be charged.
Access to the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is entirely free: it is a remnant visible in the public space of XQV0Z village. No ticketing. Checked on 10 June 2026.

Access to the main building is possible but the sawmill has several levels and stairs. Contact the team to adapt the visit.

Access to the old ramparts of the Château de XQV0Z is free and open year-round. The remains lie in the heart of the village, against the rectory, a few steps from the church and the town hall. No reservation, no fixed hours: simply stroll through the village.
Access to the park is

Access to the park itself is free all season. Each activity offered by providers is charged individually. The exceptional natural setting — flower-filled meadows, fir forests and XQV0Z peaks as a backdrop — makes Pontet a cherished place for relaxation and leisure for families in summer.
Access to the plateau, Monument and historical trail: free. Guided tours Maquis Memory: adult €3, reduced €2, free under 8 and first Sunday of the month.

Access to the ruins of the Château de XQV0Z is free. Guided tours by the Guides du Patrimoine Savoie Mont Blanc are paid — rate to be confirmed depending on format (individual/group). Checked on 10 June 2026.

Access to the waterfall is via a hiking trail from the

Access to the waterfall is via an aerial trail carved into the cliff face, secured by metal barriers refurbished in 2018 and chains. This narrow, sometimes slippery path passes beneath a smaller waterfall before reaching the main cascade. The final section requires crouching under rock and progressing with cable assistance in a humid, mineral environment. The site is also a premier canyoning destination near XQV0Z, with 60-meter rappel descents possible in summer.
Access via XQV0Z Express station
Access via cycle path from Chamonix town centre (5 min). Bike parking available at domain entrance.
Access via the Super-XQV0Z cable car (foot passenger ticket included with the activity in many packages). Summer 2026 season, daily during school holidays.

Accessibility at XQV0Z: partial. Check specific conditions on site. Verified on June 5, 2026.
Accessibility depends on the specific workshop layout (floors, access width, wheel height). We recommend calling ahead to discuss your needs and adapt the welcome.

Accessibility for people with reduced mobility is exemplary: Unikart water-entry chair, dedicated changing rooms, lift to all levels. The snack bar with sunny terrace invites you to savour a gourmet break between swims. Open from December to May in winter, then June-July in spring, the Aquatic Centre welcomes families, experienced swimmers and visitors seeking relaxation in the simple, friendly Alpine atmosphere.

Accessible June to October depending on snow conditions. In winter: ski touring or snowshoeing only. The summit is inaccessible in case of unstable snow.

Accessible activity; health conditions for VR (epilepsy, vertigo) — check.

Accessible after a short forest hike from Chamonix centre (approximately 45 minutes) or a 10-minute walk from the car park on the tunnel access road, the waterfall offers an impressive natural spectacle amid lush greenery. It is the last in a series of cascades where the tumultuous, sparkling water forms a true marvel of nature. An adjoining chalet-café (Chalet du Dard) allows you to enjoy refreshment and rest facing the waterfall, with clear views over the Aiguilles Rouges range, the Bossons Glacier and XQV0Z.
Accessible by bicycle from XQV0Z town centre or from the XQV1Z (Lake Geneva greenway) via XQV2Z/Coudrée (approx. 3 km). Partial cycle paths to XQV3Z town centre.
Accessible by bike from Saint-Gervais via the D902 (15 km, +350 m elevation). Mountain road — mountain cycling experience recommended.
Accessible by bike via the Lake XQV0Z Shoreline cycle path that runs along the French shore. XQV1Z (EuroVelo 17) route nearby.
Accessible by car, bike, motorcycle. Not stroller-friendly (uneven terrain beyond parking). Hiking trails from pass: moderate elevation gain but karst terrain. Hiking shoes recommended.
Accessible by mountain bike from XQV0Z centre via forest trails (about 5 km, moderate elevation). Old Mill Road is suitable for vehicles.
Accessible changing rooms; for wheelchair access ask the club (level courts depending on installation).
Accessible facilities for people with reduced mobility. Free immersion wheelchair (ask lifeguards). Reserved parking spaces and equipped facilities.
Accessible for hiking June to October depending on snow cover. Winter closure (snow, difficult conditions). Refuge open June to September.
Accessible from 7 years old

Accessible from early July to mid-September depending on snow conditions. The lake is frozen or snow-covered for approximately 9 months per year. Check conditions with La Chamoniarde before departing.

Accessible in 30 to 40 minutes on foot from the village of Les Lindarets — known for its free-roaming goats and traditional architecture — the waterfall is a popular spot for hikers and families. The trail winds through forest alongside the river to a viewpoint overlooking the falls. At the base of the waterfall is a natural pool for a refreshing break. The site is also served by the Refuge des Brochaux, open in summer and winter.
Accessible to families with children from age 6-8 depending on games. Ideal for nostalgics, enthusiasts, teens, adults.
Accessible to families with children from age 8-10 depending on games. Catalogue adapted: gentle experiences for the youngest, intense ones for teens/adults.
Accessible to people with reduced mobility with assistance. Slope > 5% but reasonable. Adapted for hearing, mental and visual disabilities.
Accessible vallée Arve XQV0Z
Accessible via Lake Geneva cycle routes.
Accessible via cycle path along the Giffre from XQV0Z (2 km) or from XQV1Z village (500 m). Marked mountain-bike trails in the valley. Mountain-bike wash area on site.
Accessible via the 'Arve Banks and XQV0Z' cycle path, easy route starting from XQV1Z town centre (approximately 2 km).
Accessible via the Lake XQV0Z cycle route (route along the lake between Thonon and Geneva). Bike parking available on-site.
Accessible via the XQV0Z green lane from XQV1Z (1.5 km, 5 minutes) or XQV2Z (3 km, 10 minutes). Dedicated pedestrian and cycle path, safe and flat.

Accessible via the departmental road from XQV0Z town centre (steep climb). Bike parking at the water sports centre entrance and near the play areas. The internal 4 km circuit is suitable for bicycles and balance bikes.
Accessible via the greenway running along Lake Geneva from XQV0Z (XQV1Z route nearby).
Accessible water entry ramp. Free flotation chairs available July–August.
Accessible year-round subject to good weather. Ideal May to October. South-facing exposure allows spring and autumn climbing. Avoid in rain: limestone rock becomes very slippery.

Accessible year-round, XQV0Z offers a peaceful atmosphere away from the bustle of managed beaches. In summer season, measures are taken to ensure cleanliness and tranquility. The site is also highly valued for walks in all seasons, with views of Lake XQV1Z and the Alps.

Accessible year-round, the leisure park is a popular destination in fine weather for swimming, water activities and family walks. In winter, the site becomes a peaceful place to stroll with spectacular views of snow-capped peaks. The lake is managed by XQV0Z municipality with a commitment to sustainable tourism and environmental preservation.
Accessible year-round. Recommended period: May 1 – October 15 (weather and snow conditions permitting).
Accessible year-round. Spring (February-June) ideal for amphibians and migratory birds. Autumn for golden reedbed colors. Prefer mornings for wildlife observation.
Accessible, well-kept course for beginners and confident players. €28 weekday, €34 weekend (9 holes).
Accommodation and event rates available on request from the establishment. No admission fee — the castle no longer offers public visits since 2020.
Accommodation at XQV0Z can be reserved online via the castle's website through the direct-book.com platform, or by phone at +33 (0)4 50 22 42 02. Several period rooms and suites are available.
Accommodation rates at XQV0Z are not publicly displayed. They vary depending on the room, season, and type of stay. Contact the establishment directly for a personalized quote.
Accompanying adults are usually free, but check on site.

According to local legend, it was at the foot of XQV0Z that the first inhabitants of Grand-Bornand are said to have met, giving birth to the "Bornandins". The exact origin of the name remains unclear, hence its mystery.

According to the National Forestry Office, the lake was formed following the Dérochoir rock fall in the 15th century and contains five rocks protruding from its surface. A trail circles the lake overlooking it, accessible in about fifteen minutes in summer. Access is from the Plaine-Joux car park (1,350 m) via an easy forest trail of approximately 30 minutes' walk. In summer, free shuttles run between Plaine-Joux and the lake to reduce vehicle pressure on the site.
According to the Office de Tourisme Fier et Usses, the XQV0Z overlooks the Usses river by 147 m. Wikipedia and other sources indicate ~150 m: a minor estimation difference.
According to the official mk-circuit.com, the track hosts the French karting school accredited by the FFSA: introductory courses for young drivers and stages from beginner to advanced.
Accredited treatments in dermatology, ENT, respiratory tract (covered by Social Security). Innovative programs: post-cancer, long covid, tinnitus, relaxation and flexibility.

All-weather activity
Allow 1 h 30 on the cable, 20 min steep approach from the car park, and 40 min return via the path. Total: about 2 h 30 to 3 h depending on pace and crowding.
Allow 1 h 30 per building, that is around 3 hours to visit both buildings of the Mechanical Music Museum of XQV0Z with the A+B combined ticket.
Allow 1 h 30 to 2 h to explore the 4 spaces of the Nature Museum at XQV0Z (wildlife, bees, teaching garden, conservation orchard).
Allow 1 h 30 to 2 h to visit the PAYSALP Peasant Museum at XQV0Z, depending on engagement with the 1924 classroom and possible participation in the Absurd Game or Explor Games.
Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for a self-guided visit to the XQV0Z, tasting included. The guided tour (€3 supplement) may take a little longer.
Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for a visit to the Granges de Servette Museum at XQV0Z, depending on your interest in contemporary art and the antique-tool collection.
Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for a visit to the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in XQV0Z, depending on projector demonstrations and Philippe Piccot's own stories.
Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for the via ferrata itself (depending on crowding), plus 15-20 min approach walk from Le Buet car park and 10-15 min return. Total: around 2 h to 2 h 30.
Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 to visit the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum of XQV0Z, depending on your interest in precision engineering and the Arve's industrial revolution.
Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 to visit the Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z, plus 1 h to enjoy the Domaine de Guidou park (ponds, trails, lake view).
Allow 1 h to 2 h round-trip to visit the Lappiaz mine alone from the Avoriaz tourist office. 3 to 4 h on the loop to include the Ferme de Séraussaix and the Lil'Stash.
Allow 1 h to 2 h to visit the Vintage Aircraft Museum at XQV0Z, depending on use of the simulator, conversations with the volunteers and your interest in the restoration work.

Allow 1 hour 30 minutes to 2 hours for a round trip from Grépon car park (central Chamonix), including time to view the XQV0Z. From the tunnel access road car park, 30 to 45 minutes is sufficient. Add extra time if you wish to eat at Chalet du Dard.
Allow 1 hr for the green loop of the Boucles du Coteau, up to 2 hr for the full red loop. The duration depends on pace and the number of loops chained together.
Allow 1 hr in free access to explore the ruins of the Château de XQV0Z and the medieval village, and 1 hr 30 to 2 hr on a guided tour with a Guide du Patrimoine Savoie Mont Blanc.
Allow 1 hr to 1 hr 30 for the full Lake XQV0Z Ecomuseum visit with indoor puzzle-game. Longer with the outdoor puzzle trail — a good half-day in all.
Allow 1 to 1.5 h for the guided visit and exhibition. Add 30 min for the outdoor interpretation trail if weather allows.
Allow 1 to 2 hours for a full visit. Keen botanists can easily spend half a day examining the collections.

Allow 1 to 2 hours to visit the temporary exhibition at XQV0Z, depending on your pace. Guided tours last approximately 1 hour (+ €4 in addition to admission). Family visits for children aged 6–12 last 40 minutes.
Allow 1 to 2 hrs to walk the animal trail and the barefoot 'Nature et Sens' loop at the Ferme de Chosal, taking time to watch the animals and read the panels.
Allow 10 to 15 minutes of walking from the parking lot to reach the base of the XQV0Z. On site, plan 1 to 2 hours to enjoy the waterfall, picnic, or swim.
Allow 15 to 20 minutes to find the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache and take a quick tour of XQV0Z village. Longer if combined with a walk in the Pays du Vuache.
Allow 15 to 30 minutes to admire the XQV0Z from outside, photograph the four pepper-pot towers and read about its history. Longer if you combine it with a country walk.

Allow 1h for the Children's Course, 2h for the Junior Course, 2h30 for the Advanced Adult Course, up to 2h for Acro'Filet, 1h30 for the Treasure Hunt including Sensory Forest, 30 min for Hop Up Challenge. For a family half-day at XQV0Z with multiple activities, plan for 3 to 4 hours on site.
Allow 1h to 2h to tour the enclosures while strolling, reading the educational panels and observing the animals. More if you picnic or combine with a Semnoz hike.
Allow 1h30 to 2h round trip to walk the whole trail up to the Cascade de l'Aigle.
Allow 1h30 to 3h on site
Allow 2 h 30 to 3 h 30 round trip from Petit Port car park in XQV0Z-le-Vieux to XQV1Z's summit (1,299 m), depending on pace and breaks. Add 30 minutes if you extend to Mont Veyrier.
Allow 2 h 30 to 3 hours round trip for the Pointe Percée Geological Trail up to the refuge, not counting stops at the interpretation stations. Plan a half day to full day with breaks.
Allow 2 hours return for the Fox Trail to the Cascade de Nyon (+160 m of climb). If you carry on to Lac des Mines d'Or from the Nyon car park, add another 2 hours.
Allow 2 to 2.5 hours total, including welcome, equipment, safety briefing, descent (1–1.5h on water) and return. Full-day or weekend packages combine multiple activities.
Allow 20 to 30 min on site to discover the Ruins of the castle of XQV0Z and read the memorial panels. The stop can be extended with a walk in the village and the Vallée Verte.

Allow 20 to 45 minutes to walk through the remains of the ramparts of the Château de XQV0Z and tour the old village. With an extended walk through the village and its surroundings, allow up to 1 hr 30.
Allow 2–3 hours total: 10 min approach walk, 1h30 to 2h30 for the route depending on level, and 30 to 45 min for descent via trail.

Allow 3 to 4 weeks between the workshop and collecting your pieces: drying (1-2 weeks), first bisque firing (~950 °C), glazing and final firing (~1240 °C for stoneware). The workshop lets you know when the pieces are ready.

Allow 3 to 5 hours for a complete visit to XQV0Z. This time allows you to visit the aviaries (including La Terre des Aigles), attend the 4 daily shows included with your ticket, participate in educational activities and enjoy the playgrounds and dining facilities.
Allow 30 min to 1 h for the Calvary Ermitage Museum alone, and 2 to 3 h combined with the walk up via the 14 chapels from central XQV0Z.
Allow 30 min to 1 h for the Wildlife Museum at XQV0Z alone, and 1 h 30 to 2 h to visit both XQV1Z museums with the combined ticket.
Allow 30 min to 1 h to visit the Cobbler's Museum at XQV0Z, and about 2 h combined with a stroll through the medieval town (arcades, castle, old bridge).
Allow 30 to 45 minutes to complete the Tour of Lac du Môle at a family pace, up to 1 hour with a picnic stop or bird-watching at the lake.
Allow 30 to 45 minutes to discover the remains of the Counts' Castle of Ternier; 1 hr to 1 hr 30 for a circuit combined with the neighbouring Poype de Ternier.
Allow 45 min to 1 hour per village for the XQV0Z Heritage Trail, so 2 to 3 hours to walk Charly, Jussy and Saint-Symphorien in one go. You can also split it across several outings.
Allow 5 to 6 hours in total: 30 min approach, 3 to 4 h via ferrata, and 1 h descent. A full day is necessary.
Allow a minimum of 2 hours to walk around the lake (2.5 km) and enjoy a picnic. For swimming and water activities, allow a half day or even a full day at XQV0Z.
Allow a minimum of 2 to 3 hours for the Historic Trail and visit to the National Monument at XQV0Z. Allow a half-day to full day for longer hikes on the plateau.
Allow about 1 hour for the guided tour of the Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z alone, and 1 h 30 to 2 h combined with the Baroque Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce church.
Allow about 1 hour to walk the 2 km Follow the Fly trail in XQV0Z at a leisurely pace, stopping at each stage.
Allow approximately 1.5 to 2 hours for the round trip from Burzier parking (30 min walk up, time to observe the XQV0Z, 30 min return).
Allow approximately 1.5 to 2 hours total for the round trip from Vallon Lake car park to the XQV0Z: 30 minutes walking each way, plus time to explore the site.
Allow around 1 hr 30 to complete the XQV0Z village walking trail round trip, taking time to sit at the reading and drawing tables.
Allow minimum half a day to enjoy several activities, or a full day to explore the entire site.

Allow ~€50-70 for a 2-hour introductory session, ~€70-90 for a half-day, ~€100-130 for a full day. Children's courses ~€30-50. The price includes clay, use of materials and firing of the pieces. 2026 prices to be confirmed.

An Alpine Coaster ride lasts 3–4 min. Allow 1 hour for 2–3 rides (including queuing in high season). A half-day is recommended to enjoy the amusement park in summer or the slopes in winter, plus a restaurant break.
An arthouse cinema in the heart of XQV0Z, XQV1Z curates a sharp programme: arthouse films in subtitled OV, documentaries, restored classics, previews and talkback screenings throughout the year.

An associative/independent cinema with a human scale, Ciné Actuel mixes mainstream releases, art-house films, subtitled OV and family screenings. Partners with local schools. The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.
An aviary housing several bird species — a popular stop for children at the heart of the park.
An ecological planted pond at the heart of the park, ideal for observing aquatic plants, dragonflies and amphibians from spring to autumn.

An escape route, the 'Sortie à Fred', allows you to exit after the first section (500 m, grade D). Note: this section also has cabled passages, so keep your equipment on. After the footbridge, you are fully committed.

An essential cultural player in the Pays Rochois, the cinema combines mainstream releases, art-house films, family animations and partnerships with local schools. The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.

An event-focused cinema, XQV0Z attracts a French-Swiss audience for IMAX and 4DX blockbusters, preview operations and live broadcasts (opera, concerts, sport). The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.
An external visit to the XQV0Z combines with a discovery of the village of XQV1Z and its church — a small but complete stop in the heart of the XQV2Z.
An hour is enough for the loop; allow 1h30 to 2h to observe from the tower.
An iconic mountain overlooking XQV0Z that peaks at 1,291 metres, offering an exceptional panorama of Lake XQV1Z, the Bauges, Aravis and Mont Blanc from the Sentier des Crêtes.
An iconic section of the GR5 between the Col de Bassachaux and the Rochassons, a wide driveable track accessible to all.
An ideal cultural fallback on bad-weather days during a ski or hiking stay in the Portes du Soleil. Walking distance from the village centre.
An immersive museum at XQV0Z plunging visitors into 19th-century Savoyard life on the shore of Lake XQV1Z: playful trails for ages 2-6, puzzle-games from age 6, independent or family visits.
An individual jump generally costs between €50 and €90 depending on site, height and package. Photo and video are often optional extras. 2026 prices to confirm with operator.
An intro session 1-2h costs around €15-25/person with gear included. For regular classes, the annual FFTA licence is around €150-200/year. 2026 prices to confirm.
An intro session 1h costs around €15-25/person, an improver session a bit more. Half-day or full-day camps ~€30-50. 2026 prices to confirm with the club.

An intro session costs ~€15-25/person with gear included. The annual FFTA licence (regular classes) is around €150-200/year. Booking essential for intro sessions, ideally via the local tourist office or directly with the club. XQV0Z is easily reachable from Évian and Thonon (~5-10 min), with free parking. Comfortable clothes and closed shoes recommended.

An unusual rock block split in two, located at 1,380 m in the Cœur pastures, near the waterfall. 360° viewpoint over the Fiz, Aravis and XQV0Z massifs.

April 18 – June 30: daily 10am–5pm. July 1 – August 31: daily 9am–6pm. September 1–20: daily 10am–5pm. Seasonal opening only (closed October to mid-April). Verified on official site May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

April 19, 2025 to January 4, 2026: Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday and public holidays 10 am–6 pm. Tuesday 2–6 pm outside school holidays, 10 am–6 pm during school holidays. Closed December 25 and January 1. Verified on the official website on May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

April and November: Wednesdays, weekends and public holidays 11am–7pm. Easter and All Saints holidays: Daily 11am–7pm. July and August: Daily 11am–7pm. Last briefing at 5pm. April to October
April to October for cycling and walking. Beautiful colours in June (flowering pastures) and autumn. Upper sections may be snowy in winter.
April to October for the course in best conditions. Practice usable year-round thanks to covered bays.

April to October season. Optimal conditions: morning for calm flights (ideal first flight), late afternoon for thermal flights. Summer: high demand, book early. Activity rescheduled or cancelled in case of strong wind, rain or storms.
April to October · closed in winter

April to October, depending on river flow. Spring (May-June) offers the strongest flows, ideal for hydrospeed and sporty rafting. July-August: calmer waters, perfect for families and canoraft. September-October: variable flow, autumn atmosphere. Booking advised, departures in small groups of 6–8 people maximum.
April to October. May-June: iris and water-lily bloom, soft light. July-August: peak season, booking required. September-October: Lake Geneva autumn, golden light.

April to October. Summer (June–September) offers the best weather but high crowds. Spring and autumn are quieter. In winter, the XQV0Z trail is icy and slippery; reserved for experienced hikers with proper equipment.
April to late October. September-October offer optimal conditions (mild temperatures, reduced crowds). In summer, start early (before 9 am) as the wall faces full south and heats strongly from 11 am.
April to late September
April to late September. May-June offer the best flow (snowmelt, strong sensations). July-August are perfect for families (calmer and warmer water). September is the quieter shoulder season.

April–May–June–Sept–Oct: Friday, Saturday, Sunday and public holidays 1 pm–6 pm (last entry 5:30 pm). July–August (until 6 Sept): daily 10 am–7 pm (last entry 6:30 pm). Exceptional opening Easter holidays (3 Apr–3 May) and All Saints' (17 Oct–1 Nov): daily 1 pm–6 pm. Closed mid-Nov to end Mar (except Christmas Castle). Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — hours subject to change during events (medieval fairs, festivals, Christmas).
Aprons, tools (loops, ribs, sponges) and earthenware or stoneware clay are provided. Wear work clothes that don't mind stains and closed shoes.

At 1,100 m between XQV0Z and Geneva, a family leisure park with summer luge, Tubby, mini-karts, mini-golf and inflatables.
At 1,585 m facing Mont Blanc, the Jaillet summit offers a free panoramic playground, family trails and mountain bike routes — reached from XQV0Z village in minutes.

At 5 place de la Manufacture in XQV0Z, the municipal museum 'Notre Histoire' takes a contemporary look at the history of the town and the Albanais: paintings, prints, sculptures, crafts and industry. 2026 exhibition on town and countryside.

At Grand Piton (1,379 m), you'll find the Bastian Tower built between 1820 and 1830, an orientation table, a magnificent limestone pavement plateau (karst formation), and an engraved cartouche recalling the passage of Lord Byron and Alphonse de Lamartine. Exceptional panoramic view over Geneva, Lake XQV0Z and the Alps.
At Pré-la-Joux, chairlifts may open in summer to reach the higher elevations of the Portes du Soleil area (check the schedule).

At XQV0Z, paid activities include: boat and pedalo rental, ropes course adventure park, equestrian centre, archery, mini golf, golf practice range, donkey hikes, inflatable structures. Rates vary by provider.

At XQV0Z, south of Lake XQV1Z, the Nature Museum reveals XQV2Z's biodiversity across four spaces: naturalised wildlife, a glass-fronted hive and the history of beekeeping, a teaching garden of medicinal plants and a conservation orchard.
At XQV0Z, the P'tit Kid Park welcomes children from age 2 (up to 1.05 m), accompanied by an adult — first thrills in the trees in complete safety.

At XQV0Z, the PAYSALP Peasant Museum plunges you into mountain life between 1850 and 1950: a 500 m² immersive exhibition where a carpenter, a blacksmith and peasants tell their daily story. A 1924 classroom in which to sit your certificat d'études.
At each stop, a question whose answer hides a few metres further on: a playful format to discover the heritage.
At the Chablais Museum (XQV0Z) in 2026: temporary exhibition "Portrait. From painting to the selfie, an art of (re)presenting oneself" — the evolution of the self-portrait.
At the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm, discover the cowshed where cows wintered and the hayloft for storing hay — the practical layout of a 1815 Savoyard farm.

At the Domaine de Guidou in XQV0Z, the Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV1Z retraces more than a century of fire-fighting and rescue: hand pumps, period engines, uniforms, videos and the testimonies of the soldiers of fire.
At the Dronières Lake you can catch carp, roach, rudd, tench, zander, pike, perch and trout. Les Eaux Closes regularly stocks the lake with trout.

At the Ermitage of XQV0Z, the museum opened in 2013 is the interpretation centre for the Calvary — 14 chapels and oratories built between 1840 and 1878 by Father Ambroise Martin, listed as Historic Monuments and on the European register of Sacred Mountains.

At the Espace Carpano & Pons in XQV0Z, this guardian of time of the Arve valley retraces the saga of the peasant clockmakers from the 18th century to the bar-turning revolution. The world's largest collection of escapements.
At the Rochassons summit, an open view over the Vallée d'XQV0Z, the Cornettes de Bise and the Chablais range.
At the Vintage Aircraft Museum at XQV0Z you discover military aircraft (Mirage IIIE), helicopters, gliders and aeronautical pieces undergoing restoration by enthusiast volunteers since 1992.
At the XQV0Z in XQV1Z you can stroll the paths, picnic, enjoy the view of the town and the Giffre valley, and attend the FestiGrat's festival in summer.
At the base of the waterfall, a cross engraved in stone marks the former territorial boundary of Carthusian monks. A signpost with a yellow arrow indicates its exact location.

At the foot of XQV0Z's Old Town, the Marquisats watersports centre brings together the SRVA and the Club des Sports Nautiques: sailing, rowing, paddle on the lake.
At the foot of the slopes, an aquatic centre with indoor pools, an outdoor 50 m Olympic pool, slide, saunas and hammams.

At the heart of the Bourg of XQV0Z, the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum brings together every film projector designed by the Lyon inventors Jean Buisse and Paul Bottazzi between 1946 and 2007 — a one-of-a-kind collection in France, completed by period posters.

At the heart of the XQV0Z resort, the Mechanical Music Museum brings 1,000 instruments of yesteryear back to life: chimes, orchestrions, mechanical pianos, music boxes, automata and phonographs. A musical guided tour unique in Europe.

At the heart of the medieval town of XQV0Z, beneath the arcades of the Place du Trophée, the Cobbler's Museum recounts the story of a village that counted nearly 300 cobblers in 1880. Tools, sewing machines and the clogs of yesteryear.

Blue Flag 2026, a first (Teragir ranking, first year). The last French village before Switzerland, on the Franco-Swiss border. Peaceful, away from the crowds.
Blue Flag since 2009 (confirmed 2026). 7,000 sqm of lawn and sandy banks with paddling pool, T-shaped pontoon, diving board, snack-restaurant. Tiralo on site.
Blue Flag since 2010
Blue Flag since 2020 (confirmed 2026). 3.5 ha of lawn, 50 m Olympic pool, 160 m slide and direct Lake Geneva access via diving boards. Historic Parc Dollfus.

Book directly with the musher via the official website chien-traineau-haute-savoie.fr (contact form) or by phone. In peak winter school holidays, book several days — or even weeks — in advance.
Book online at indianaventures.com (XQV0Z site section) or directly at Beunaz Beach ticket office. Tickets are sold individually for each course. Booking is recommended in peak season (July–August).

Book online at lesthermesevian.com or by phone +33 4 50 75 02 30 (Monday-Saturday 9am-6pm). For conventional cures, a medical prescription is required. Wellness treatments and aquabike/aquarun classes must be reserved in advance at XQV0Z.
Book online at parc.annecy-lacrique.com or purchase on the day at XQV0Z. Online booking is optional, except for special events (Easter and Halloween) where it becomes mandatory.

Book online at passyaccrolac.com (secure payment) or by phone 06 02 66 09 88. Booking is strongly recommended in high season (July-August) and weekends. On-site purchase possible subject to availability. XQV0Z also offers group and birthday packages on prior booking.

Book online at the official website lesaiglesduleman.com (Ticketing section). Tickets can also be purchased directly at the park's ticket offices at the entrance to Domaine du Guidou. Booking recommended during peak season (July–August).

Book online at the official website parcoursaventure-sciez.com or by phone on 04 50 72 51 86 (mobile: 06 70 63 34 73). Purchase is also possible on-site at the XQV0Z ticket office according to availability. Reservation is recommended, especially during peak season.
Book the Super-XQV0Z Gondola online at skipass-chatel.com or buy at the ticket desk on Chemin de Sous le Crêt in XQV1Z village.
Book via the XQV0Z tourist office (lesgets.com, +33 4 50 75 80 80) or directly with the operator. Book several days ahead in peak season (winter school holidays).
Bookable Thu-Mon for parties and seminars (up to 100).
Bookable guided tours: seasonal botanical themes (cherry blossom, rose garden, medicinal plants), 1 to 1.5 hours. Calendar on the official site.

Booking and ticket purchase via XQV0Z or partner platforms (AlloCiné, BilletReduc). Seat selection depending on screen. Collection at kiosk or e-ticket.
Booking at XQV0Z is done by phone at 06 83 19 21 18 or via the contact form on the official website cascadeaventure.wixsite.com. Highly recommended in high season, it is mandatory for canyoning.

Booking at XQV0Z is done online at acro-aventures.com/talloires or by phone on 06 07 56 90 58. Online booking is recommended during peak season (July-August). XQV1Z accepts payment on site (card, cheque, holiday cheques, cash) or online when booking.
Booking at XQV0Z is mandatory and done exclusively online at acro-aventures.com/reignier/. You can book until the last minute. By phone at 06 43 16 27 59, leave an SMS if voicemail.
Booking at XQV0Z: 04 50 93 75 37 / 06 80 14 68 19 or kmb74@hotmail.fr. Recommended for groups and events. Verified on June 5, 2026.
Booking essential

Booking essential (small groups for personalised supervision: 4-8 people max per session). Clay, tools and an apron are provided; wear old clothes and closed shoes. Pieces are ready for collection about 3-4 weeks later. Gift cards available. The workshop occasionally offers themed courses (raku, porcelain, sculpture).

Booking essential for clinics and workshops — schedule on chez-el.com or by phone. Prices are around €30-50 per session depending on the format (duration, materials, firing), with multi-session passes and group/birthday rates. Finished pieces are fired (and glazed if chosen) and returned 2 to 3 weeks later. The studio has tables, wheels, kilns and professional gear. Ideal alongside a day in XQV0Z in rainy weather or in winter.

Booking essential, ideally several days ahead in summer peak. Session prices ~€15-25/person, camps ~€30-50/day, nature course extra. XQV0Z is reachable from XQV1Z in ~50 min, from Geneva in ~1h15. Bring comfortable clothes, closed shoes, cap/sunglasses as needed. The activity is weather-sensitive (rain, strong wind) — sessions may be rescheduled.

Booking essential, subject to pilot availability and weather. Flights cancelled or rescheduled in bad weather (wind, rain, poor visibility). Prices ~€80-120 for 20-30 min, ~€200-280 for 1h. Bring warm clothes (cooler at altitude) and sunglasses. The pilot provides headset and full briefing before take-off.
Booking for XQV0Z is done by phone at 06 76 29 71 04 or by email at cote2000aventure@aliceadsl.fr. Booking is required outside July–August and for all groups.
Booking is advised at XQV0Z, especially in peak season (July–August). Bookings on chatel.com or chatelreservation.com.
Booking is advised in peak season and for kids' workshops. For individual visits, drop-in is possible during opening hours.
Booking is essential, ideally several days ahead in summer peak. Contact the club via the XQV0Z tourist office or its socials.
Booking is essential: these are dedicated slots in private rooms. Book online on issuesecrete.com or by phone, several days or weeks ahead in high season or for groups.
Booking is essential: these are dedicated slots in private rooms. Book online on latelierdesenigmes.fr or by phone, several days or weeks ahead in high season.

Booking is generally not required — access is open for autonomous climbers (with insurance card and autonomy certificate). Expect €15-20 full entry, with student/child rates, gear packs (shoes + harness) and passes (10 entries, monthly, annual) at lower rates. For lessons and clinics, booking is essential. Changing rooms, showers, lockers. Combines well with a day in XQV0Z or a weekend of outdoor climbing in the massif.
Booking is not required for general spa access, but strongly recommended for treatments (72h advance). During high season and weekends, arrive early to avoid crowds.
Booking is online on the club's site (tenniscluballinges.com) or by phone. Cancellation possible under the club's terms.
Booking is online via padel apps (TeniUp, etc.) or directly with the club. Strongly recommended evenings and weekends, when slots fill up fast.
Booking is recommended for individual visits to the Nature Museum at XQV0Z, and required for groups and schools: resa.musee.gruffy@gmail.com or 04 50 77 58 60.

Booking is recommended in July–August and on sunny weekends. The restaurant welcomes cyclists, motorcyclists and walkers without a booking depending on availability, but it can be very busy at lunchtime.
Booking is recommended, especially on weekends, school holidays and for groups/stag/hen/birthdays/seminars. Book online on urbankart-in.com or by phone.
Booking is strongly advised at XQV0Z, especially in high season (July-August) and at weekends, on tactiqaventure.com or by phone on 04 50 32 91 85.

Booking is strongly recommended Friday evenings, weekends and for groups/birthdays. The venue is in Cran-Gevrier, reachable by Sibra bus from central XQV0Z and by bike. Neighbourhood parking nearby. For children's birthdays or stag/hen, tailored packages are offered with possible private area. Audience from age 5-6 for family games, no upper limit.
Booking is strongly recommended in July-August (online at combloux.com/boutique until 10:30 am on the day). Outside school holidays, tickets are available directly on site.
Booking is strongly recommended in July-August because capacity is limited per slot. Out of peak season, you can come more freely, but check the timetable.
Booking is strongly recommended, especially on weekends, school holidays and for groups/stag/hen/birthdays. Book online on onkart.fr or by phone.

Booking is strongly recommended, especially weekends and evenings: it can be done online on lancerdehache-annecy.fr or by phone. Prices vary by package (single session, birthday, stag/hen, privatisation) and generally run around €20-30 per person for a session. The activity is indoor, open year-round and ideal in rainy weather or for evenings. Comfortable clothes and closed shoes recommended; the coaches provide all equipment and ensure safety at all times.
Booking is very strongly recommended on weekends, during school holidays and for groups or birthdays. Mid-week walk-ins may be possible but are not guaranteed.
Booking is very strongly recommended several days ahead in July and August. In June or September, places are easier to find, but plan ahead.
Booking is very strongly recommended several days ahead in July and August. In June or September, you can sometimes find places more easily, but plan ahead.

Booking is via lasergame-evolution.com or by phone — strongly recommended on weekends, during school holidays and for any group. Prices are around €9-12 per game at full rate, with discounts for multi-game packs and groups. The centre has a waiting area, lockers and drinks. Set in a business park in XQV0Z, car access from Geneva via the Mon-Idée or Moillesulaz border crossings is very quick (15-20 min); from XQV1Z, allow 45 minutes via the A41.
Booking is via the club's online platform (often Anybuddy, Doinsport or in-house) or by phone. Several days ahead recommended for evening and weekend slots.
Booking mandatory online on qcterme.com or by phone — essential in high season.
Booking mandatory online or by phone. Confirmation 24-48h before depending on weather.
Booking mandatory online or by phone. Plan well ahead in high season.
Booking mandatory with XQV0Z Tourist Office (+33 (0)4 50 34 40 28). Small groups (10 people max) to preserve the atmosphere.
Booking on issuesecrete.com or by phone, strongly recommended on weekends, school holidays, evenings and for groups. Choice of slot and room.
Booking on lasergame-evolution.com or by phone, strongly recommended on weekends, school holidays and for groups.
Booking on latelierdesenigmes.fr or by phone, strongly recommended on weekends, school holidays, evenings and for groups.

Booking on onkart.fr or by phone, strongly recommended on weekends, school holidays and for groups. Prices are around €20-30 per session depending on duration and kart type, with multi-session packs, birthday packages (cake, party room), stag/hen packages and tailored corporate grand prix on quote. Bar/snack on site, free parking. As an outdoor activity, it mainly runs from April to October — check weather and seasonal opening on the official site.

Booking on urbankart-in.com or by phone, recommended on weekends, school holidays and for groups. Prices are around €20-30 per session depending on duration and kart type, with multi-session packs, birthday packages and group rates. Bar/snack, reception room for birthdays and seminars, free parking. As an indoor activity available year-round, it is an excellent alternative in the rain, summer heat or winter.

Booking recommended for groups, events and high season (July-August). Individual sessions possible without booking depending on availability. Contact 04 50 64 62 90 or book online at kartingrumilly.fr.

Booking recommended on ereel.fr or by phone, especially on weekends, school holidays and for groups. Prices are around €15-25 per person for a 45-60 minute session, with birthday, stag/hen and corporate packages. The venue has several pods and a friendly waiting area. Ideal in rain, summer heat or winter — a perfect alternative for an original afternoon with family or friends.
Booking recommended on weekends, for birthdays and groups.

Booking required as groups are deliberately kept small (4-6 max) for personalised guidance. Aprons and tools provided. Wear work clothes — clay stains. The workshop also has a small shop where you can buy the potters' own pieces, and runs themed workshops (raku, sculpture) during summer.

Col des Pitons

Col des Pitons (1,335 m) is the highest road pass on Le Salève, accessible by car and bike. Grand Piton (1,379 m) is the massif's summit, accessible only on foot from the pass (45 min hike). Bastian Tower stands at Grand Piton.
Col des Pitons (Salève) · Viewpoint & cycling pass

Col des Pitons can be climbed via 6 different routes. The easiest is the south route from XQV0Z (7.5 km, 4.8% average). The hardest is the west route from XQV1Z (11.7 km, 7% average, sections exceeding 12%). Other possible starting points: La Muraz, XQV2Z, Mont Sion, Malbuisson.

Compagnie du XQV0Z publishes live updates on the new 3S cable car construction. Commissioning timeline and site news available at montblancnaturalresort.com.
Compagnie du XQV0Z targets December 2026 for the commissioning of the new Renzo Piano 3S cable car, linking Argentière to Lognan and the 3,275 m summit. Follow progress at montblancnaturalresort.com.

Compared with the boat-towed spot at the Ponton de l'Embarcadère in XQV0Z, the cable park offers a more affordable and more accessible alternative. A one-hour cable session typically allows several jumps and tricks, where a 15-minute boat tow only fits one or two runs. The cable park is therefore the go-to spot for progress-oriented wakeboarders looking for repetition.

Edelweiss and gentians

Edelweiss, gentians, rhododendrons, primulas and many other iconic high-altitude species. The trail is paired with the reserve's welcome chalet (in season), where naturalist guides offer information and guided outings. Panoramic views of the Aiguille Verte, the XQV0Z massif and the XQV1Z valley.

Entry fee
Entry is
Entry is free all year for under-22s and students. A museum committed to cultural access for young people.
Entry is modest, usually €3 to €5, or even free during events or for children. Discounts possible for students, jobseekers and groups. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
Entry to XQV0Z Grand Casino is free. Only games and dining are charged. ID required to enter. Verified 10 June 2026.
Entry to XQV0Z Grand Casino requires no booking, just ID (18+). Booking recommended at Le Trèfle, especially in the evening and at weekends.
Entry to the Calvary Ermitage Museum at XQV0Z is free. The guided Calvary tour with chapels opened is €5.50 full price. Verified 10 June 2026.
Entry to the Casino of Saint-Julien is free of charge. Only games and dining are paid. ID is required to enter (adults only). Checked on 10 June 2026.
Entry to the Casino of Saint-Julien is without reservation, on presentation of valid ID (18+). For the restaurants, booking is recommended, especially for Le Safran at weekends.
Entry to the Casino of XQV0Z is reserved for adults aged 18 and over, on presentation of valid ID. Minors are not admitted, even with an adult.
Entry to the Cobbler's Museum at XQV0Z is free for self-guided visits. Group guided tours are paid, on request from the town hall. Verified 10 June 2026.
Entry to the Granges de Servette Museum at XQV0Z is free. No ticket or booking required to visit this contemporary-art collection. Verified 10 June 2026.
Entry to the Vintage Aircraft Museum at XQV0Z is free. Donations to the Ailes Anciennes XQV1Z association are welcome to fund aircraft restoration. Verified 10 June 2026.
Entry to the XQV0Z is €5.50 full price and €4.50 reduced (students, jobseekers, families). The DUO Abbey + Maison du Fromage ticket is €9. Verified 10 June 2026.
