Escape Game in XQV0Z: several immersive rooms across varied themes (mystery, adventure, mild horror), 60 minutes per session, 2 to 8 players per team. Ideal with family, friends, stag/hen or team-building.
Escape game
Escape game in XQV0Z on Lake XQV1Z: 3 themed rooms, teams of 2 to 6, board-game bar and axe throwing. From €24 per person.
Escape game in central XQV0Z with 7 rooms: Alchimiste, K74, Lys Noir, Allobroges, Grand Tour, Gourmet, Trolldom. 2-6 players, €22-38.

Escape game is the ideal group activity: friends, families (from age 10-12 depending on room), stag/hen, teen and adult birthdays, business seminars and team-building. No special skills required — team spirit, logic, observation and creativity are enough. Before each game, a briefing sets up the universe and rules. During the session, a game master follows the team via camera/intercom and can provide clues. At the end, debriefing around a souvenir photo.
Escape game suitable from age 8-10 depending on the room, with adult supervision. Board-game bar and quiz room open to all.
Escape game: €38/pers as duo, €32/pers for 3, €28/pers for 4-5, €24/pers for a team of 6. Axe throwing, board-game bar and quiz room à la carte (see website).
Escape rooms are suitable from age 8-10 depending on the room, with adult supervision. The board-game bar and quiz room are open to all ages.

Facility

Facing the XQV0Z massif and benefiting from a due south orientation, the site offers ideal aerological conditions for free flight discovery and practice year-round. The takeoff area, covered with synthetic matting since 2018, has been specially designed to accommodate wheelchair-bound pilots. It is also one of the only officially certified hang gliding sites in XQV1Z.

Faverges Castle entered history in 1112, built by the lord Berlion. The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges, a 13th-century round tower, was a strategic post between the counties of Geneva and Savoy. Restored in 2008, it has been listed as a Historic Monument since 1991.

For 18-day conventional cures (rheumatology, digestive conditions, urinary conditions), a medical prescription is mandatory. For free cures, wellness treatments and fitness access, no medical certificate is required, but XQV0Z recommends checking your physical condition with a doctor before any sports activity.
For XQV0Z, plan closed shoes (trainers), sportswear, long hair tied back. Helmets, harnesses and lanyards are supplied by the park.

For XQV0Z, wear sportswear and closed shoes that must cover heel and toes. Crocs, flip-flops and sandals are prohibited. Secure long hair and glasses, remove scarves and jewelry. Equipment (harness, pulley) is provided.
For a guided outing, specific gear (oversuit, helmet with headlamp, harness) is provided. Bring high hiking or mountain shoes, gloves, warm clothes (the cave is ~8-10°C).
For ages 10 and up, the Lake XQV0Z Ecomuseum offers a more advanced puzzle-game — a fine gateway to cultural visits for teenagers.
For an easy walk at XQV0Z, allow 1 hour (Frénalay loop). For hiking to the Prazon refreshment stand: 2h30–3 hours. For Bout du Monde (sector temporarily closed): 4 hours return.
For children aged 3-6, the Lake XQV0Z Ecomuseum offers a dedicated trail: objects to handle, riddles, stagings of Savoyard life.
For experienced hikers: extend via the ridge toward Aiguilles Croches and Col de Véry. High mountain atmosphere with exposed passages.
For families,
For individuals, booking is strongly advised on Friday/Saturday evenings and during school holidays. For groups (birthdays, hen/stag, team building) booking is required with a deposit.
For lead climbing on routes, yes: an autonomy certificate or gym-issued passport is generally required. For bouldering and accompanied access, free access under conditions.
For mountaineers accompanied by a guide, the ascent of the Pointe Percée (2752 m) starts from the refuge  the hiking trail alone stops at the refuge.
For relaxation, the
For rides, the musher drives and you are a passenger. For longer specialised courses (by booking with dedicated coaching), a driving introduction may be offered.
For seasoned walkers, a second, longer and tougher loop complements the family circuit on the same theme.
For the Smugglers' Audio Trail, bring a charged smartphone (and a spare battery), headphones, the paper topoguide, walking shoes and water.
For the history-curious visitor, the
For the keenest: introduction to rope abseiling, longer galleries, easy down-climbs. By booking, level-dependent.
For the short route: at Ajon, at the end of the paved road. For the loop from XQV0Z: in the village center, at the town hall square. Free parking is available in both cases.
For thrill-seekers: acrobatic figures (wingovers, 360) on pilot agreement. Suitable if not motion-sick.

Founded in 1951 by André Caza, a former European diving champion, the leisure complex retains today the pioneering spirit of its founder with the emblematic "orange star" that marks the site. Operated since 1995 by Indiana'Ventures, it offers a range of aquatic, land and aerial activities on a preserved natural site with reed beds and views of Dent d'Oche.

Founded in 2017 by Etienne Andréys with an eco-responsible approach, XQV0Z is part of the multi-activity La Crique site, which also includes a cove-shaped beach (free access), water sports rentals (paddle, kayak, pedalo), and Criq'Bloc (bouldering wall). The park organises seasonal events (treasure hunts, autumn festival, Easter and Halloween activities). Seasonal opening from late March to early November, Wednesdays, weekends and daily during school holidays, from 10am to 6pm.
Fountains and public toilets in the villages crossed (XQV0Z, La Chapelle, Vacheresse).

Four different daily shows in the Eagle Stadium: Lords of the Sky (11:00 and 15:30) featuring 80 birds in free flight, and Birds of the Lake (14:00 and 17:00) dedicated to aquatic raptors with fishing and diving demonstrations.

Free swimming, swimming lessons at all levels (children and adults), baby swimmers, aquagym, aqua-power, aqua-mix, aquabike, aquatic playground, teen first-aid lessons, free-access aquafitness area. Detailed schedule and sign-up on the official site or on +33 4 50 58 12 32.
Free taster sessions, amateur tournaments and discovery days announced on the official site.
Free the first Sunday of the month, September to June
Free trail access
Free trails. Booklet + accessories: €5 at the Alpes du XQV0Z Tourist Office or at the Foyer des Moises.
Free trampolines
Free under 6
Free visit every Wednesday
Free walk along the park's stabilised paths: lawns, mature trees and views over XQV0Z and the XQV1Z peaks.
Free walk among 250 trees and more than 1,000 shrubs: ginkgo biloba, Virginia tulip tree, Corsican pines, giant sequoias. Botanical labels on many species.
Free year-round access
Free year-round walk through the farm: goats, pigs, poultry, rabbits and the donkeys Clarinette and Fripon. No booking required.
Free · Parc Biolley in Chedde (5–10 spaces, unpaved) · 5 min walk to the cascade
Free — Grépon car park (central Chamonix) or car park on the XQV0Z tunnel access road
Free — XQV0Z lake beach parking, direct access to recreation base. Spaces for cars and coaches.
Free, multiple spaces in immediate vicinity (Corzent lake parking areas)
Free-access pumptrack, free play area (slides, swings, wooden trail course). Trampolines and Big Air Bag (paid). Inflatable water structures in summer (Le Splash).
Free-fall from a platform followed by a pendulum swing above the void. Professional supervision and double safety equipment.
Free-jump
Free-jump / foam pit
Free-jump zone with foam pit to learn tricks and cushion landings. Supervision nearby.

Free. 4 tiered parking areas (150 spaces total): P1 (300 m from entrance) and P2 (800 m) reserved for persons with disabilities, families with young children, elderly persons and disabled persons. P3 (1 km) and P4 (1.5 km) for all. Allow 10–30 minutes walking uphill (50–100 m elevation gain). Free shuttle from XQV0Z SNCF station in peak season (12 round trips/day).
Free. Car and trailer parking near the marina.
Free. Car parks at the Sports and Congress Hall (502 route du Palais des Sports).
Free. Chablais or Val d'XQV0Z guide office supervision: €60-100/person, gear included.
Free. Exhibitions/guided outings may be paid.
Free. Large car park along national road D1005 in the Domaine du Guidou. Access to park entrance via a short path between the Fire Museum and the Prehistory Museum.
Free. Large shaded car park in front of the beach (substantial capacity). In peak season, overflow parking: marina and sports complex in village centre.
Free. Multiple car parks in immediate proximity to the park and lakes.
Free. Multiple car parks on site near the lake, animal park, water sports centre and tennis courts. Disabled spaces available.

Free. Multiple car parks on site: Ilettes South car park (2 disabled spaces), central car park and north car park. No motorhomes. Central and north car parks closed during works (Sept 2025–June 2026).
Free. Multiple parking areas on the leisure base. Also accessible on foot from XQV0Z town centre (1.5 km).
Free. No ticket or booking required. Verified 10 June 2026.
Free. No ticketing: trail in free access, land-art creations at will. Verified on 10 June 2026.
Free. Open access. Verified on 10 June 2026.
Free. Paid activities on site: swimming pool, tennis, adventure course (independent providers).
Free. Parking charged from 30 June to 31 August: €3 per day (car), €1 (motorcycle), €2 (bus). Season pass: €20 for 2 registration plates.
Free. Parking on Port Road (500 m from beach), recommended in summer. Two small parking areas immediately nearby, often full.
Free. Parking paid July–August: €3 per day, €1 motorcycle, €2 bus. Season pass €20 for 2 registrations.
Free. Public path with free access. Verified 10 June 2026.
Free. Route with open access. Verified 10 June 2026.
Free. Several car parks at Chinaillon, including the base of slopes car park (Vallée du Bouchet road) and the Mottaz/Arbelay car park.
Free. Supervision by Sixt Guide Office from €50/person in group, gear included.
Free. XQV0Z town centre car parks: Place de la Résistance, ice rink car park. No dedicated waterfall car park.
Free. XQV0Z-Aravis Guide Office supervision: €60-90/person half-day gear included.
Free: Marquisats, Colmyr, Tillier parking lots (full during peak season). Alternative: Hôtel de Ville underground parking (paid, 5–10 min walk)

French (English depending on staff)
French Genevois cycle paths to Geneva and XQV0Z. Bike racks in the centre.
French Karting School (EFK)

French Open paragliding (national competition) took place at Plaine-Joux in 2024. The site regularly hosts competitions and free flight gatherings. Check FFVL calendar and XQV0Z XQV1Z website for upcoming events.

French billiard tables for carom practice, in free or supervised slots. Discovery possible for new players.
French billiards
French billiards (carom)

Fri & Sun 10-12 / 2-7 pm · Wed & Sat 2-7 pm · Mon & Thu 8-10 pm + 10-12 / 2-7 pm · Tue 10-12 / 2-6 pm
Friendly bar with drinks and light food to relax before or after the session.
Friendly bar/snack to grab a drink, a bite or simply chat between climbers.
Friendly bouldering contests, themed nights, holiday courses, occasional promotions. Schedule on the Ablok website and social media.
Friendly contests (bouldering/lead), themed nights, occasional promotions, holiday courses for kids and teens. Schedule on the website and XQV0Z social media.
Friendly tournaments and group outings possible, supervision on request.
Friendly waiting area, lockers, interactive kiosks. Combines with a family day in XQV0Z or return to XQV1Z at the end of the day.
Friendly welcome, waiting room, debriefing with souvenir photo. Free parking. Combines well with a trip to the Lake Geneva shore or XQV0Z.
Frises rabelaisiennes XIXe (visite JEP)
Frogs, common toads, newts, and dragonflies populate the ponds and wet meadows. The trail allows observation, particularly in spring during breeding season.
From 1 July to 30 August, daily from 11:15 AM to 5:45 PM. Rescue posts active during this period.
From 1 May to 31 October, every day, weather permitting.
From 1.40 m, about 3 h 30 of adventure between 2 and 15 m above ground. Over 40 stations: zip-lines, rope bridges, walkways, ladders, Tarzan swing.
From 13 June to 6 September 2026. High season (4 July-30 August): daily 10:30 am-7 pm. Low season: variable hours, see the official site.
From 15 June to 15 September, every day, subject to favourable weather. Off-season, the trail is not maintained.
From 15-30 min (short ride) to 3-4 h (half-day)
From 15-30 min (short ride) to half-day (3-4 h) depending on package.
From 15-30 minutes for a discovery ride, to 1-1.5 hours for an initiation, up to 3-4 hours for a half-day. A kennel briefing usually adds 15-30 minutes.
From 2 to 8 players depending on the room. For larger groups (birthdays, stag/hen, businesses), several rooms can be booked simultaneously.
From 2019 to 2021 the commune of XQV0Z carried out a
From 5 as passenger, 14-16 to drive the team. Families, sporty, enthusiasts.
From 6 yo. Some parent/child slots for younger kids — check schedule.
From 8-10 years depending on canyon. Families, sporty, groups, stag/hen.
From A40 motorway (XQV0Z exit), follow town centre then railway station direction. The aquatic centre is at 82 rue Charly Mottet with free on-site parking. Accessible for disabled visitors.

From April 19, 2025 to January 4, 2026, XQV0Z presents 'Paris-Brussels, 1880-1914', a selection of over three hundred works from a private collection: paintings, drawings, watercolors, prints, illustrated books. Guided visits daily at 14:30.

From Chamonix (20 km, 30 min), pass Argentière and Col des Montets towards XQV0Z. Park at Le Buet train station car park (2 h free, then €3 for 5 h or €5/day). Start of the via ferrata is a 15-20 min walk.
From Chamonix or XQV0Z, follow XQV1Z center. Recommended parking at Bettex gondola (free), then 15 min walk via the XQV2Zet ruins trail to the Thermal Park. Via start behind the thermal baths.
From Cornu Pass, you can continue to Lacs Noirs (2,535 m) by following the yellow trail markers. Allow approximately 1 hour additional round trip from the pass.

From Geneva (45 km, 45 min): take the A40 towards Chamonix, exit 16 Thonon-Ouest, then D1005 towards Évian. From XQV0Z (75 km, 1 h 10): D1508 then D1005. XQV1Z is located on quai Charles-Albert Besson, facing the lake, next to the town hall. Port parking nearby.
From Geneva (Cornavin): TPG tram to Veyrier Douane, then 9 min walk to the cable car. CHF 21 Unireso combo (tram + cable-car return).

From Geneva (~1 h) or XQV0Z (~1h15): A40/A41, exit XQV1Z. The Planpraz gondola base station is in the town centre, walkable from most accommodation. Paid Les Pélerins car park at the entrance to the town.

From Geneva (~25 min by car), take D1005 towards Thonon, XQV0Z exit. Free parking at the marina. By bus, regular Geneva–Thonon service (XQV1Z Port stop). By boat via CGN from XQV2Z or Hermance in summer.

From Geneva (~25 min), take D1005 towards XQV0Z. In Thonon, follow signs for 'Château de Ripaille' or 'Piscine municipale'. Park at the municipal swimming pool parking (facing the entrance), then walk approximately 10 minutes along the trails to reach the beaches.
From Geneva (~35 km), A40 then A411 to XQV0Z, ~40 min. From XQV1Z (~75 km), N203/D1508, ~1h15. Public car parks in the city centre nearby.
From Geneva or Greater Geneva: XQV0Z Express to XQV1Z (terminus). 5 min walk from the station. By bus: TAC line 8 stop Château Bleu.

From Geneva: A1 towards France, exit XQV0Z/St-Julien-en-Genevois (1 km after customs). From XQV1Z: A41 towards Geneva, exit St-Julien-en-Genevois, then D1201 towards XQV2Z/XQV3Z. Free on-site parking.
From June to late September depending on flow. July-August: warmer water, ideal for families. June and September: fewer people, cooler water, magnificent light over the lake.
From June to late September depending on water levels. July-August: warmer water, ideal for families. June and September: fewer people, cooler water, magnificent light.
From May to October
From May to October for classic hiking. In May to witness the transhumance of XQV0Z cattle. In winter (December-March) for snowshoeing, in clear weather.
From May to October, early morning or late afternoon to avoid car traffic. Summer mornings often offer the best visibility of XQV0Z.

From Petit Port, allow 1 hour 30 minutes to 2 hours 30 minutes to climb to the summit of XQV0Z depending on your pace. The complete loop with Mont Baron and return takes 4 to 6 hours. Plan to bring enough water (no water source on the route).
From Pitchounes (from age 2) to Everest (96 games, 14 m high), with continuous safety line for optimal safety. More than 140 varied activities.
From Plaine-Joux car park, allow about 30 minutes' easy forest walk on marked trail. From 14 June to 14 September 2025, free shuttles run every 15–20 minutes between Plaine-Joux and XQV0Z.
From Plan Joran (2,134 m), a summer path (not accessible summer 2026) leads to a viewpoint over the Argentière glacier serac cascade — one of the most dramatic glacier views in the French Alps.
From Planpraz, several climbing and alpine hiking departures into the Aiguilles Rouges massif. Check route conditions at the Chamonix Office de Haute Montagne.
From Talloires (8 km, 7%) or from Montmin/Vesonne
From Thonon ~10 min, from Geneva ~50 min via A40. On-site parking.
From Vérel: 10–15 min (short, minimal elevation) · From Angon: 30 min to 1 hr (300 m elevation gain, steep climb) · From Talloires: 1–1.25 hr (400 m elevation gain) · Fairy Bridge loop: 2.5 hr

From XQV0Z (1 hour), take the A40 towards Chamonix, exit 21 (Chamonix). On arriving in Chamonix, two options: (1) park at Grépon car park (town centre) and walk 45 min to the waterfall, or (2) continue on the XQV1Z tunnel access road and park before the last bend (10 min walk). From Geneva (1 hour 15 min), A40 then Route Blanche to Chamonix.
From XQV0Z (10 km, 15 min) or XQV1Z (15 km, 20 min): follow direction Bernex / Gavot region, then sign "La Beunaz" with orange star. Access via D21 then D52. Free parking on site.

From XQV0Z (10 km, 15 min), follow D1206 towards XQV1Z then Reignier exit, follow Route des Rocailles. From Geneva (20 km, 25 min), take A40 exit 13 XQV2Z then D1206. From XQV3Z (20 km), follow D1206 towards XQV4Z then Reignier exit. A40/A410 interchange 5 km away. Free parking on site.

From XQV0Z (100 km, ~1h20), take the N205 towards Chamonix, exit at XQV1Z no. 27 "XQV2Z Centre" and follow signs for "XQV3Z" on 6 km of uphill minor road. From Chamonix (12 km, ~20 min), take the N205 towards XQV4Z, exit at XQV5Z "Chef-lieu", cross Le Coupeau and follow signs. GPS: 2495 Chemin de Merlet, 74310 XQV6Z. Parking P1–P4 tiered between 300 m and 1.5 km from entrance.

From XQV0Z (12 km, ~15 min), follow the D25 towards XQV1Z. Park in XQV2Z town centre (town hall, 614 avenue de XQV3Z) or at XQV4Z port. Pedestrian access from town centre: follow green marking with yellow arrow towards Bonnatrait, then enter the estate after the last house. From Geneva (~30 km, 40 min via XQV5Z).

From XQV0Z (12 km, ~30 min in summer due to traffic), follow the D909a towards Talloires. Pass through XQV1Z then enter XQV2Z. In the village centre, follow signs for 'Plage' or 'Route de la Plage'. Recommended parking: Port Road (500 m walk). Two small parking areas immediately nearby often full.

From XQV0Z (13 km, 30 min), follow D909a direction Talloires then D42 towards Planfait/Montmin. The park is located in the forest on Planfait forest road, near the paragliding take-off site. Municipal car park on site (paid 2 May to 30 September).

From XQV0Z (15 min) or Chamonix (40 min), follow D43 towards Plaine-Joux. Free Plaine-Joux car park (1,350 m). Then walk downhill (30 min easy forest walk) or take the free summer shuttle (mid-June to mid-September).

From XQV0Z (20 km, 25 min), take the D907 towards XQV1Z then XQV2Z. At the entrance to XQV3Z, follow signs for 'Base de loisirs' or 'Lac aux Dames'. Free parking on site. From Geneva (60 km, 1 hour), A40 exit 18 XQV4Z, then D907.
From XQV0Z (20 km, 30 min) or XQV1Z (25 km, 30 min), follow the D26 then D32 towards XQV2Z or XQV3Z. Free parking in the center of XQV4Z (town hall square) or at Ajon (end of paved road).

From XQV0Z (20 km, ~25 min): take the D14 toward Groisy / XQV1Z. Follow signs for « XQV2Z » in the village of XQV3Z (formerly XQV4Z). The castle is situated on a hill above the village, at 326 chemin du Château. Free parking on site. From Geneva (40 km, ~40 min): A41 exit XQV5Z, then D14 toward Groisy / Thorens.

From XQV0Z (22 km, ~30 min) or XQV1Z (20 km), follow the RD 1201 to XQV2Z town centre. At the town centre roundabout, take the Route des Dronières (first right). The park is 400 m from the town centre. Multiple free car parks on site.
From XQV0Z (25 km, ~30 min), take the D902 towards XQV1Z/Aulps Valley to XQV2Z. From XQV3Z (35 km, ~40 min), take the D902 towards XQV4Z then XQV5Z. Free parking at the abbey.

From XQV0Z (30 km, 35 min), take the D1508 toward Faverges then follow signs for "XQV1Z" via the route des Grottes. From Albertville (15 km, 20 min), take the D1508 toward Faverges then turn at Seythenex following the signs. Free parking on-site. The site is accessible midway between XQV2Z and Albertville, after the Tamié pass.
From XQV0Z (30 km, 40 min), take the D902 towards XQV1Z, then the D907 to XQV2Z. Follow the D907 towards XQV3Z. The abbey is in the village centre, Place du Tilleul. Free parking nearby.
From XQV0Z (30 km, 45 min): D909 via XQV1Z, then D16 via XQV2Z or XQV3Z. Free parking at the pass (can be saturated in high season).

From XQV0Z (30 km, ~40 min), take the D909 via XQV1Z then direction XQV2Z. Cross the village of XQV3Z and follow the sign for « Les Confins » for 5 km on the uphill road. Free Bossonnet parking upon arrival at the lake.
From XQV0Z (30 min), take the D907 towards XQV1Z then XQV2Z. Cross the village and continue 6 km on the Cirque road (D907A) to the terminal parking. From Geneva (1h30), A40 exit XQV3Z then D907.
From XQV0Z (32 km, 40 min): D909 towards XQV1Z. Municipal car park nearby, 1 reserved disabled space.

From XQV0Z (40 min / 25 km): RD909 via XQV1Z, XQV2Z then XQV3Z. Cross the village and climb towards Col des Aravis. Signposted via ferrata car park 4 km past the centre (2nd car park on left going up).

From XQV0Z (40 min): head towards XQV1Z via the D909, then towards Grand-Bornand via the D4. At Grand-Bornand village, continue towards Chinaillon (D4). At the entrance to Chinaillon, the waterfall is signposted on the right. Park in the village car parks (base of slopes or Mottaz/Arbelay). The trail begins discreetly from a bend in the main road, but remains easy to spot thanks to the "Mysterious Cascade" sign.
From XQV0Z (45 min), head towards XQV1Z then XQV2Z via the D909, then take the D55 (Route des Glières) to the plateau. Winding mountain road.

From XQV0Z (50 km, approximately 50 min), reach XQV1Z via the A40 (exit no. 20 XQV2Z). From XQV3Z town center, take the Doran road (D308) which rises behind the church. Follow signs toward Burzier. Pass through the hamlet of XQV4Z and arrive at the end of the paved road at Burzier, where there is free parking. From Geneva (60 km, approximately 1 h), take the A40 toward Chamonix, exit no. 20 XQV5Z, then follow the D308 toward Burzier.

From XQV0Z (7 km, ~10 min), take D1205 towards Chamonix, exit XQV1Z, follow 'XQV2Z Lake Recreation Base'. From Chamonix (~25 km, ~25 min), D1205 towards XQV3Z, exit XQV4Z. Free parking on site. From XQV5Z (~75 km), A41 then A40 towards Chamonix, exit 21 XQV6Z, follow signs 'XQV7Z Lake'.

From XQV0Z (8 km), take D43 towards Plateau d'Assy then Plaine-Joux. From XQV1Z town center, follow Plateau d'Assy then Plaine-Joux. Winding road, terminus at Plaine-Joux station car park (1,360 m), takeoff area adjacent. From XQV2Z (80 km): A41 exit XQV3Z. From Chamonix (30 km): D1205 via XQV4Z.

From XQV0Z (8 km, 12 min) or Chamonix (28 km, 30 min), follow the D1205 towards XQV1Z centre. At XQV2Z roundabout, take the 'Leisure park / Lake of XQV3Z' exit. Car parks marked around the lake (Granges car park to the north, beach car park to the east).

From XQV0Z (8 km, 12 min): reach XQV1Z then follow D13 toward Chedde/Les Soudans. Parc Biolley parking is on Route des Soudans at Joux hamlet. From Chamonix (35 km, 35 min): take N205 toward XQV2Z, exit at XQV3Z, then D13 toward Chedde. From A40 motorway: exit 20 XQV4Z, then D13 toward Chedde.

From XQV0Z (9 km, approx. 15 minutes off-season), follow the west shore of Lake XQV1Z on D909a towards XQV2Z/Faverges. At XQV3Z, turn left at the traffic light towards the beach (signposted). Large free car park in front of beach entrance. Warning: frequent congestion in summer evenings heading back to XQV4Z.

From XQV0Z (90 km, 1 h 15 min), take the A41 then A40 (White Road) towards Chamonix, exit no. 20 XQV1Z-Le Fayet. Follow signs for XQV2Z centre, then 'XQV3Z' or 'Îles Leisure Base'. From Chamonix (20 km, 25 min), take the A40 towards XQV4Z, exit no. 20 XQV5Z-Le Fayet. From Geneva (70 km, 1 h), A40 towards Chamonix via the White Road.
From XQV0Z (A40 exit 17): towards XQV1Z-le-Bas then high-mountain road to Solaison, about 30 min. Free parking at the end.
From XQV0Z (A40 exit 19), take D902 via XQV1Z to XQV2Z, approx. 25 min. From Geneva, A41 then D902 via XQV3Z or N205 via XQV4Z. Chavannes car park (paid) next to the lower station.

From XQV0Z (A40 exit 19), take D902 via XQV1Z to XQV2Z, approx. 25 min. From Geneva, A41 then D902 via XQV3Z or N205 via XQV4Z. Lower station is in the village centre; use XQV5Z village car parks (200 m).

From XQV0Z (about 35 km, 40 mins), take the D902 towards XQV1Z then follow signs for "Nyon Waterfall". From Geneva (60 km, 1 hour), head to Thonon then XQV2Z. The park is accessible via the Old Mill Road (chemin sous le rocher), parking on site.
From XQV0Z (approx. 60 km, 1h), follow A40 then N205 towards Chamonix, exit Saint-Gervais-le-Fayet. Free parking nearby.

From XQV0Z (~10 km, 15 min), follow D907 towards XQV1Z, then continue to Salvagny and XQV2Z (signs). Nant Sec parking in Salvagny or parking at the base of the waterfall if road open (closed in winter after Salvagny). From XQV3Z (~70 km, 1h15), A41 exit XQV4Z, then D902 and D907 via XQV5Z.
From XQV0Z (~10 km, 15 min), take the D907 towards XQV1Z. Drive through XQV2Z village; the leisure park is signposted 500 m from the centre, on the left. Free car parks near the lake.
From XQV0Z (~10 km, 15 min), take the D909A towards XQV1Z/Faverges. At XQV2Z, follow signs for 'La Crique' or 'Digue à Panade' towards the lake shore. Free car park on site (La Crique).
From XQV0Z (~10 km, 15 minutes), take the D25 towards XQV1Z then XQV2Z. The domain is located on the D25 (XQV3Z road) about 1.5 km east of XQV4Z town centre. Free parking on-site (blue zone 4 hours).
From XQV0Z (~10 min), take D1005 towards XQV1Z/Geneva, then follow signs to XQV2Z-sur-XQV3Z. From Geneva (~25 min), D1005 towards Thonon, XQV4Z exit. Free parking at the marina, 650 route du Port.
From XQV0Z (~100 km), A41 then A40 exit XQV1Z, D902 to XQV2Z then Portes du Soleil road, ~1h45. From Geneva (~70 km), A40 exit Thonon, D902 to XQV3Z, ~1h15.
From XQV0Z (~12 km), D1508 to Bellegarde, ~15 min. From Geneva (~35 km), A41 exit XQV1Z-Nord then D1508, ~35 min. Parking nearby.
From XQV0Z (~12 km), D1508 towards Bellegarde, ~15 min. From Geneva (~35 km), A41 exit XQV1Z-Nord then D1508, ~35 min. Free parking on site.

From XQV0Z (~12 km, 20 min), take the D909a towards Albertville/Faverges, cross XQV1Z, then follow the "Château de Menthon" sign on the left before XQV2Z. Narrow but well-marked road, free parking on arrival.

From XQV0Z (~12 km, 20 min): take the D25 toward XQV1Z. From Geneva (~25 km, 30 min): A40 exit 15 Vallée Verte, then D25. The village is pedestrian-only. Paid parking approximately 300 m from the garden.
From XQV0Z (~15 km): direction Bellegarde via D1508, exit XQV1Z — 20 min. From Geneva (~45 km): A41 exit XQV2Z then D1508 to XQV3Z — 45 min. Free parking.
From XQV0Z (~15 km, 20 min), follow D25 towards XQV1Z then XQV2Z. From Geneva (~25 km, 30 min), take direction XQV3Z then XQV4Z. Large car park at beach entrance, Avenue de la Plage.
From XQV0Z (~15 km, 20 min), take the D14 or D1508 towards XQV1Z then XQV2Z. Follow local 'Parc des Jardins' signs. On-site car park.

From XQV0Z (~15 min), follow the D1005 towards Geneva/XQV1Z. The park is located in Domaine du Guidou in XQV2Z, between Thonon and the Swiss border. Free parking along the D1005. From XQV3Z (~50 min), take the A41 then the D1005 towards Thonon and continue towards XQV4Z.

From XQV0Z (~20 km): east-bank lake road (D909a) to XQV1Z, follow signs for beach and paragliding landing — 30 min. From Geneva (~60 km): A41 exit XQV2Z-Sud, then D1508 and D909a to XQV3Z — 1h. Parking at XQV4Z beach.
From XQV0Z (~22 km), D909 via Alex and XQV1Z, ~30 min. From Geneva (~70 km), A41 XQV2Z exit then D909, ~1h. Free on-site parking.
From XQV0Z (~22 km), D909 via Alex and XQV1Z, ~30 min. From Geneva (~70 km), A41 XQV2Z exit then D909, ~1h. Free town-centre parking.
From XQV0Z (~25 km), A41 then A40 exit 16 XQV1Z, ~25 min. From Geneva (~35 km), A40 exit 16, ~30 min. Public car parks nearby.
From XQV0Z (~30 km), A41 exit 18, ~25 min. From Geneva (~30 km), A40 exit 16 XQV1Z then D903, ~30 min. Town-centre parking.
From XQV0Z (~30 km), A41 exit Saint-Julien then D1206 to XQV1Z, ~30 min. From Geneva (~15 km), A40 or Saint-Julien crossing, ~15-20 min. Free on-site parking.
From XQV0Z (~30 km), D909 to XQV1Z then D12 to XQV2Z and La Croix-Fry, ~40 min. From Geneva (~70 km), A41 exit XQV3Z then as above, ~1h15. Resort parking.

From XQV0Z (~30 km, 35 min), follow D902 towards XQV1Z. On arrival in XQV2Z town centre, follow signs for 'Palais des Sports' or 'XQV3Z'. Free car parks on-site. From XQV4Z (~30 km, 35 min), take D902 then D338 towards XQV5Z.
From XQV0Z (~32 km, ~45 min), D909 then D909A to XQV1Z via XQV2Z. From Geneva (~70 km, ~1h15), A41 exit XQV3Z-Nord then D909/D909A.
From XQV0Z (~35 km), A41 exit XQV1Z, ~30 min. From Geneva (~30 km), A40 exit La Roche, ~30 min. Free on-site parking.
From XQV0Z (~35 km), A41 then exit XQV1Z, ~40 min. From Geneva (~25 km), towards XQV2Z then XQV3Z, ~30 min.
From XQV0Z (~35 km): A41 then A40 towards Chamonix, exit 18 XQV1Z, follow XQV2Z — 35 min. From Geneva (~45 km): A40 towards Chamonix, exit 18 XQV3Z — 40 min. Free car park on site.
From XQV0Z (~35 km): D909 then D12 to XQV1Z, ~45 min. From Geneva (~70 km): A40 exit XQV2Z, then D902/D909, ~1h15. Parking at the meeting point.

From XQV0Z (~35 km, 45 min), take D902 toward XQV1Z, then D228 toward XQV2Z and Les Lindarets. From XQV3Z (~25 km, 35 min), D902 then D228. Free parking at the village entrance. In July–August, traffic restrictions to Les Lindarets: park at Ardent car park and walk up (45 min) or use Ardent cable car + free shuttle train.
From XQV0Z (~40 km), A41 exit 13 Saint-Julien / XQV1Z, ~30 min. From Geneva (~15 km), A40 exit Bardonnex / Saint-Julien, ~15 min. Large free on-site car park.

From XQV0Z (~40 min), reach XQV1Z via the D26 through Vailly and XQV2Z. In XQV3Z, follow the direction "Lac de Vallon" (D32) to the hamlet of La Chèvrerie. Two free car parks available at Vallon Lake. The trail starts near Saint-Bruno chapel, at the western end of the lake.
From XQV0Z (~45 km), A41 exit 14 XQV1Z, ~35 min. From Geneva (~10 km), Pont du XQV2Z + A40 XQV3Z exit, ~20 min. Town-centre car parks.
From XQV0Z (~50 km), A41 then A40 XQV1Z exit, ~45 min. From Geneva (~55 km), A40 XQV2Z exit, ~45 min. Free parking in front.
From XQV0Z (~50 km), A41 then XQV1Z exit, ~45 min. From Geneva (~15 km), via Mon-Idée or Moillesulaz crossings, ~15-20 min. Parking on site.
From XQV0Z (~50 km): A41 then A40 towards Chamonix, exit XQV1Z/XQV2Z — 45 min. From Geneva (~25 km): A40 exit Findrol/XQV3Z — 25 min. Free car park on site.
From XQV0Z (~55 km, ~50 min), A41 then A40 exit XQV1Z, then D1205 to XQV2Z. From Geneva (~70 km, ~1 h), A40 exit XQV3Z-Centre, then D1205.
From XQV0Z (~7 km), D14/D1508 to XQV1Z, ~10 min. From Geneva (~40 km), A41 exit XQV2Z-Nord then XQV3Z, ~35 min.
From XQV0Z (~70 km), A41 then A40 exit 21 XQV1Z, ~50 min. From Geneva (~80 km), A40 exit 21 XQV2Z, ~1 h. Free parking or street parking nearby.
From XQV0Z (~70 km), A41 then A40 exit Bellegarde, D1005 to Thonon, ~1h15. From Geneva (~40 km), Lake Geneva shore via XQV1Z, ~40 min.
From XQV0Z (~70 km): A41 then A40 then A410, exit XQV1Z — 1h. From Geneva (~40 km): Chablais motorway A40, exit Thonon — 40 min. Street parking or public car park.
From XQV0Z (~70 km): A41/A40/A410 exit Thonon, follow XQV1Z — 1h05. From Geneva (~45 km): A40 exit XQV2Z then D903 — 50 min. Free parking.
From XQV0Z (~75 km), A41 then D1005 along the lake, ~1h15. From Geneva (~35 km), A40 exit XQV1Z then D1005, ~45 min. Town-centre paid car parks.
From XQV0Z (~75 km), D1005 along the lake or A41 + A40, ~1h-1h15. From Geneva (~35 km), A40 then D1005, ~45 min. Town-centre car parks.
From XQV0Z (~75 km, ~1h10), A41 then A40 exit Saint-Gervais/Le Fayet, then D902 to the site. From Geneva (~75 km, ~1 h), A40 exit Le Fayet then D902.
From XQV0Z (~80 km), A41/A40 exit Bellegarde then D1005 to Thonon, ~1h15. From Geneva (~45 km), Lake Geneva shore via XQV1Z, ~50 min. Free parking on site.
From XQV0Z (~80 km): A41/A40 exit XQV1Z, follow XQV2Z then XQV3Z — 1h15. From Geneva (~75 km): A40 exit XQV4Z, follow XQV5Z — 1h05. Resort parking.
From XQV0Z (~85 km, ~1h30), A41 then A40, exit XQV1Z/Le Fayet, then D902 and D1005 to Évian. From Geneva (~50 km, ~1 h), A40 then D1005 along the south shore of Lake Geneva.
From XQV0Z (~95 km), A41 then A40 exit 28 Chamonix, ~1h15. From Geneva (~85 km), A40 exit 28, ~1h. Town-centre paid car parks or free park-and-ride shuttle.
From XQV0Z (~95 km): A41 then A40 towards Chamonix, exit Chamonix-Sud — 1h15. From Geneva (~85 km): A40 exit Chamonix-Sud — 1h. Free car park on site.
From XQV0Z (~95 km): A41 then A40 towards Chamonix, exit Chamonix-Sud, follow signs to QC Terme — 1h15. From Geneva (~85 km): A40 towards Chamonix, exit Chamonix-Sud — 1h. Free car park on site.

From XQV0Z center (~5 km), head toward the hamlet of Luzier to the north. Follow the « XQV1Z » signs. Developed parking 1 km after the village of Luzier. Visible from the A40 motorway and national highway.

From XQV0Z center, head toward Mont d'Arbois – Airfield, then follow the Cote 2000 road to its end, past the airfield. Free parking in front of park entrance. Drive time approximately 10 minutes from XQV1Z center.

From XQV0Z center, head toward the hamlet of Luzier to the north of the town. Pass through Luzier and continue for approximately 1 km. Free parking is available at the base of the XQV1Z. Allow 5 km from the town center.

From XQV0Z centre (~5 min), take the D1205 towards XQV1Z, at the roundabout take the 2nd exit onto Luzier road, follow the D313 to Ancienne Route Impériale. Ilettes South car park signposted. From the A40, exit 20 XQV2Z, same route.

From XQV0Z centre or Balcon Sud, several cycling/mountain bike routes lead to the park via forest trails. Passable road by bike (6 km uphill, 500 m elevation gain). Reduced entrance fee for cyclists who hiked or cycled from the valley (code to remember en route).

From XQV0Z centre, follow the cycle path along the lake (Voie Verte du XQV1Z) towards XQV2Z, then join Petit Port via the D909 (approximately 7 km from the centre). Bike racks at the hiking departure point.
From XQV0Z centre: 10 min by car north on the D1201 / D14 to XQV1Z. From the A41: exit 17 XQV2Z Nord / XQV3Z, then follow the business zone signs.
From XQV0Z church, you cross the old bridge, pass the library to reach rue Bidon, then drop under the road via a footbridge and follow the path along the Dranse.
From XQV0Z or Chamonix, exit XQV1Z then follow 'Chemin de Curalla' signs in the village. Free car park at the end of the road on the left.

From XQV0Z or Chamonix, head to XQV1Z. Le Fayet station is on the D902 main road with free parking. Saint-Gervais station (792 m) is in the village. From Geneva via A40 toward Chamonix, exit Saint-Gervais–Le Fayet (~55 min).
From XQV0Z or Geneva: routes to be checked once the exact location is confirmed on the official site.

From XQV0Z or XQV1Z, follow D907 (winding road, light traffic on weekdays, transit circulation on weekends). Approximately 10 km from XQV2Z, 400 m elevation gain. Mountain bike route possible via Fonts path (technical).

From XQV0Z or XQV1Z, head to XQV2Z (D902), then to Avoriaz via the mountain road (D338, ~12 km, 20 min, series of hairpin bends). Park at Prodains car park (+ cable car) or Avoriaz car park (paid, car-free resort). Tracked shuttle buses from car parks to the resort centre. XQV3Z is located in the village centre, within walking distance of all accommodation.
From XQV0Z town centre, follow signs for 'XQV1Z'. Lower station at 821 route du Jaillet, just outside the village. Free parking 7 h (disc required). From XQV2Z ~15 min via D1212.

From XQV0Z town centre, follow the D1205 towards XQV1Z/XQV2Z. Turn towards Lintre-Phiterne via Sainte-Anne road (D113). Car park at the start of the Pont de la Flée trail. From XQV3Z, allow 50 minutes via XQV4Z (approximately 60 km).
From XQV0Z via D13 (8 km, gentle elevation). Bike parking at Parc Biolley.
From XQV0Z ~30 km / 50 min via D909. From Geneva ~70 km / 1h15 via A41/A410/D909. Resort car parks.

From XQV0Z, XQV1Z is accessible by car via the D41 in ~20 min from the roundabout near the Basilica of the Visitation. SIBRA runs a dedicated bus line and free shuttles in July-August, equipped for MTB in summer and skis in winter.
From XQV0Z, follow signs for 'Château de Ripaille' or 'Piscine municipale'. Park at the municipal swimming pool parking, then walk approximately 10 minutes along the trails to access XQV1Z's beaches.
From XQV0Z, follow the D907 towards XQV1Z. Stop at the free Gorges des Tines car park, just before the sharp hairpin bend (GPS: 46.056009, 6.757989). Cross the main road and follow Via Ferrata signs.

From XQV0Z, head to Chinaillon then follow the D4 towards Col de la Colombière. Via ferrata car park on the left on the way up, approx. 2 km before the pass (well signposted). GPS: 45.983139, 6.465222. Free parking, but very busy in summer: arrive early or park at the col car park.
From XQV0Z, take the D16 towards XQV1Z (10 min). Free parking at XQV2Z stadium, near the skate park (trail starting point).
From XQV0Z, take the D902 towards XQV1Z/XQV2Z. At the Bioge tunnel, follow the D22 towards XQV3Z. At the entrance to XQV4Z, parking on the left at the foot of the quarry (via ferrata sign).
From XQV0Z, take the D910 then the D31 towards Vallières-sur-Fier. 5–10 minutes. Free parking on site.

From XQV0Z, take the Doran road (D308) behind the church and follow signs toward Burzier to the parking area at the end of the road. Allow approximately 30 minutes walk from Burzier to reach the XQV1Z.
From XQV0Z, the Counts' Castle of Ternier is reachable on foot or by bike (≈ 2 km) via rural lanes. By car, free parking in the hamlet of Ternier. No direct public transport.
From XQV0Z/Le Fayet ~10 min via the Arve valley. From Chamonix ~20-25 min via A40. Free parking.
From XQV0Z: 10 min via XQV1Z. From Geneva: A40, about 5-10 min. Free parking.
From XQV0Z: A41 + A40 to XQV1Z then D1005 to Thonon, about 1h15. From Geneva: D1005 along Lake Geneva, about 50 min. Follow 'La Grangette' signs.
From XQV0Z: A41 + A40 to XQV1Z, then D1005 to Thonon, then D902 to XQV2Z, about 1h30. From Geneva: D1005 along Lake Geneva then D902, about 1h15.
From XQV0Z: A41 + A40 to XQV1Z, then D1005 toward Thonon, about 1h15. From Geneva: D1005 along Lake Geneva to Thonon, about 50 min. Amphion access via the N5 at XQV2Z.
From XQV0Z: A41 + A40 to exit 28 XQV1Z/XQV2Z, then D43 to XQV3Z, about 1h. From Geneva: A40 to exit 28 XQV4Z/XQV5Z, about 1h10. From Chamonix: RN205 toward Le Fayet, exit XQV6Z, 15 min.
From XQV0Z: A41 exit 17 (Charvonnex / Pringy) then D14 to XQV1Z, 15 min. Free parking.
From XQV0Z: A41 exit XQV1Z, then D12 to XQV2Z, D4 via XQV3Z (50 km, 1h). From XQV4Z via XQV5Z: D909 then D4 (45 km, 1h). Free parking at summit.
From XQV0Z: A41 then A40 to Geneva, exit XQV1Z-Le Salève. Free parking at the foot of the cable car. Allow 40 min from XQV2Z.
From XQV0Z: A41 to XQV1Z, then D903 toward Boëge / XQV2Z, about 1h15. From Geneva: A40 exit XQV3Z-XQV4Z, then D903 / D12 to XQV5Z, about 50 min. Forest road to the plateau in summer.
From XQV0Z: A41/A410, exit XQV1Z Centre, about 40 min. From Geneva: A40 exit 19 XQV2Z, about 50 min. Town-centre parking.
From XQV0Z: D1508 along the west shore to XQV1Z, about 25 min. From Geneva: A41 exit XQV2Z Sud then D1508, about 1h.
From XQV0Z: D1508 along the west shore, 15 min to XQV1Z. From Geneva: A41 exit XQV2Z Sud, then D1508, about 50 min.
From XQV0Z: D1508 to XQV1Z (10 min), then route des Marais de Culas. Free parking on site.
From XQV0Z: D26 then D32 to XQV1Z (25 min). Park at the village car park, then 2 min walk to the church.
From XQV0Z: D26 then D32 to XQV1Z (25 min). The farm is on route de la Côte, signposted from the village centre.
From XQV0Z: D902 (Aulps valley), 35 km, 45 min. From Geneva via the Vallée Verte: about 1 h 15. Park at the Palais des Sports.
From XQV0Z: D902 to XQV1Z (about 45 min). Follow signs for Linga, free parking at the foot of the gondola. Then two lift sections (Linga, then Pierre Longue/Rochassons).
From XQV0Z: D902 via XQV1Z then XQV2Z (50 min). The base station is in the village centre on Chemin de Sous le Crêt. Free parking at the gondola base.
From XQV0Z: D909 / D909a via XQV1Z and XQV2Z, 32 km, about 45 min. Village car parks (Pré de Foire) nearby.
From XQV0Z: D909 to XQV1Z (30 min). In XQV2Z, follow direction Montremont for 2 km. Free parking outside the ecomuseum.
From XQV0Z: D909 towards XQV1Z – Les Aravis (20 km, approx. 25 min). XQV2Z bus station car park or near Hôtel Hermitage (av. du Vieux Pont).
From XQV0Z: N508 toward XQV1Z (10 min). At XQV2Z, take Route des Saintiers (signposted). Free on-site parking.
From XQV0Z: direction Bellegarde / Geneva via D1508 or D31, 15 min. Free parking.
From XQV0Z: drive the lake's eastern shore (Veyrier, Menthon, Talloires) then climb to the col on the D42 (8 km, 7% gradient). Allow 40 min from XQV1Z.
From XQV0Z: head to Epagny via D1508 (10 min). Take the Parc des Longeray exit, behind the Botanic store. Free parking on the business park.
From XQV0Z: via XQV1Z then XQV2Z, about 30 min. From Geneva: A40 exit 16, then XQV3Z, about 40 min.
From XQV0Z: ~10 min via the XQV1Z expressway exit. From Geneva (~40 km): A41 exit XQV2Z-Nord then towards XQV3Z, ~35-40 min. Free parking on site.
From XQV0Z: ~15-20 min via the D1508 along the west shore of the lake to XQV1Z. From Geneva (~55 km): A41 then D1508, ~50 min. Free on-site parking.
From age 10 depending on rooms, with an adult. Families, friends, stag/hen, business.
From age 10-12 approximately depending on height. Confirm with the centre for younger children.
From age 10-12 approximately depending on height. No driving prerequisite.
From age 10-12 depending on room. Minors accompanied.
From age 13-15 with parental authorisation. Weight between ~45 and 110 kg depending on operator.
From age 14 · friends, stag/hen, teen/adult birthdays, businesses.
From age 3 (Pitchoun); teens and adults for sporty trails.
From age 3 (toddlers' zone) to adults. Non-slip socks compulsory.
From age 3 in a dedicated toddler zone, accompanied. From age 6-7, access to the main zones after a briefing. No upper age limit.
From age 3-4 as accompanied passenger. Mushing initiation reserved for adults and older teens.
From age 3-4 as accompanied passenger; mushing initiation for adults/older teens
From age 3. Equipped with a map, search for clues to open the treasure chest. Fun forest activity adapted for the youngest.
From age 4 (dedicated kids' area with suitable blocks)
From age 4 in the dedicated kids' area with suitable blocks. Weekly classes start at age 4-5 and go up to adults.
From age 5 (pre-school slots at some times). Families, teens, adults, stag/hen, business.
From age 5 and 1.10 m, about 1 hour in the trees at low height. Ideal for first introduction to tree-climbing as a family.
From age 5-6 (adapted workshops), teens, beginner or experienced adults.
From age 6-8
From age 6-8 depending on areas (with adult), all levels including advanced climbers.
From age 6-8 depending on games. Families, nostalgics, students, stag/hen, birthdays.
From age 7 (kid karts), age 12 (leisure karts), all adult ages.
From age 7, supervisor required for under 15s. Search for 20 markers in the Rocailles forest with map and compass. €6.
From age 8 (discovery rafting), 12-14 (hydrospeed, sporty rafting). Families, sporty, groups.
From age 8-10
From age 8-10 depending on games. Families, friends, stag/hen, business.
From around 12 (depending on programme and height). Weight approximately 45-110 kg.
From around 14-16 (depending on age and height). No upper limit.
From around age 10 (subject to size), adult leisure karts, powerful karts for experienced drivers.
From around age 7-8 depending on the centre. Families, friends, stag/hen, business, schools.
From around age 8 (strength/height). Minors accompanied.
From around age 8, depending on strength and child height. For youngsters, adapted bows subject to availability. Minors accompanied.
From around age 8. Families, friends, stag/hen, business, French-Swiss audience.
From around age 8. Minors accompanied or with consent.
From around €30 for 15 minutes (to confirm on emotion-concept.fr). Long packages, multi-session passes, private hire and gift vouchers available.
From baby zone, laser from 4 yo, main park from 3-4 yo

From beginners curious about racing to keen amateurs wanting to perfect their racing line, the centre welcomes all profiles from age 14-16. 30 min discovery sessions, 1h or half-day clinics, group events (stag, birthdays, seminars). Coaching available with an experienced driver to progress faster.
From central Geneva ~15 km / 20 min via A40 exit XQV0Z then XQV1Z. From XQV2Z ~35 km / 35 min via A41 then A40. Free Vitam parking.
From central XQV0Z, climb via the D43 then the route des Plagnes toward Plaine-Joux (10-15 min). Snow equipment mandatory in winter. Parking at the station.
From central XQV0Z, direct access to the meeting point. From Geneva (~45 km, ~40 min), A41 exit XQV1Z-Nord then city centre.
From central XQV0Z, route du Semnoz (D241), ~10-15 min. Parking along the road. From Geneva (~45 km), A41 exit XQV1Z-Nord then Semnoz, ~50 min.

From central XQV0Z, take the D338 road towards Avoriaz / Manche valley heading south for 2 km. Free parking at Nyon cable car. Then, marked trail of 5 to 15 minutes on foot to XQV1Z. Alternatively, 30-minute walk from XQV2Z centre via Chemin du Renard.
From central XQV0Z, ~10 min via the ring road and the XQV1Z/Vovray exit. Free parking on site.
From central XQV0Z, ~5-10 min by car depending on exact address. From Geneva (~45 km), A41 exit XQV1Z, ~40 min. Street parking and public car parks nearby.
From central XQV0Z: A41 exit 16 XQV1Z Sud, Cran-Gevrier direction, Verts Prés business park. Free parking.
From central XQV0Z: Cran-Gevrier direction, La Turbine cultural hub (Place Chorus). On-site parking.
From central XQV0Z: take D1201 to XQV1Z (10 min). Exit XQV2Z centre, then avenue Jean Clerc. Free parking on site.
From central XQV0Z: ~10 min via the N201 towards XQV1Z/Vovray. From Geneva (~50 km): A41 exit XQV2Z-Sud, ~45 min. Free parking on site.
From easy (F) on the Vire de l'Ours to difficult (D) on the most exposed sections, allowing gradual discovery of the activity.
From mid-April to late October in fair weather. Spring is the best time to see young animals.
From nursery age. The farm suits children aged 3 to 12 best, but welcomes the whole family.
From the 1,535 m summit: 360° views over Mont Blanc, the Aravis, Lac XQV0Z and the Swiss Alps. Waymarked trails to Mont Caly and the Turche alpine meadows.

From the Ajon alpine pasture, the route is easy (400 m elevation gain, 1h30 ascent), suitable for families with children accustomed to walking. From XQV0Z, the complete loop is moderate (700 m elevation gain, 4-4h30).
From the Linga car park in XQV0Z: Linga gondola, then Pierre Longue and Rochassons chairlifts. Allow 20-30 min for the lifts.
From the Nyon car park, allow 2 more hours return to reach Lac des Mines d'Or, a high-altitude jewel.

From the Vallon Lake car parks in XQV0Z, allow approximately 30 minutes of walking. The trail starts from Saint-Bruno chapel and follows the Diomaz stream through the forest. Follow the "XQV1Z" and "Chartreux Cross" signage.
From the XQV0Z (733 m altitude, Boissy hillside), an open panorama over Lake Geneva, the Swiss shore and the Jura range on clear days.
From the XQV0Z Art Nature Trail, you can extend 15 minutes to the Croix de Vin, a heritage orientation point of the Pays de Genève.
From the XQV0Z, continue to nearby waterfalls located upstream in the Sales valley, approximately 30 minutes additional.
From the XQV0Z, walk down to the port of Rives via the funicular or the steps — the fishermen's district, port and CGN landing stage.
From the free Parc Biolley parking on Route des Soudans (D13) in Chedde, follow the maintained trail for 5 to 10 minutes. A platform allows you to admire the heart-shaped XQV0Z.
From the gondola arrival, the Morclan chairlift climbs to 1,970 m for exceptional views over the Franco-Swiss Alps.
From the grassy beach and granite sun deck, observe the Mont Blanc massif, Aravis and Fiz ranges. Peak-spotting game between swims.
From the park, the Lake XQV0Z cycle route starts just steps south (Albigny, XQV1Z-le-Vieux, Veyrier) and offers 42 km of traffic-free path.

From the parking in summer, allow 5 to 10 minutes of walking to reach the main viewpoint of the XQV0Z. From Salvagny in winter, allow 1 hour round-trip. For a combined hike with nearby waterfalls or Refuge des Fonts, plan 2 to 4 hours.

From the pass parking, roughly 45 minutes on foot to Grand Piton (1,379 m), the Salève's highest point. Marked GR Balcon du XQV0Z trail. Limestone pavement plateau and historic tower at the summit with orientation table.
From the summit, breathtaking view of the XQV0Z valley, XQV1Znette and the Aravis. Chamois are often seen on the wall.
From the summit, panorama over the Massif des Bauges, the Bornes massif, the town of Faverges and the surrounding basin. Ideal for landscape interpretation.
From the summit: 360° view over Geneva, Lake Geneva, the Jura and the Mont Blanc range on clear days.
From the viewpoint at 717 m, admire one of the finest panoramas of Chablais: dominant view over XQV0Z, Lake XQV1Z, the Swiss and French Jura, and the Dent d'Oche (2,222 m).
From the village, several marked trails lead to the famous glacial cirque and the Sixt-XQV0Z National Nature Reserve.

From ~8-10 depending on cave. Families, teens, adults, groups, stag/hen, seminars.
From ~8-10 depending on the canyon. Good physical condition required (approach walk, optional jumps).
From Évian ~5 km / 10 min along Lake Geneva shore. From Thonon ~10 km / 15 min. From Geneva ~50 km / 1h via A40 then south shore. Free parking.
From Évian ~5 km / 10 min. From Thonon ~10 km / 15 min. From Geneva ~50 km / 1h via A40 then south shore. Free parking.
Frénalay Loop
Frénalay loop accessible for mobility-impaired visitors and strollers. Trails to Prazon: 4x4 track, minimal elevation gain.
Frêtes du Vargnoz via ferrata, Roc Lancrenaz road, 74230 XQV0Z

High season (mid-July to end August) offers daily access without booking from 10 AM to 5 PM, ideal for spontaneous family visits. The rest of the year, the park operates by booking only, ensuring a quieter and more personalized experience. Courses may close at any time for weather reasons (wind, storms), so mornings with sunny conditions are recommended in summer. The altitude of 1,450 m provides pleasant coolness in the middle of summer.

High season extends from late June to late August, with daily opening 10am to 7pm and peak crowds in July-August: prefer mornings or book online to avoid queues. The park also opens weekends in June (11am–6pm) and Wednesdays, Saturdays and Sundays afternoons in spring and autumn, quieter periods ideal for enjoying the site peacefully. The park is closed in April and May 2026 (maintenance). School holiday weeks (All Saints, February) see afternoon opening daily. Summer weather allows combining treetop courses and lake swimming; bring comfortable clothes (trousers, trainers) and sun protection.
High season: adult €7.50, child 5-14 years €6. Low season: adult €6.50, child €5.50. Free under 5 (ticket required). 12-entry cards and season passes available.

In

In 2024, Compagnie du XQV0Z launched a major reconstruction: a new 3S cable car designed by architect Renzo Piano, linking Argentière to Lognan and the 3,275 m summit, with commissioning planned for December 2026. As a result, the Plan Joran gondola is closed from 4 May to 30 November 2026 for construction. The site is therefore inaccessible in summer 2026. In winter (December–early May) the area operates for skiers via Plan Joran.

In 2026, the Chablais Museum at the XQV0Z presents the temporary exhibition "Portrait. From painting to the selfie, an art of (re)presenting oneself" — the evolution of the self-portrait through the centuries.
In 2026, the XQV0Z Au Sommet event at Col des Pitons takes place on Tuesday, July 7 from 9am to 12pm. The D41A is closed to cars and reserved for cyclists. Free, no registration, open to all levels.

In July and August, the 2-hectare, 6-metre-deep alpine lake of Super-XQV0Z is accessible from the gondola arrival — also reachable on foot in 50 minutes from the village. A mountain restaurant, the Morclan chairlift (access to 1,970 m) and MTB trails complete the summer offering. The gondola runs from 26 June to 31 August 2026 in summer; in winter, it is the main village entry point to the XQV1Z ski domain.
In July–August, you can come without booking between 10 AM and 5 PM. The rest of the year, booking is required. For groups of 9 people or more, booking is always required regardless of the period.

In June: Saturday-Sunday and public holidays 10am–6pm. In July-August: daily 10am–6pm. Off-season: afternoon opening from 1:30pm for unsupervised swimming and adventure course. Hours verified on the official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
In October, the beech forest surrounding the waterfall displays flamboyant colours, offering a different and equally magical setting.
In XQV0Z at XQV1Z Locaskis (by the station) or in Chamonix at Snell Sports, Zero G, Sanglard Sports and guide offices. Count €15-25 per day for the full kit (harness, lanyard, helmet).
In XQV0Z, the Maison du Lieutenant and its interpretation centre extend the visit with the ethnographic aspects of XQV1Z.

In an old Savoyard farmhouse from 1783 at XQV0Z, the Old Ski Museum brings back the history of skiing in the Portes du Soleil from 1900 onwards: wooden skis, sealskins, sleds and snowshoes — plus the reconstructed interior of an alpine farm.
In autumn (September–October)

In case of rain or fog, it is strongly discouraged to take the Sentier des Crêtes. Rocky passages become very slippery and zero visibility makes progression dangerous. Choose another hike or postpone your outing.
In case of storms, floods or unfavourable conditions, the outing can be postponed or cancelled for safety. A reschedule or refund is then offered according to conditions.
In case of storms, floods or unfavourable conditions, the outing may be postponed or cancelled for safety. XQV0Z then offers a reschedule or refund according to terms.
In case of strong wind, rain or storm, the activity is postponed for safety reasons. The operator offers a free reschedule to another slot.
In central XQV0Z, rue Carnot, 10 min walk from the station. Public car parks nearby.
In high season the stop also welcomes some of the CGN's five historic

In just a few years, XQV0Z has become one of the main cultural centers in the XQV1Z basin, with a programming of internationally renowned temporary exhibitions: Marc Chagall, Raoul Dufy, Rodin, Charlie Chaplin, Félix Ziem. In 2026, the building received the S3A label guaranteeing welcome and accessibility for persons with intellectual disabilities.
In spring (April–May)
In strong wind, heavy rain or storms, the jump is postponed or cancelled for safety. The operator then offers a reschedule or refund according to terms.
In summer (1 July–31 August), free bus line 20 from XQV0Z to XQV1Z. Get off at Petit Port / Bois Jettaz stop. Rest of the year, line 20 is paid (Sibra network).

In summer (July – August), the gondola opens access to the alpine lake and altitude trails. The lake is only accessible by gondola in July and August. In winter (December – April), the gondola serves the ski domain. Outside these seasons, the installation is closed.

In summer (July – August), the gondola serves XQV0Z's renowned bikepark, a summer sledge run and marked hiking trails. On Tuesday and Thursday evenings from 8 July to 28 August, it runs until 7:00 pm for sunset hikes and dinners in altitude. In winter, it is the main access from the village to the XQV1Z – XQV2Z ski domain.
In summer (June–August)
In summer the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm offers events around the bread oven: a demonstration of how it works and, on some days, a tasting of freshly baked bread.
In summer the outdoor area transforms the place:
In summer the plateau is explored on foot via many

In summer the terminus is the Nid d'Aigle at 2,412 m, at the foot of the Bionnassay glacier. The renovated platform offers secure access to the séracs and altitude trails. In winter the terminus is Bellevue (1,800 m).

In summer, Giffre shuttles (Rando'Bus) line 6 from XQV0Z towards Le Lignon, XQV1Z stop. Operates daily during summer season (July–August). Check schedules on samoens.com or haut-giffre.fr. Outside summer season, no regular public transport.
In summer, Lac de Vonnes offers supervised swimming, pedalos and catering - ideal to round off the family outing.

In summer, Mont-Chéry suits hikers seeking peace and panoramas — quieter than the Chavannes sector. The UCI MTB World Cup in late August brings crowds and suspends MTB access on this flank from 24 August. In winter, the north-facing aspect holds snow longer and offers a calmer alternative to the busier main sectors of XQV0Z-XQV1Z.
In summer, free meg-bus shuttle from XQV0Z bus station, Mont d'Arbois/Cote 2000 line (departures from 8:20 AM, every 20 minutes).
In summer, glass windows open onto 1,000 m² of mineral decking and over 6,000 m² of green lawns with a mini water park. Solarium and green spaces to relax in the sun.

In summer, swimming is the main draw: cool water, safe bottom, dedicated children's area. Pedalo and light-boat hire is available depending on the season, as is fishing (federal permit required). Large picnic areas with tables and benches invite you to stay all day; a snack bar usually opens in high season. On land you'll find a children's playground, walking paths around the lake and plenty of grass for outdoor games. The setting, open to the peaks of Le Môle and Le Bargy, is also great for photography and birdwatching.
In summer, the
In summer, the XQV0Z is visible from the parking (accessible for mobility-impaired). The approach trail to the base of the waterfall is rocky and steep, not suitable for strollers and wheelchairs.

In summer, the best window is July–mid-September: the cable car is fully open, trails are clear, and the Mont Blanc panorama is at its best in clear morning conditions. In winter (December–mid-April), Brévent is a respected ski area known for south-facing slopes. Avoid the intersaison closure (20 April–12 June 2026). Mornings are best for panoramic clarity before afternoon clouds build over the massif.

In summer, the gondola gives access to a network of downhill and enduro MTB trails as well as waymarked hiking paths across the alpine meadows. In winter it integrates into the XQV0Z – XQV1Z ski area within the Portes du Soleil. From the plateau, the Chavannes Express chairlift extends access towards higher terrain and cross-resort links.
In summer, the top of the lift opens onto a
In summer, the waterfall is descended by guided groups via rappel. Impressive spectacle from the base of the cascade. Eight waterfalls total in the canyon, with the 60 m main cascade as the finale.
In summer, waterfall visible from the road (accessible for mobility-impaired from parking). Rocky and steep approach trail, not suitable for wheelchairs/strollers.
In the 19th c. XQV0Z was a major leather centre in Savoy: about
In the early morning, low sunlight crosses the spray and creates a rainbow at the base of the fall. Best in spring, during high flow.

In the former 18th-century presbytery of Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce, a historic hamlet of XQV0Z, the Sacred Art Museum preserves polychrome sculptures, gold and silverware, paintings and liturgical vestments of a Savoyard Baroque treasure.

In the hamlet of Collongette at XQV0Z, the Granges de Servette Museum blends contemporary art (the Lyon school since 1958) with more than 1,000 antique tools of traditional crafts. Free entry all summer long.

In the heart of XQV0Z village, the Maison du Fromage takes you from the alpine pastures to the ripening cellars of the AOP XQV1Z cheese. A sensory scenographic route, with a tasting served alongside a glass of Savoie wine or apple juice at the end of the visit.
In the heart of XQV0Z's medieval village, the Shoemaking Museum traces 300 years of cobblers' tradition. Free entry, open June-August.

In the heart of XQV0Z, between the Vassé canal and the north shore of the lake, the Jardins de l'Europe form an English-style arboretum park laid out in 1863. Ginkgo biloba, Virginia tulip tree and giant sequoias make up the green setting of the locals' favourite stroll.
In the heart of XQV0Z, seven themed escape-game rooms (history, fantasy, culinary worlds) for 2 to 6 players.
In the heart of XQV0Z, walking distance from the train station and town centre. Public car parks nearby. Combines with a walk around the lake, Old Town, cinemas.
In the heart of the Aravis, view over the Belledonne range and Tournette in clear weather. Ideal to combine thrills and discovery of the resort in summer.
In the late 18th century the wealthy English writer
In the summer season, snack bar and large lawn for picnics or relaxing between swims.
In toponymy, Miribel means "beautiful view", a name perfectly justified by the 360° panorama the summit offers of Lake Geneva, Mont Blanc and the Alps.
In winter
In winter (November to March)
In winter Loëx becomes a popular
In winter the TMB serves the Bellevue Plateau for snowshoeing, cross-country skiing and access to the XQV0Z–Saint-Gervais ski area (ski pass not included).
In winter the plateau hosts the Moises Nordic area (cross-country skiing, snowshoeing), and in summer longer family walks.

In winter, the TMB serves the Bellevue Plateau (1,800 m) and the XQV0Z–Saint-Gervais ski area. In summer it delivers hikers and mountaineers to the foot of the Bionnassay glacier and the Nid d'Aigle refuge — the classic starting point for the ascent of Mont Blanc via the Voie Royale. Around 75% of Mont Blanc climbers use the TMB.
In winter, the beach becomes a refuge for many birds: ducks, swans, coots, pochards, grebes, sandpipers, curlews, herons and egrets.

In winter, the cascade may freeze partially or completely, offering a magical scenery appreciated by snowshoe hikers and ice climbing enthusiasts. The water, sourced from snowmelt, remains very cold even in summer (a feature noted in canyoning guides). The natural setting of the gorge and the power of the fall after heavy rain make this site particularly impressive.
In winter, the road is closed after Salvagny hamlet. Access to the XQV0Z is on foot or by snowshoe from Salvagny (Nant Sec parking), approximately 1 hour round-trip, depending on snow conditions.
In winter, the road is closed after Salvagny. Access on foot or by snowshoe from the hamlet, approximately 1h round-trip in spectacular winter scenery.

In winter, the waterfall freezes partially or completely, offering a different spectacle prized by photographers. In spring, flow is maximal thanks to snowmelt. In summer, hiking is particularly enjoyable for the cool forest cover and refreshing mist generated by the waterfall's spray. The site also offers occasional views of Lake XQV0Z, notably XQV1Z Castle and the peninsula from trail clearings.

In winter, the waterfall may freeze entirely, forming spectacular ice columns. Site appreciated by ice climbing enthusiasts and snowshoe hikers. Winter access practicable on Wednesdays, Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays depending on cable car opening.

In-house tournaments and regional inter-club matches during the sports season (September-June). Calendar on the club's site.
Inclined trampoline tracks for acrobatics.

Included in the path: Italian aperitivo in late afternoon with selection of products, enjoyed in bathrobe.

Inclusive beach with Hippocampe and Tiralo floating wheelchairs, wheelchair lift, secure pathways, audio beacons, accessible changing facilities. Inclusion ambassadors present Mon–Tue 1–3:30 p.m., Wed–Sun 1–7 p.m. (July 5–August 31)
Independent
Independent course through curves, dead ends and nets, accessible to children and teenagers. New activity and integrated treasure hunt.

Independent neighbourhood cinema in XQV0Z, where the French, Swiss and Savoyard borders meet. National releases, arthouse films in subtitled OV, family screenings and event operations punctuate a weekly programme refreshed every Wednesday.

Indoor and outdoor pools at 34°C, saunas at different temperatures, hammams, herbal tea room. Alternate between floating, observation and stimulation in thousand-year-old water with therapeutic properties.

Indoor billiards — ideal in rainy weather or in the evening, a perfect complement to a day on Lake Geneva.

Indoor play park at XQV0Z (north XQV1Z): inflatables, slides, trampolines and baby area — ideal year-round family outing.

It is the youngest lake in the Chablais. It is recognised as a key geosite of the UNESCO Chablais Global Geopark and has been classified as a Natural Zone of Ecological, Faunistic and Floristic Interest (ZNIEFF) since 1991. Maximum depth: 16 m. Free, open access year-round. Camping, bivouac and fires are prohibited on site. Access from XQV0Z is by car to the lakeside car park, then a few minutes' walk on a flat path. The site is open April to November depending on snow cover, ideal for a half-day visit or lakeside picnic.

Its name comes from the horseshoe shape of its cliffs, which close off the eastern part of the Giffre valley. In spring, over thirty waterfalls fed by snowmelt cascade down its walls to form short streams, tributaries of the Giffre. The most famous are the Pleureuse, Sauffaz, Méridienne, Fontaine de l'Or (Pierrette), and the Lyre. Classified as a Grand Site of France, the cirque welcomes approximately 500,000 visitors annually and forms part of XQV0Z's largest nature reserve (9,200 hectares of 11,200).

Next to it stands the Pont Caquot, or Pont Neuf, built between 1924 and 1928 under the direction of engineer Albert Caquot. Its unreinforced concrete arch was one of the largest vaults in the world at the time. It is this bridge that carries today's traffic on the RD1201. The Pont Charles-Albert, relieved of vehicle traffic since 1929, is reserved for pedestrians and cyclists. One site, two bridges, one history.
