No regular service to the plateau; buses run to XQV0Z from XQV1Z, then taxi/carpool.
No regular tourist visit. External view from the road free. Verified on 10 June 2026.

No reservation is necessary to access XQV0Z. Simply arrive at the reception during opening hours (1:30 pm–7:30 pm, last entry 6:45 pm). Groups over 20 people must complete the online form on avoriaz.com.
No reservation necessary (except groups over 20 people, mandatory form on avoriaz.com). Check real-time crowd levels via Affluences app.
No specific events — free visit. Possible occasional activities organised by ONF or the city. See annecy.fr.
No strict age limit, but the weight floor (≥ 35 kg) effectively means roughly 10-11 for a standard child. Confirm at the ticket office.
No ticketing: no interior visit. External view from the road is free. Verified on 10 June 2026.

No, Nordic dogs (huskies, Alaskans) are genetically adapted to pulling and love running. Mushers look after their health, training and recovery. They are fed, cared for and pampered all year by their musher.
No, access is entirely free, year-round, with no ticket or booking required.
No, access is free year-round.

No, access to XQV0Z is free. However, the trail starts from the Planpraz cable car arrival point (2,000 m), which is paid. It is possible to walk up from Chamonix, but this adds approximately 1,000 m of elevation gain.
No, access to the Plaine-Joux paragliding takeoff area is free and open year-round. The station car park is also free.
No, access to the massif's trails is free and open year-round. Only the cable car and the summit restaurant are paid.

No, all safety equipment is provided by XQV0Z: harnesses, helmets, continuous safety line for treetop courses. For canyoning, wetsuit, helmet and technical equipment are also provided. Simply bring closed-toe shoes.
No, as it is private; the estate has its own rules. Sign in at reception for the day card.
No, as long as the coach's instructions are followed. The centre is designed with netting, regulation targets and systematic briefings. The coach is present throughout.

No, canyoning at XQV0Z is a regulated activity requiring a certified guide. The Nyon canyon is a beginner canyon (400 m, AD difficulty, engagement level 2) with rope rappels requiring professional supervision. Several providers offer guided outings from June to September.
No, discovery trips require no prior experience. The instructor explains everything, supplies the kit and adapts the pace to the group. For technical trips, a level may be needed.
No, dogs and other pets are not permitted on the XQV0Z estate, including in the gardens and ruins.
No, dogs are forbidden in the reserve even on a leash, to protect wildlife.
No, dogs are not allowed on site to protect the farm animals.
No, dogs are not permitted at XQV0Z because the lake is within the Aiguilles Rouges National Nature Reserve, where domestic animals are prohibited to protect wildlife.
No, dogs are not permitted on XQV0Z. They are allowed on leash in the adjacent Châtaigneraie park.
No, dogs are not permitted on the beach or around Lac Bleu itself. They are allowed on-leash on trails along the Giffre between XQV0Z and XQV1Z.
No, dogs are not permitted on tree-top courses at XQV0Z for safety reasons (risk of distraction, incompatibility with continuous safety lines and safety procedures).
No, dogs are strictly forbidden, even on a leash, to protect wildlife.
No, feeding the animals is strictly forbidden. Their feeding is controlled by ONF to guarantee their good health and preserve their natural behaviour. Respect the enclosures.
No, for hygiene reasons, only close-fitting swimwear (briefs, swim trunks) is allowed. Beach shorts, bermudas and boxers are not permitted. Swim cap is not required.
No, free access in summer. In winter a nordic pass is required to use groomed tracks.
No, free access year-round.
No, free along the whole route.
No, generally no booking required: tickets on site. In high season (summer holidays, fine weekends), prefer morning or late afternoon to avoid queues.
No, hourly rental is open to everyone, members or not. Members usually get preferential rates and priority access. FFT/FFP licence is required for official competition.
No, just purchase a pass on-site or online. The resort is family-friendly and less crowded than XQV0Z. Adult day pass approximately €28. Night skiing included with XQV1Z multi-day passes.
No, no experience required. A full 15-20 min briefing and familiarisation are provided before the tour.
No, no licence needed. It's a leisure activity, open even to teens (no physical risk).
No, no previous experience needed. The state-qualified instructor introduces the gear, demonstrates safety and guides the first shots.
No, no prior experience required. Workshops are open to beginners and experienced alike. The host adapts the programme to your level and wishes.

No, no reservation needed for the Alpine Coaster or the park. Tickets on site (counter or machine). Online purchase available for the toboggan (collect on site, valid 1 year). Private hire possible outside opening dates: contact the site directly.
No, no tokens or coins. Entry is paid by duration (1h, 2h, 3h or day pass) and gives unlimited access to all machines for that time. Once inside, you play freely.
No, not for individual self-guided visits. Booking is required for group guided tours, by request to the XQV0Z Town Hall on +33 (0)4 50 68 11 09.
No, occasional visitors can take part in intro sessions without a licence. For regular classes and competitions, the annual FFTA licence is required.
No, open access during opening hours. Booking recommended only for lessons (swimming, aquagym, aquabike) and lane hire for groups.
No, padel is very beginner-friendly. The basics come in one or two sessions, and the smaller court and solid (stringless) racquet make it quick to pick up.
No, pets are not allowed at Château Bleu Aquatic Centre (indoor). Aquatic centre regulations.
No, public sessions are walk-in. Eisstock Monday tournaments are by booking (recommended).
No, public sessions are walk-in. Groups must book on 04 50 79 08 43.
No, road access to the col is entirely free. Free parking. Paragliding flights, the restaurant and some shuttles are paid.
No, summer only: daily 27 June to 31 August 2026, plus a few September weekends (5-7 and 13-14). Closed in winter.
No, swimming is forbidden at the Lac des Plagnes: it is a protected natural area within the Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark. You can however picnic on its shores.
No, swimming is forbidden in XQV0Z. The lake is a protected natural alpine site (1,358 m), and the water is cold even in summer.

No, swimming is forbidden in the Dronières lake. The lake is reserved for fishing (permit required). To swim, the water sports centre at Espace Bernard Pellarin (heated pool) is open from 18 May to 30 August.
No, swimming is forbidden in the reserve. The neighbouring Amphion municipal beach is open for swimming in season.

No, swimming is prohibited at XQV0Z itself. Swimming is possible in the Nant d'Oy at Fairy Bridge level (10–15 min upstream from the waterfall) if flow is not too strong. Swimming recommended at Angon beach on the lake after the hike.
No, the Château de Fonbonne building at XQV0Z is not open for interior visits. Only its Herbularius garden is accessible to the public.
No, the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm is a 19th-century farm with uneven floors and an upper floor reached by stairs. It is not wheelchair accessible. Sturdy shoes recommended.

No, the Lake XQV0Z Ecomuseum runs mainly from April to September following a detailed seasonal calendar. Off-season, it opens by reservation for groups. Verified on ecomusee-lacannecy.com on 10 June 2026.
No, the XQV0Z at XQV1Z is private property and closed to the public. It can only be seen from the road. No interior visits are organised.
No, the XQV0Z at XQV1Z today houses the Bas-Chablais Community of Communes (CCBC) and is not open for regular tourist visits. Free external view from the road.
No, the XQV0Z is a private property closed to interior visits. The silhouette of the fortified manor and its four pepper-pot towers are clearly visible from the road and the local paths of XQV1Z.

No, the XQV0Z water sports base operates mainly in the summer season, peaking from mid-June to the end of August. The adjacent beach is lifeguarded from June to September. Check the calendar on ville-evian.fr.
No, the high-mountain road is usually open May to November depending on snow.
No, the offer covers all levels: family hiking up to technical routes. The guide adapts the outing to your level. State your experience at booking.
No, the outdoor area (leisure pool, slide, solarium) is open only in summer, usually from June to late August depending on weather. The three indoor pools are open the rest of the year.
No, the park is accessible to beginner families after a safety briefing. Outdoor routes are equipped with top-rope.
No, the park is free. Only some activities (mini-golf, water rentals) are paid.
No, the practice is open to all without a membership. Buckets are €3 each, paid at reception.
No, the season runs from 1 April to 1 November. The farm is closed to visitors outside that period.
No, the self-guided visit is walk-in. But workshops, walks, escape game and stays must be booked in advance via the site or phone.
No, the site is seasonal: usually open May to mid-October. Closed in winter for safety (freezing, footbridge maintenance).
No, the site of the ruins of the Château de XQV0Z is not wheelchair-accessible: dirt paths, steps, uneven ground on a rocky spur. Walking shoes recommended.
No, the trail has many stairs and footbridges. Use a baby carrier for toddlers.
No, the trails are free year-round.

No, the workshop primarily welcomes beginners. The potter guides you step by step through your first movements on the wheel or in hand-building. The termly cycles allow the most motivated participants to progress week by week.
No, there are no toilets at the site. Plan a restroom stop before arriving.
No, there is no maximum age. Courses are adapted according to height and physical condition. The Grand Course requires good fitness, while the Acro'Net suits all ages without harness.

No, there is no refuge at the XQV0Z summit. The XQV1Z Chalet-Refuge is located at 2,002 m elevation, before the final ascent. It offers meals and lodging by reservation. From the refuge, allow another 1.5 to 2 hours to reach the summit.
No, there is no rental at the start point. Specialist shops in Saint-Gervais offer rental of harnesses, lanyards and helmets. Arrange equipment in advance.
No, walk-in for public sessions.
No, walk-in for public sessions. Pay at reception.
No-kill
No. Access to Albigny Beach is free year-round. It is one of two free public beaches in XQV0Z, along with Marquisats Beach.
No. Access to Col des Pitons is free and complimentary year-round. Pass parking is also free (spaces limited in peak season; arrive early on weekends).
No. Access to XQV0Z and the plateau is free and costs nothing year-round. Parking on the plateau car parks is also free.
No. Access to XQV0Z is closed from November to March. The trail is frozen, rocks are slippery and conditions are dangerous. Open mid-June to early November only.
No. Access to XQV0Z is free and open year-round. Only parking is charged during summer season (30 June to 31 August): €3 per day for a car.
No. Access to XQV0Z is free and open year-round. Only parking is paid May to September (€2/hr, €18/day in 2023 at Vérel and Angon), free off-season.
No. Access to XQV0Z is free and uncharted year-round, except during snowy periods.
No. Access to XQV0Z is free and unrestricted from mid-June to early November.
No. Access to XQV0Z is free and unrestricted year-round for pedestrians. Only parking is paid during peak season: €6 per day (free from 5 PM), from 1 June to 31 August and May weekends.
No. Access to XQV0Z is free and unrestricted year-round. The parking at the road's end is paid (approximately €7 per vehicle), a contribution towards site maintenance.
No. Access to XQV0Z is free year-round. Only meals or overnight stay at Camille Dunant refuge are charged.

No. Access to XQV0Z, the National Resistance Monument and the historical trail is free year-round. Only guided tours offered by the Maquis Memory building are charged (adult €3, reduced €2, free under 8 and first Sunday of the month).

No. Access to the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges is free, with the visit organised by the Association of Friends of Viuz-Faverges. The castle itself is a private events venue, but the keep and inner courtyard, listed as Historic Monuments, are open to the public.
No. Access to the Lappiaz mine trail is free and open. Only parking at Avoriaz is paid in season.
No. Access to the Parc de l'Imperial (officially Parc Charles Bosson) is free year-round. Only the adjoining Impérial beach (in season) and some neighbouring leisure facilities are paid.
No. Access to the Ruins of the St Michel castle and the trail leading to them is free and unrestricted year-round.
No. Access to the XQV0Z is free and open year-round, every day. Only fishing requires a permit, issued by APPMA and sold at the OT des Alpes du XQV1Z.
No. Access to the XQV0Z is free and open year-round, subject to snow and weather conditions (normally open April 15 to November 15).
No. Access to the XQV0Z is free and open year-round. Parking at Vallon Lake is also free.
No. Access to the XQV0Z is free year-round, every day. Free parking on both sides.
No. Access to the XQV0Z is free, as the pass is crossed by a public departmental road. Free parking on site.
No. Access to the gaming room at XQV0Z XQV1Z is free. Valid ID is required at the entrance and appropriate dress is expected. Only the wagers are paid.
No. Access to the trails, fitness course, skate park and play areas at XQV0Z is free and open year-round. Some adjacent facilities (swimming pool, ice rink, tennis) are paid.
No. Admission to XQV0Z (trails, lake, animal park, play areas, fitness trail, barbecue, parking) is free and open year-round. Only the water sports centre (pool) and tree-climbing are paid.
No. Admission to XQV0Z is free and open access all season. Only activities offered by providers (boats, adventure course, equestrian centre, etc.) are charged individually.
No. Admission to XQV0Z is free and open year-round. It is a protected natural site owned by the Conservatoire du Littoral, open to the public at no cost.
No. Admission to XQV0Z is free year-round. The Plaine-Joux car park is also free. In summer, free shuttles operate between the car park and XQV1Z.
No. Animals are not permitted at XQV0Z (internal regulations in effect).
No. Camping, bivouac and fires are strictly prohibited on the site of the XQV0Z. Accommodation is available in XQV1Z and La Chèvrerie.
No. Climbing shoes can be rented at the entrance (~€3-5) and chalk is sold on site. Comfortable flexible clothing recommended.
No. Dogs (even on leash) and horses are prohibited on the entire municipal beach at XQV0Z from 1 March to 30 November.
No. Dogs and other pets are not allowed at the XQV0Z, including those on leash, in a bag, or in your arms. Only guide dogs are authorized to accompany their owner.
No. Dogs are not allowed at Albigny Beach.
No. Dogs are not allowed at XQV0Z Beach.

No. Dogs are not allowed in XQV0Z to preserve the tranquility of freely roaming animals (llamas in particular respond aggressively to dogs). A secure kennel with water is available at the entrance to house your dog during your visit.
No. Dogs are not allowed on the Ferme de Chosal Trail, due to the presence of farm animals in the pens.
No. Dogs are not permitted on XQV0Z. Off-season, you may walk your dog in the tree-lined area on the south side (toward XQV1Z), but not on the beach lawn itself.
No. Dogs are not permitted within XQV0Z for safety reasons: protection of visitors, park birds, and the dogs themselves. This rule applies to all pets.
No. Dogs are prohibited at XQV0Z as it sits within the Aiguilles Rouges national nature reserve. This restriction protects wild fauna (ibex, marmots, chamois) and fragile ecosystems.
No. Dogs are prohibited at XQV0Z year-round, even on leash, in accordance with Blue Flag certification.
No. Drone overflight of the XQV0Z site is prohibited by municipal order.
No. Entry to XQV0Z is free year-round. Only parking is paid in July–August (€3 per day). Activities offered by providers (go-kart, rope course, pedal boats, mini-golf) are paid.
No. Entry to the XQV0Z is free and accessible year-round. The developed parking nearby is also free.

No. Parking Albigny and Parking Petit Port near Albigny Beach are paid. They are very full in summer — it is recommended to arrive by bike (cycle path) or bus (Sibra lines 2 and 5, free in July–August).
No. Pets are not accepted at XQV0Z, in accordance with the castle hotel's policy.
No. Settings adapt to beginners (assists on, ABS, traction control) and experienced drivers (pro mode, telemetry). A team briefing is enough to get started.
No. Since the order of 26 July 2024, vehicle access to Col du Pré Vernet and Col des Contrebandiers is prohibited to protect the overcrowded site. You must park at Petit Port and walk up.

No. Strollers are not allowed on the footbridges of the XQV0Z (insufficient width). A baby carrier is mandatory for children who cannot walk independently; a loan is available on site against ID, subject to stock.
No. Swimming and water activities are prohibited at XQV0Z to preserve the lake's fragile ecosystem and aquatic vegetation.
No. Swimming at XQV0Z is free and unsupervised. Watch children and inexperienced swimmers carefully. The water is cool even in summer.
No. Swimming is prohibited in XQV0Z and Lacs des Chéserys under a prefectural decree of 22 May 2025. This measure protects the fragile alpine lake ecosystem and limits visitor impact.

No. Swimming is prohibited in the XQV0Z by a permanent municipal order from the communes of XQV1Z and XQV2Z, along with all water activities (boating, diving, ice skating). The site is listed under Natura 2000.

No. The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges is not accessible to people with reduced mobility due to the 145 steps without a lift. Heritage Guides remain available at the bottom to answer questions from visitors unable to climb.

No. The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges is open from mid-June to end of August (Monday to Friday 2:30 PM–6:30 PM, Saturday 10 AM–12 PM, closed Sundays). Outside season, visits are available during Heritage Days or by group request on 04 50 32 45 99.
No. The Fox Trail has free access year-round.

No. The Geological Trail stops at the Pointe Percée refuge. The ascent of the 2752 m summit is a mountaineering route and is normally done with a high-mountain guide, via the routes from Les Troncs or the Col des Verts.
No. The Jardins de l'Europe are a public park with free, open access, available 24/7 year-round. No ticket or booking is required to visit this lakeside arboretum in XQV0Z.
No. The Pont Charles-Albert (called XQV0Z) has been pedestrian-only since 1929. Cars use the neighbouring Pont Caquot, which carries the RD1201.
No. The TMB ticket covers the train only. A separate ski pass for the XQV0Z–Saint-Gervais area is available at the Le Fayet and Saint-Gervais stations.
No. The Uffin Walking Loop is freely accessible year-round, like all the rural trails of the commune of XQV0Z.

No. The XQV0Z are not accessible by stroller or wheelchair. The access trail from the parking lot has a fairly steep climb over 20 minutes, and the site itself features uneven terrain with ruins and stairs.
No. The XQV0Z in XQV1Z is a freely accessible public space, open year-round, 24/7. No ticket or booking required. Verified on 10 June 2026.

No. The access trail to XQV0Z from Plaine-Joux crosses the forest with roots and steep terrain, unsuitable for pushchairs and wheelchairs. People with reduced mobility can obtain a barrier code for direct access to the XQV1Z car park (with proof at the tourist office).
No. The accompanying adult (who supervises from the ground without climbing) enters XQV0Z free of charge. The participating adult (who climbs with children) pays €8 (sliding scale from 2 adults).
No. The footbridges of the XQV0Z are not wheelchair accessible due to the insufficient width of the passage. The Chemin des Châteaux around also has uneven terrain.
No. The interior of the XQV0Z is not wheelchair accessible due to numerous stairs and steps. The gardens and terraces are partially accessible. The site has no adapted PMR toilets.

No. The road to Col des Pitons closes in winter (November–March approximately) from Grotte du Diable, 1.5 km before the pass. The final section is not snow-cleared. Full access reopens May through October depending on weather.
No. The route along the Dranse at XQV0Z has free, open access. No booking or ticket is needed.
No. The trail around XQV0Z is not accessible by stroller. The terrain is uneven with roots and stones, but it is suitable for children aged 3-4 and older who can walk.
No. The trail from the parking lot to the XQV0Z passes through a forest area not suitable for strollers and wheelchairs. The waterfall remains visible from the road and parking lot.
No. The trail leading to XQV0Z is not suitable for pushchairs or wheelchairs. Muddy terrain, steep descent towards the torrent. Good boots essential.
No. The trail to XQV0Z has elevation change and uneven surface that does not permit stroller or wheelchair access.
No. The trail to the XQV0Z is a mountain trail with elevation gain and rocky sections, not suitable for strollers or wheelchairs. The waterfall remains visible from the valley.

No. The trail to the XQV0Z is not suitable for strollers or wheelchairs. It is a forest trail with several stream crossings, sometimes slippery terrain, and some technical sections near the waterfall.
No. The trail to the XQV0Z passes through forest with moderate elevation gain (around 200 m from Grépon car park) and is not suitable for pushchairs or wheelchairs.
No. The walk has a 110 m elevation gain and many stairs. A baby carrier is required for children who cannot walk on their own. Dogs are not allowed.
No. The walking route along the banks of the Arve from XQV0Z to Arthaz is free with open access, year-round, with no booking needed.
No. The water is biologically filtered by more than 10,000 aquatic plants, without any chemical product. A regeneration-pool system ensures water quality at all times.
No. This route is very demanding physically and technically. For a first experience, choose introductory routes (F or PD) in the region.
No. Visitors' dogs are not allowed near the team, for safety and to keep the working dogs calm.
No. Visitors' dogs are not allowed near the team, for safety reasons and to keep the working dogs calm.
No. XQV0Z is a summer leisure base open from 6 April to 31 October. In winter, the site closes and XQV1Z focuses on alpine skiing.
No. XQV0Z is a tree playground without harnesses: all workshops are less than one metre from the ground or surrounded by safety nets, accessible independently from age 3.
No. XQV0Z is completely free year-round. It is one of only two free beaches in XQV1Z, along with Albigny Beach.
No. XQV0Z is free and open year-round. Parking at Les Lindarets is also free.
No. XQV0Z is free to access year-round. No entry fee is charged on the trails leading to the summit.
No. XQV0Z no longer offers public guided tours since 2020. Access to the castle is reserved for hotel guests, participants in private events, and restaurant diners by reservation.
Non adapte aux poussettes; terrain rochers et terre (fiche officielle Chamonix).
Non admis dans le XQV0Z (musée). Exception possible pour chiens d'assistance.
Non admis dans le centre, à l'exception des chiens d'assistance.
Non admis dans le parc.
Non admis en cabine (altitude et conditions de pression).
Non admis en cabine, sauf chiens d'assistance pour personnes handicapées.
Non-profit karting and motocross club XQV0Z in XQV1Z, XQV2Z
Non-slip socks
None
None for an individual self-guided visit. Booking required for school groups.
None for hiking; ski/snowshoe pass at the nordic centre
None; Asters guided outings on registration (online programme)
None; book bike/e-bike rentals in the valley
None; sign-up for guided outings on the Asters website
Nordic Event 74
Nordic Event 74 XQV0Z — Dog sledding & rides
Nordic Event 74 dog sledding XQV0Z
Nordic area

Nordic area of 25 km (skating and classic) with breathtaking views of XQV0Z and the Aravis. Suitable for beginners and advanced. Night skiing on Saturdays from December to March. Welcome chalet, equipment rental.
Nordic dog team on snow at Plaine-Joux, XQV0Z, XQV1Z, XQV2Z

Nordic nights every Thursday evening in winter (night cross-country skiing under the stars, 10 km lit 6 pm–8:30 pm). French club cross-country skiing championship in March. Annual Glières battle commemorations end of March (26 March). Guided tours, documentary screenings, educational workshops and temporary exhibitions organised by the XQV0Z Department throughout the opening season of the Maquis Memory building (April–September).
Nordic skiing domain with 36 km of groomed trails (classic and skate) and 17 km of snowshoe routes. Renowned cross-country skiing site in the region, open December to March weather permitting.
North American and Asian species (tulip tree, ginkgo) put on a firework display of yellows and reds in October-November. A much-loved photo spot.
Northern XQV0Z, chemin des Fins. Public car parks nearby.
Not accepted (hotel policy)
Not accessible by pushchair or wheelchair (forest trail with elevation gain)
Not accessible by stroller or wheelchair. Challenging hike reserved for experienced hikers.
Not accessible by stroller or wheelchair. Mountain trail with rocky sections, roots and steep sections. Exposed sections on the Sentier des Crêtes (cables and railings).
Not accessible by wheelchair or stroller. Mountain trail with elevation gain and rocky sections.
Not accessible for mobility impaired or strollers — steep trail, significant elevation gain, cable-equipped passages
Not accessible to people with reduced mobility (145 steps without lift). Guides available at the top to answer questions from visitors unable to climb.
Not accessible with stroller or wheelchair (steep trails, rocky passages)
Not allowed
Not allowed (historic farmhouse with sensitive collections)
Not allowed (including on leash, in a bag, or in arms). Only guide dogs are accepted.
Not allowed (sensitive collections)
Not allowed inside
Not allowed inside the rink
Not allowed on courses for safety reasons
Not allowed on the beach. Off-season: tolerated for walks, favor the tree-lined area on the south side (toward XQV0Z)
Not allowed on the courses
Not allowed within the playground
Not allowed. Dogs not permitted in XQV0Z (even on leash) to preserve the tranquility of freely roaming animals. Secure kennel with water available at entrance.
Not always for individual entry; mandatory for birthdays and recommended at busy times.
Not directly from the trail; XQV0Z municipal beach is right next door.
Not directly in the park, but the Impérial XQV0Z beach adjoins it: a municipal beach supervised in summer, with paid access in high season. The Parc de l'Imperial extends to the lawns of the beach.

Not for the trail itself. Access to the Smugglers' Audio Trail is free; the audio files are downloaded by visitors themselves. Renting a battery or an audio guide at the tourist office or the Vieille Douane may be paid.
Not for walk-in. Recommended for birthdays and busy days.

Not in summer 2026: the Plan Joran gondola is closed from 4 May to 30 November 2026 for construction. In winter, pedestrian access is not permitted on Plan Joran (skiers only). Full public reopening is planned for December 2026.
Not in the CCSTI itself, but the La Turbine hub has a cinema nearby.
Not in the week, recommended on weekends. Groups/private hire: contact the bar.
Not listed — please check official site or Géoportail

Not on the beach and coves. Dogs are strictly prohibited on the beach, grassy coves and riverside paths at XQV0Z. They are allowed on leash on upper trails around the lake and on the south shore between fishing pontoons and the play area.
Not on the footbridges: dogs are not allowed on the trail of the XQV0Z. They are however allowed on the free Chemin des Châteaux loop around the site.
Not permitted
Not permitted at the leisure complex
Not permitted on the estate
Not permitted on tree-top courses (safety reasons).
Not permitted on treetop courses (activity at height)
Not recommended for children or beginners. The route is vertical, athletic and very exposed. Minimum recommended age: 13 years, with good physical condition and guide supervision.
Not recommended for children under 1.20 m tall. From age 12-14 with good fitness and no fear of heights. Professional supervision mandatory for children. The 15 m footbridge can be intimidating.
Not recommended for infants — consult a doctor before ascending with very young children.
Not recommended in winter (snow, ice, big climb). In summer: possible but the dog-sled activity closes outside the snow season.
Not recommended. The water at XQV0Z and the torrent is glacial year-round. The site is primarily for observing the waterfall and gorge.
Not required
Not required (tickets on site only; groups by e-mail)
Not required (tickets on site; groups on request)
Not required for aquatic access. For the spa area, booking may be needed depending on demand (especially in high season).
Not required for aquatic. Spa: booking possible depending on demand.
Not required for free access; guided outings by booking.
Not required for open bouldering. Recommended for the park in high season, classes, courses and birthdays.
Not required for open climbing. Recommended for classes, courses, birthdays.
Not required for public sessions
Not required for public sessions; recommended for Eisstock tournaments
Not required for visits; recommended for birthdays
Not suitable for people with reduced mobility. Minimum height required: 1.10 m for discovery course, 1.40 m for main course.
Not suitable for persons with reduced mobility or strollers (uphill trail, rough terrain, ruins)
Not suitable for pushchairs or wheelchairs. Steep trail with muddy terrain in places. Good hiking boots essential.
Not suitable for pushchairs or wheelchairs. Technical trail with final descent on rungs. Hiking boots essential. Not recommended for children under 7 for the descent.
Not suitable for strollers and wheelchairs. Forest trail with stream crossings, terrain sometimes slippery.
Not suitable for strollers or persons with reduced mobility. Rocky terrain, exposed passages secured by cables, steps carved into rock. Children from age 5 with supervision.

On site, a
On the Birds Trail, set off early in the morning: that is when the mountain birds are most active around the Col de Bassachaux. Bring binoculars and walk quietly.
On the Lappiaz mine trail, you discover the old XQV0Z slate quarry worked from 1856 to 1963, educational panels on the extraction of the 'Grey Gold', and an orientation table with panoramic views.
On the Route des Grandes Alpes, the Dranse has carved a dizzying canyon climbed on walkways bolted to the rock — a site developed for over 130 years, open even in the rain.
On the XQV0Z Art Nature Trail, each walker can create and leave an ephemeral land-art work in stone, wood or soil — nature as an open-air studio.

On the XQV0Z-XQV1Z plain, the Ailes Anciennes XQV2Z association has been safeguarding French aeronautical heritage since 1992: aircraft, helicopters and gliders undergoing restoration, a flight simulator and the workshop are open to the public.
On the shore of Lake XQV0Z, the Jardins de l'Europe form an English-style arboretum laid out in 1863: 250 remarkable trees, free 24/7 access.

On the surface, a developed circuit of paths and walkways provides access to the top of the waterfall where visitors can admire its 40-meter drop. The site also offers two 150-meter-long zip-lines that soar above the waterfall at 40 meters height, as well as a snack-restaurant with panoramic terrace, picnic area, children's play area, and a shop selling local products and souvenirs.
On-demand coaching to analyse lines, braking and improvement points.

One of Europe's largest paragliding sites — up to 200 flights per day in season. Several schools offer tandem flights (≈ €100-130).
One of the best-known canyons of the XQV0Z area: optional jumps (1-8 m), moderate abseils and slides in a wooded setting. Family-friendly from age 10.
One of the finest sunsets in the region. Many bivouac campers pitch tents to witness the spectacular nocturnal display over the lake. Clear westward views.
One of the longest via ferratas in the Aravis range: 1100 m of aerial route at the Col de la Colombière, suspension bridge, panoramic views of the massif.

One of the only certified hang gliding sites in XQV0Z. Landing mandatory at Marlioz for deltas. Practice reserved for experienced pilots.
One of the region's flagship paragliding sites — Salève Airlines and others offer tandem baptism flights from the summit.
One of the world's most beautiful free flight sites facing XQV0Z. Car access 1360 m, wheelchair accessible, paragliding and hang gliding. Free, year-round at XQV1Z.
One section of beach marked with signs is officially reserved for naturist practice. Secluded area that respects the site's peaceful character.
One-hour loop between the Maquis Memory building and the Monument, with 12 reading tables and 2 orientation tables. Free adult and children booklets. Free access.
One-on-one lessons with a qualified coach, to make fast progress on a technical point (serve, volley, baseline).

Onkart welcomes families, groups of friends, stag/hen parties, business seminars and birthdays. Kids can drive from around age 7 on specific karts, teens from age 12 on leisure karts, and adults can access the full range. Packages run from a few-minute session to full grand prix events (practice, qualifying, final, podium). Mandatory safety briefing, equipment (overalls, helmet, head sock) supplied — closed shoes required.

Online booking (via padel apps like TeniUp or directly with the club) strongly recommended, especially evenings and weekends. Prices around €20-30/hour for court rental (shared between 4 players), with hour passes and subscriptions. Racquet rental available (~€5). The centre is near XQV0Z, accessible by car (free parking) from XQV1Z/Le Fayet or Saint-Gervais.
Online booking at acro-aventures.com/talloires. Recommended during peak season (July-August). Payment by card, cheque, holiday cheques or cash accepted.

Online booking at indianaventures.com or on-site purchase at the entry to XQV0Z. Reservation is advised in July-August for activities (adventure course, relaxation areas) and mandatory for unusual accommodation.

Online booking is available at jardin5sens.net (ticket office). It is recommended during peak season (July–August). You can also purchase your ticket directly at the XQV0Z entrance desk on the day of your visit.

Online booking is essential, especially on weekends and evenings. Prices range from ~€22 to €30 per person depending on scenario and group size (the price drops with more players). Several rooms can be booked simultaneously for larger groups. La Grande Évasion is located in XQV0Z close to the centre, accessible by public transport, bike or car (parking nearby). 100% indoor activity, ideal in rainy weather or evening.

Online booking is highly recommended at weekends and during school holidays: slots fill fast. Non-slip socks (sold on site, around €3) are compulsory and reusable. Plan around €12-16 for a 1-hour session at full rate, with duo deals, memberships and birthday rates. Lockers, snack bar and a parent corner with wifi complete the facilities. Ideal in rainy weather, in winter or to burn off energy after school.
Online booking is strongly recommended
Online booking is strongly recommended on Wednesday afternoons, weekends and during school holidays. Sessions are timed and may sell out.

Online booking is strongly recommended on weekends and during school holidays, when the park is busy. Prices are around €15-19 for a 1h session, with family passes and subscriptions. The park is part of the Vitam leisure complex, which also includes a spa, water park, climbing gym and restaurant — perfect for a full day out. Easy access from the A40 motorway (XQV0Z exit), free on-site parking.
Online booking is strongly recommended on weekends, Wednesday afternoons and during school holidays. Mid-week in term time, walk-ins are usually possible but not guaranteed.

Online booking is strongly recommended, especially on Wednesday afternoons, weekends and during French and Swiss school holidays, even British half-term in ski season. Prices are around €10-15 per 1-hour session, with multi-session passes and group rates. Lockers, snack and drinks on site; free parking in front. Perfect as a family outing before or after a ski/hiking day, and easy to combine with the nearby XQV0Z aquatic centre.

Online booking on issuesecrete.com or by phone — strongly recommended on weekends, during school holidays and for groups. Prices are around €25-35 per person depending on the slot and group size, with discounts from 4 players and group packages for stag/hen and businesses. The venue is in a business park in XQV0Z, free parking on site. Quick access from XQV1Z, Geneva (via the lakeshore or the Swiss border) or XQV2Z via Bellegarde.
Online booking on lasermaxx74.fr or by phone, strongly recommended on weekends, school holidays and for any group.

Online booking on latelierdesenigmes.fr or by phone — strongly recommended on weekends, during school holidays and for groups. Located in the heart of XQV0Z: quick walking access from the train station and town centre, public car parks (Bonlieu, Hôtel de Ville, Courier) nearby. Combines well with a walk around the lake, the Old Town or a cinema.

Best in summer and early autumn, in dry weather. Absolutely avoid in storms or on wet rock. Southwest-facing: prefer mornings in heatwaves.
Open in winter season (December-May) and spring (late May-early July). Ideal après-ski or after hiking. Inflatable structure on Wednesday and Saturday afternoons.
Open mainly April to October. Hours vary with weather and season. See onkart.fr for daily opening.
Open spaces for jogging, stretching or free play. The park extends to the Clos Babuty and XQV0Z's green network.

Open to adults (16-18 depending on venue, sometimes with parental consent) and to children from age 8 in junior / low impact format (adapted markers, softer balls, reduced intensity). Very popular for birthdays, hen/stag parties and team building. Booking required with a minimum number of players (often 6-8). Wear sturdy covering clothes and sport or cleated shoes. Some paint splatter is unavoidable — bring a change of clothes.
Open to all from 6-7 years (kids' introduction). Licensed and casual players.

Open year-round 7 days a week. Wednesday and Saturday are busy (families, kids' classes). Weekday evenings popular with adults after work. Booking advised for classes and camps.
Open year-round Monday to Saturday (closed bank holidays). Spring and summer are the best seasons to see the kids and enjoy the donkey walks. Winter: shorter visit, but the shop stays open.
Open year-round by reservation. Busy on Fri/Sat evenings and during winter school holidays. Confirm slots by phone.
Open year-round depending on weather conditions. Best period: April to November. Not practicable in rain, snow, ice or storms. In summer, prefer morning to avoid heat.
Open year-round except annual maintenance closure. Weekly schedule (public/schools/clubs) published by the town hall.

Open year-round subject to favorable weather conditions. Best in summer and early autumn. South-facing exposure allows spring and autumn climbing. Avoid in thunderstorms or on wet rock (very slippery). Arrive early morning in high season to avoid crowds (frequent school groups).
Open year-round with free access. Supervised July–August (10am–7pm).
Open year-round, every day on request (SMS booking required mentioning "Musée du Ski" on +33 (0)7 62 21 88 68, at most 2 days before the visit). Verified on 10 June 2026.
Open year-round, extended hours in school holidays. Public slots on the website.

Open year-round, it suits all-terrain strollers and dogs on leads — a gentle alternative to the more demanding hikes of the nearby Saleve. With the Vitam water park, the Saleve cable car and the A40 motorway nearby, it is also an easy warm-up to discover the Genevois with the family.
Open year-round, mainly Wed/weekends/holidays. Extended hours during school holidays. Timed sessions.
Open year-round, panoramic terrace facing Mont Blanc. Traditional Savoyard cuisine (raclette, tartiflette, mountain burger) and snacks. Group evening reservations available.
Open year-round, typically 10am-10pm. Seasonal hours: see official site.

Open year-round. Lunchtime slots (11:30-13:30) are quieter on weekdays for lap swimming. Wednesday, Saturday and Sunday afternoons are busy with families. In summer, the retractable roof turns the centre into an almost-outdoor pool.
Open year-round. Peak flowering in June — the best time. May and September still offer a pleasant ambience. In winter the garden is accessible but botanical interest is limited.
Open year-round. Peak times late afternoon/evening and Wednesdays (school sessions). For quieter sessions: mornings or early afternoon. Outdoor walls open May to September in good weather.
Open year-round. Shop and self-guided visit: Monday-Saturday 9-12 and 14-18. Closed Sundays and public holidays.

Parmelan is accessible year-round, but the best season runs from May to October. Trails are passable from snow melt (late May–early June depending on snow cover). In summer, start early to avoid weekend crowds and heat on the climb. Autumn offers remarkable golden light on the cliffs. In winter, hiking is possible with snowshoes or ski touring, but the Grand Montoir becomes dangerous with snow or ice (fall risk). The refuge is closed from mid-September to mid-May. Avoid foggy days on the plateau: visibility reduced in the lapiaz and faults.

Plan 1 h 30 to 2 h 30 for a complete visit to XQV0Z: interpretation centre, 3D visit, remains of the abbatial church, medieval gardens and conventional spaces.
Plan 1 h to 1 h 30 for a visit to the Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z, covering the permanent display and the current temporary exhibition.
Plan 1-2h to walk the trail at a leisurely pace and enjoy the chalet and information boards.
Plan 1.5 to 3 hours to walk the marked trails at XQV0Z. A complete tour with stops at the birdwatching observatory and exploration of the Bois du Vernay fossil dunes takes approximately 2.5 hours.
Plan 1h30 to 2h30 for a full course depending on holes and your level. A nice combination of sport and mountain walking.
Plan 1h30-2h on site: safety briefing (~10 min), gear (~15 min), 1-3 driving sessions (10-30 min each) with breaks, debrief and lap times. For groups, half a day.
Plan 2 to 3 hours total: meeting at XQV0Z, briefing, shuttle to the Col de la Forclaz take-off, waiting for conditions, flight (15-45 min depending on package), pickup at landing.
Plan 2-3 hours of cycling round trip on the main segment, or a full day if you combine village visits.
Plan 2-3h on site: safety briefing, kitting up, several 10-15 min games with debriefs, totalling 1h30-2h of actual play. Half a day for groups or birthdays.
Plan 45 minutes for the basic out-and-back of the XQV0Z Art Nature Trail, up to 1 h 30 with an extension to the Croix de Vin, the GR Balcon du XQV1Z and a visit to the village of Charly.
Plan about 1h30 on site: briefing (~10-15 min), game (60 min), debriefing and photo (~10-15 min).
Plan about 30 minutes round trip from Parc Biolley parking to the XQV0Z platform, including viewing time. The unmarked trail adds 10–15 minutes.
Plan around 1 hour for the visit to the Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z, led personally by the owner of the historic farmhouse.
Plan around 1h30 to 2 hours on site: welcome, briefing, gear check, weighing, climb to the viaduct, jump, descent, debrief and photos. The jump itself lasts a few seconds.
Plan around €40-50 for a half-day intro and €70-80 for a more committing day. 2026 prices to confirm with local caving instructors.
Plan around €70-100 per person depending on the package. Photo/video options are often extra (~€15-30). 2026 prices to confirm with the operator.

Ride down the Slidewinder, a 10 m high tube slide half-pipe style, for fast descents and adrenaline rushes. Reserved for the more daring (adults and teens).

Ride up in under 5 min from XQV0Z to 1,100 m altitude on cables opened in 1932 — a cross-border heritage.
Ride ~€50-80/person, kennel visit ~€10-15/person, outing ~€80-150/person. 2026 to confirm.
Rides are open from around 3-4 years, in a tandem with an adult. For the youngest, some mushers offer only the kennel visit. Check with the kennel.

Rides are open to families with children (depending on age — usually from 3-4 in a tandem with adult), couples and friend groups. Typical format: 30 min to 1h ride on a dedicated circuit, or longer 2-3h outing for enthusiasts. Kennel visits are also offered to discover the dogs, their behaviour and the Nordic lifestyle. The musher drives; the passenger sits in the sled to enjoy the scenery.

Run with a local cultural-service ethos, XQV0Z offers an accessible programme blending mainstream, art-house, family films and event collaborations with local associations. The cinema welcomes families, couples, students and film buffs in a friendly setting where comfortable seats, quality sound and digital HD projection (with laser projection where available) ensure a modern experience. Ticketing typically includes full price, reduced rate (students, under 14/18, jobseekers), the French youth Pass Culture (ages 15-20), morning shows and a season pass (5- or 10-ticket booklet) to lower the per-seat cost.
Running theme of the trail: 10 panels explain the life of the young chamois and other mountain animals.

Several packages are offered: beginner outings for families with children (from around 8-10 depending on the canyon), sporty routes for fit adults, fun aquatic canyons for groups of friends or stag/hen parties, and technical full-day outings for confirmed practitioners. Full gear is provided (5 mm neoprene wetsuit, helmet, harness, boots), so you only need to bring swimwear, a towel and closed shoes that can get wet (such as sports trainers). Groups are kept small (4 to 8 people per instructor) to ensure safety and quality.

Several parts of the XQV0Z at XQV1Z are listed Monument Historique (1994, PA00118442) for their architectural value and decoration: 17th-century wall paintings and an 1840 panoramic wallpaper in one of the salons.

Since 2020, XQV0Z no longer offers public guided tours. It welcomes only hotel guests, as well as clients for weddings, seminars, and private events. The site has been listed as a Historic Monument since 1990.

Since 29 July 2024, road access to Col des Contrebandiers is closed to vehicles. You must park by the lake (XQV0Z or XQV1Z-le-Vieux) and hike up the marked Mont Baron trail. Allow 1 h 30 to 2 h of walking.

Since 29 July 2024, the road has been closed to motor vehicles as part of the Mont Veyrier Massif Management Plan 2024-2029. The closure aims to facilitate emergency vehicle access (often blocked by chaotic parking), protect biodiversity and regulate growing visitor numbers.
Since April 2018 the centre has been managed by

Since July 2024, vehicle access to Col des Contrebandiers and Pré Vernet has been prohibited by municipal decree. Hikers now start from the small harbour of XQV0Z-le-Vieux (447 m), adding approximately 600 metres of extra elevation. The trail first crosses beech and spruce forest before emerging onto the panoramic ridge. Halfway up, the concrete platform of the former XQV1Z cable car (1,254 m) remains, equipped with an orientation table — the aerial station was dismantled in 2001 following a tragic accident in 1998.

Since its creation, XQV0Z has conducted an important conservation and reintroduction programme for the white-tailed eagle in the Lake XQV1Z region, visible in the dedicated eagle house. The park also operates a winter park located at 1,500 m altitude at Pointe de Nyon (XQV2Z), open December to March during ski season.

Since the mid-18th century, the cascade has been a popular stop for travelers journeying from Geneva to Chamonix. Tourists organized their departures to arrive at sunrise, when light painted a rainbow in the water mist. The site once powered nearby mills and sawmills. Today, a discreet small hydroelectric plant is integrated at the top of the cascade. The site was designated a Natural Site of France in June 2024.

Spring
Spring (April-June) for songs and nesting; autumn (September-October) for migrations.

Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are ideal for enjoying the park with fewer crowds and changing forest colours. Summer (July-August) offers daily opening but more visitors. Choose Wednesdays and weekends outside July-August for a balance between availability and tranquillity.
Spring (April–June) is ideal for XQV0Z: flow is maximum thanks to snowmelt and the heart shape is spectacular. The site is also impressive after heavy rain.
Spring (April–June) is the ideal period for the XQV0Z: flow is at its maximum thanks to snowmelt and the Pélerins Glacier. Summer offers a cool refuge, and autumn brings beautiful forest colours.
Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) are ideal for XQV0Z: fewer crowds, pleasant temperatures, and changing forest colours. Avoid summer (very busy) and winter (slippery in snow or ice).
Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) are the best seasons to visit the XQV0Z: water flow is strong and the potholes are clearly visible. Summer remains pleasant thanks to forest shade.
Spring (April–May) is ideal to see XQV0Z with maximum flow thanks to snowmelt. Autumn (October) offers a flamboyant setting with the colours of the beech forest.

Spring (April–May) is the best season to admire the waterfall at full power, thanks to snowmelt generously feeding the two Nants. Flow is then impressive and the spectacle breathtaking. Summer (June–September) offers a different experience: the forest trail is shaded and pleasant even in strong heat, and mist generated by the waterfall creates a refreshing atmosphere particularly valued during heat waves. This is also canyoning season, with groups rappelling the main cascade. Autumn (October–November) is quieter, with beautiful forest colors and reduced crowds, ideal for enjoying the site in peace. In winter, the waterfall freezes partially or completely depending on temperatures, offering an ethereal spectacle prized by photographers—but the trail can be slippery and dangerous in frost or after snowfall. Absolutely avoid hiking during rain or just after a shower: the final cliff-side trail becomes extremely slippery on wet limestone, and water spray makes progression hazardous.
Spring (April–May) is the best time to visit the XQV0Z with maximum flow during snowmelt. Summer allows you to enjoy the swimming pool at the base of the waterfall.
Spring (February to June) is ideal for observing breeding amphibians and migratory birds. Autumn offers beautiful golden colors in the reedbeds.
Spring (May-June) for strong flows and thrills, summer (July-August) for families and playful waters, September for fewer crowds. The season runs from April to October depending on conditions.
Spring and early autumn
Spring and summer (April–September) are ideal for discovering the biodiversity of XQV0Z: meadow flowering, bird song, and free guided tours in July–August. Autumn offers beautiful colours.
Spring and summer: young animals are visible, temperatures are pleasant, and the poultry yard is very active.

The "Happy Accro" slot (4–5 pm) at XQV0Z offers a one-hour session at a reduced price — a clever afternoon option.
The "mysterious" name of the waterfall originates from a
The 'Sortie à Fred' allows you to exit after 500 m (grade D) for those wishing to shorten the route or test their level.
The 1.5 km loop takes 20 to 30 minutes at a leisurely pace. Allow 1 hour if you take time to observe wildlife and read the educational panels.

The 10 courses at XQV0Z differ in number of games, height and difficulty. Pitchounes (10 games, €6) for ages 2-3, Little hikers (27 games, €11) and Big hikers (37 games, €14) for children, Climbers (54 games, €17) and Alpinists (77 games, €23) for teens/adults, Everest (96 games, €26) for most athletic (14 m high). All with continuous safety line.

The 1st section (Le Cabri, AD+) is accessible to accompanied beginner ferratists. Sections 2 and 3 (TD) demand arm strength, stamina and good exposure management. The full link represents a sporty 3–5-hour challenge.
The 2 Kid courses at XQV0Z are designed for children between 1.05 m and 1.35 m tall — zip lines, bridges, jumps sized for them.
The 20,000-object collection of the Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z was assembled over a lifetime by Bernard Lacroix, a son of the village and a great collector of Alpine ethnology.
The 2025–2026 temporary exhibition
The 2026 temporary exhibition at the Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is "XQV1Z, town or countryside?", running from 11 February 2026 to 2 January 2027.

The 4,200 m² building now houses 700 m² of exhibition rooms across two levels, highly equipped to host international exhibitions (painting, sculpture, photography), the Charles-Ferdinand Ramuz media library spanning 700 m², and a congress space of 2,200 m² with a 382-seat auditorium. The building has recovered its central dome measuring 8.5 m in diameter reaching 35 m in height, its two stone campaniles adorned with flamed sandstone, and the murals by Jean Benderly in the vestibule.
The 750-meter track, considered
The Ajon pasture hosts herds of XQV0Z cattle during summer. The transhumance in late May is a festive event followed by hundreds of people.
The Alchemist

The Alpine Coaster is open year-round except for an annual closure from early November to mid-December. Summer (June–August) combines the toboggan and the full amusement park. Winter (December–April) adds the ski area. Weekends and school holidays: very busy, arrive early.
The Arve is a major ecological corridor: birdlife (grey heron, dipper, kingfisher), riparian forest, pioneer plants. Several interpretation panels along the way.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z route is open 24/7 year-round (snow possible in winter). The booklet can be picked up during the opening hours of the valley's Tourist Offices. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z route is open 24/7 year-round. The booklet can be picked up during the opening hours of the valley's Tourist Offices. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is best enjoyed between spring and autumn. XQV1Z sits at 1,260 m: snow may cover the path in winter and make the clues harder to read.
The Au Fil du Rail XQV0Z trail game is entirely free. No booking, no ticket: just pick up the booklet at the Tourist Office and play independently. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Au Fil du Rail trail game in XQV0Z covers 3 km for about 1 hr 30 of walking at a family pace, with stops to answer the booklet's questions.

The Au Fil du Rail trail game in XQV0Z is a free family quiz route created by the Chamonix Valley Tourist Office. About 2 hours of walking around XQV1Z SNCF station, booklet in hand, to discover the village and its heritage.
The Au Fil du Rail trail game in XQV0Z takes about 2 hours at a family pace, with stops to answer the questions. The duration can be adjusted to suit the children's ages and walking pace.

The Auberge de Miribel, located at La Glappaz between XQV0Z and XQV1Z, is a mountain inn accessible from the hiking trails. Traditional Savoyard cuisine, open in summer and some autumn weekends. Bring cash.

The Bike Park runs from late May to early September, with peak season in July and August. The UCI MTB World Cup takes place late August (crowds, restricted MTB access on the Mont-Chéry side). For hiking, July–August offers the best alpine flower displays. In winter, the resort opens mid-December and is busiest during French school holidays.
The Birds Trail at XQV0Z is maintained from 15 June to 15 September every day, weather permitting. Out of season, access to the pass can be difficult due to snow.
The Birds Trail follows the GR5 on a wide driveable track between the Col de Bassachaux and the Rochassons summit: it is an easy route, suitable for families with children old enough to walk.

The Birds Trail is designed to spot the mountain birds seen at XQV0Z - crossbill, nutcracker, alpine chough, common raven, ptarmigan depending on altitude - thanks to panels explaining their habitat, diet and nesting.
The Birds Trail starts at the Col de Bassachaux (1,778 m), 12 km from XQV0Z via the D 228. The pass has a large car park and a seasonal snack bar.
The Black Lily Investigation

The Boucles du Coteau are a network of 3 concentric walking loops (green, blue, red) in the commune of XQV0Z, between the village centre and the Amphion-les-Bains plain. Shaded woodland, views of Lake Geneva, 5.5 km and 200 m of elevation in total.
The Boucles du Coteau in XQV0Z are open 24/7 year-round. Daytime is recommended to follow the waymarking and enjoy the views over Lake Geneva. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The Boucles du Coteau network in XQV0Z offers 3 waymarked concentric loops: green (easy), blue (intermediate) and red (the longest), with 5 entry panels installed across the commune.

The Brévent Cable Car is open in summer (13 Jun–20 Sep 2026) 8:35–18:00 (8:20–18:00 Jul–Aug). Winter: 8:50, last descent 16:45–17:15. Closed 20 Apr–12 Jun 2026 for maintenance. Verified 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Brévent offers the most direct view of the Mont Blanc massif from Chamonix. Two sections — Planpraz gondola then cable car — lift you to 2,525 m directly facing the glaciers.
The Brévent summit is the most direct viewpoint on Mont Blanc from the Chamonix valley. 360° views: Aiguilles Rouges to the west, Mont Blanc massif to the east.
The Bun-J-Ride

The Bun-J-Ride is aimed at people in good health, from a minimum age (usually 14-16 depending on operator, parental consent required for minors) and within a defined weight range (typically ~40-110 kg). No previous experience required: personal commitment and trust in the equipment matter most. Jumps can be solo or tandem (two people attached together), with photo/video options to keep a memory of the fall. This is one of the most intense activities in XQV0Z, recommended for thrill-seekers and lovers of panoramic Aravis views.
The Bureau des Guides de XQV0Z offers supervised outings with equipment rental from €50 per person in groups.
The CGN runs the
The CVT covers all main disciplines:
The Calvary Ermitage Museum at XQV0Z is mainly open in July-August, Mon-Fri and Sunday 2 pm-6 pm, and during the European Heritage Days in September. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Calvary Ermitage Museum at XQV0Z is reached on foot via the Chemin du Calvaire from the town (1 h uphill through the 14 chapels), or by car in summer via a forest road.

The Calvary of XQV0Z, presented at the Calvary Ermitage Museum, includes 14 chapels and oratories retracing the stations of the Passion of Christ, built between 1840 and 1878 by Father Ambroise Martin.

The Calvary of XQV0Z, whose history is told at the Ermitage Museum, is the work of Father Ambroise Martin (1791-1863), parish priest of XQV1Z from 1820 until his death, who built 14 chapels between 1840 and 1878.
The Casino of XQV0Z is about 10 minutes by car from Geneva via the D1201, or via the A40, XQV1Z exit. Free parking on site.
The Casino of XQV0Z offers 112 slot machines: 11 video poker, 13 classic reels and 88 video reels from international brands.
The Chablais Museum housed in the XQV0Z charges admission for exhibitions; free for some audiences. Up-to-date prices on thononlesbains.com. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The Chablais Museum housed in the XQV0Z offers a permanent exhibition on the history of the territory (lake, fishing, boatbuilding, Savoyard life).
The Chartreux Cross is a cross engraved in stone at the base of the XQV0Z. It marked the former territorial boundary of Carthusian monks. A signpost with a yellow arrow indicates its exact location.
The Chartreux potholes are cavities carved by water erosion in the rock. Several natural basins succeed each other along the waterfall, forming a remarkable geological landscape.
The Chartreux potholes are natural cavities carved in rock by water erosion. At the XQV0Z, several natural basins succeed each other, forming a remarkable geological landscape.

The Chavannes gondola: Online booking is available at pass.lesgets.com, operated directly by SOLEGETS (no reseller). Day passes, single rides and multi-ride cards can be purchased. Tickets are also sold at the lower station ticket office.
The Chavannes gondola: Single ride adult: €7.20, child: €5.40. MTB day pass adult: €34, child: €26. 6-ride card: €27.50. 12-ride card: €49. Free for under 5s. Verified on 9 May 2026.
The Chavannes gondola: Summer peak season (July–August): daily 9:00 – 17:30. Winter: approx. 9:00 – 17:00. Exact dates on lesgets.com. Verified on 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The Chez El' studio is in XQV0Z (74000). Exact address to confirm on chez-el.com. Access by car (public car parks), bike or bus (Sibra).

The Children's Course at XQV0Z is accessible from 1 metre (approximately age 4-5). The Junior Course requires minimum 1.20 m. The Advanced Adult Course and Hop Up Challenge are accessible from 1.40 m. Acro'Filet, Sensory Forest, Treasure Hunt and Orienteering are accessible at all ages and heights.

The Château de Fonbonne is a heritage building in XQV0Z, a former 14th-century fortified manor. Its garden, laid out in 2011 by the town as a Herbularius (medieval-inspired medicinal garden), is freely accessible and features sage, rosemary, fennel and other aromatic herbs.

The Château de XQV0Z was built in 1304 by Hugh of XQV1Z on the Motte de XQV2Z to counter Savoy. Edward of Savoy besieged it for eleven days in 1305 and took it. The castle flourished until the 16th century (92 m long) before being burned down in 1564 and turned into a stone quarry.

The Château du Vuache, of which the 1579 stone is the last witness, was a 14th-century seigneurial stronghold in the County of Geneva. It was abandoned in the mid-17th century when the seat of the seigneury was moved to Faramaz, and its stones were reused.
The Cistercian site of the Abbaye d'Aulps (XQV0Z) is 10 min away by car and extends the heritage outing in the Vallée d'Aulps.
The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z costs €5 full price, €3 for young people, and is free for under-12s. Free entry on the first Sunday of the month (September-June). Verified 10 June 2026.
The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is at the Espace Carpano & Pons, 100 place du 11 Novembre, in the centre of XQV1Z (74300). Train station 500 m away.
The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is free on the first Sunday of every month (September to June), and year-round for under-12s.

The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z is open Tue-Sat 2 pm-6 pm from September to June, and every day 10 am-12 pm and 2 pm-6 pm in July-August. Annual closure 11-30 November. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at XQV0Z presents the world's largest collection of horological escapements, the history of the peasant clockmakers of the Arve and the industrial revolution of bar-turning.
The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm can only be visited on a guided tour led by the Guides du Patrimoine Savoie Mont Blanc — expert interpretation of Savoyard peasant life.
The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm combines with a visit to XQV0Z village (Jaysinia garden, Place du Gros Tilleul) and a Grand Massif day out.
The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm holds a catalogued collection of more than 2,000 period utensils: tools, cookware, clogs, sledges, looms.

The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm in XQV0Z is a traditional farm from 1815, opened to the public in 1994 as an ecomuseum. It presents peasant life in the Haut-Giffre valley, with a collection of 2,000 period utensils.
The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm is on Chemin du Clos Parchet in XQV0Z (74340), on the south-facing slopes above the village, about 10 minutes' walk from the centre. GPS: 46.101° N · 6.701° E.
The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm stands on the sunny adrets of XQV0Z — slopes traditionally inhabited where the farm produced its own food.
The Cobbler's Museum at XQV0Z displays the tools, sewing machines, manuals and Savoyard footwear of the village cobblers (300 craftsmen in 1880), under the arcades of the Trophée.

The Cobbler's Museum at XQV0Z is open mainly from June to August: June and low season Tue-Sat 10 am-12 pm and 1.30 pm-5.30 pm; July-August extended hours 10 am-12 pm and 3 pm-7/8 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Cobbler's Museum is at 9 place du Trophée, in the heart of the medieval town of XQV0Z (74540), 20 min from XQV1Z via the A41 (Alby exit).
The Cobbler's Museum of XQV0Z was founded in 1980 and installed in the medieval town in 1999 to preserve the memory of the village's 300 cobblers who made Savoyard footwear in the 19th century.
The Combe des Fours is home to rich alpine biodiversity: marmots, occasional ibex, birds of prey, altitude flora. Good naturalist spot in June-July.
The Combe des Fours offers an exceptional panorama of XQV0Z, the Aravis and the Fiz massif. The area also has other natural curiosities such as
The Compagnie des Guides de Saint-Gervais offers supervised outings (€50 section 1, €80 section 2, €150 full route) to discover the route safely.
The Coteau Loops are a good option for half-day families looking for fresh air between the lake and the foothills, with shaded passages in summer and lake panoramas in clear weather.

The Counts' Castle of Ternier and La Poype are two fortified structures next to each other on the same hill. The Counts' Castle was a comital residence; La Poype was a fortified manor of the Ternier family. The two were separated by a mound and two ditches.

The Counts' Castle of Ternier dates from the 13th century. It originally belonged to the Ternier family before being ceded to the Counts of Geneva and then to the Counts of Savoy. Damaged by the Bernese and Savoyard wars, it was gradually abandoned.
The Counts' Castle of Ternier is on the hill of Ternier, a hamlet under the commune of XQV0Z (74160), about 2 km south of the town centre, in the Pays de Genève. GPS: 46.139° N · 6.089° E.

The Counts' Castle of Ternier is one of the two medieval remains on the hill of Ternier at XQV0Z. It was the seat of the châtellenie of Ternier, ceded to the Counts of Geneva and then to the Counts of Savoy. Today: wall sections and ditches in open access.
The Counts' Castle of Ternier site is open 24/7, year-round. Daytime is preferable to read the remains and enjoy the panorama. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The Dard torrent originates from the melting of the Pélerins Glacier at around 2,300 metres altitude. This glacier, highly sensitive to climate variations, accumulates meltwater in sub-glacial cavities. In 2009, the bridge downstream of the waterfall was swept away by the rupture of one of these water pockets — a glacial flood phenomenon whose most famous example in the Alps remains the one that destroyed the Saint-Gervais thermal baths in 1892. Today, the study and understanding of these phenomena allow for better control of this risk.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z costs €5 for adults and €3.50 for children. Group rates on request on +33 (0)4 50 72 39 68. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z displays 19th-century hand pumps, historic fire engines, copper helmets, period uniforms and archive videos of major interventions.
The Departmental Fire-Fighters' Museum of XQV0Z is open in May, June and September on weekends 2 pm-6 pm, and every day (public holidays included) 10.30 am-7 pm in July-August. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Discovery Walking Trail of XQV0Z Village is an easy walk in the Vallée d'Aulps, dotted with reading and drawing tables, that invites you to explore nature, learn to identify trees and sketch the landscape.
The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges has 145 steps to reach the summit and panoramic gallery. The climb takes approximately 10 minutes. Bring suitable shoes and avoid very hot weather.
The Doran valley offers an exceptional photographic setting. Best light in late morning; spray more pronounced in spring during snowmelt.
The Dranse de Sous-le-Pas is a designated mountain stream: brown trout, dipper, dragonflies. Very photogenic wild stretches.
The Dronières Lake loop is 2 km long, about 30 minutes on foot. The path is stabilised, accessible to strollers, and starts from the tennis car park.
The Dronières Lake loop is accessible to strollers thanks to its stabilised path. The adjacent aquatic centre is equipped for people with reduced mobility.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z features 7 playful wooden modules that make the walker's ball roll, descend and run — wooden flippers, ramps, bends.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z is designed for families with children from age 3-4: easy trail, gradual descent, child-friendly modules.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z runs during the summer season (mid-June/early July to end of August), depending on the summer lift calendar. Check forfait.laclusaz.com. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z takes 30 to 45 minutes to descend, depending on your pace and the time spent at each module. Longer if continued with the Luge des Bois at the Bossonnet.
The Débaroule at XQV0Z unfolds at altitude, through the pastures and forests of the Aravis massif, with views over the range and the village.
The Débaroule descends from the arrival of the Patinoire cable car (XQV0Z) towards the Bossonnet area, along an easy, shaded trail.
The Débaroule is a playful high-altitude trail in XQV0Z: from the top of the Patinoire cable car, walkers descend on foot to the Bossonnet, accompanying their wooden ball through 7 playful modules.
The Débaroule is an original outdoor summer activity in XQV0Z: a nature-based playful trail that makes a change from the classic high-altitude treetop courses.
The Dévalkart is in the heart of the XQV0Z / La Croix-Fry resort, in the Aravis massif (74230). Car access from XQV1Z via XQV2Z (~40 min) or XQV3Z (~15 min).
The Dévalkart is mainly a summer activity (June-September). In winter, the resort is open for alpine skiing and traditional snow sledging takes over.
The Dévalkart is mainly open from June to September, depending on weather and resort opening. Check daily opening at manigod.com or the Tourist Office.
The Erraticaborne is a panoramic and artistic shelter installed on the XQV0Z Art Nature Trail: an original stop combining refuge, viewpoint and artwork, to discover along the way.
The Eterlou Trail is open every day from 1 May to 31 October, weather permitting. In winter, the site is used for tobogganing and snow play.
The Eterlou Trail takes about 50 minutes, for a loop of about 600 m with no difficulty.
The Eterlou Walking Trail is at Pré-la-Joux, right next to the chairlift, in the high-altitude sector of XQV0Z.

The Fairy Trail at XQV0Z is accessible from age 3 (under parental supervision). The main course is accessible from 1.20 m height (adult supervision mandatory for children under 12). Canyoning is accessible from age 8 with an adult.
The Faune museum and the Maison de la Belle Vallée are next door. The village is also a starting point for hiking and mountain biking in the Brevon valley.
The Faune museum and the Maison de la Belle Vallée are right next door — visit can be extended over a half-day.
The Ferme de Chosal Trail features donkeys, sheep, goats, billy goats, pigs, hens, pheasants, guinea fowl, rabbits and guinea pigs in their respective pens.
The Ferme de Chosal Trail is open from 5 April to 3 November, 10 am to 6 pm, with last entry at 4.30 pm (information verified on the official website on 10 June 2026, subject to seasonal changes).
The Ferme de Chosal is at 98 route de l'usine Chosal, 74350 XQV0Z, in XQV1Z - 10 min from XQV2Z via the D 1201.
The Ferme de Chosal is run by an integration organisation employing people with disabilities: your ticket supports this social project.
The Ferme de Chosal offers birthday packages and educational workshops for children (booking required).

The Follow the Fly trail takes its name from the nail the cobblers of XQV0Z fixed under shoe soles, whose head looked like fly wings, and from the many fly-fishers on the Chéran. Flies drawn on the ground mark out the route.
The Fox Trail starts from XQV0Z church, in the heart of the village. You cross the old bridge, pass the library, then reach rue Bidon and the path along the Dranse.
The GR5 continues towards Switzerland (Lac de Tanay) and allows a longer loop for seasoned hikers.

The Glières Plateau can be visited year-round. Summer (May to October) is ideal for hiking, discovering the Monument and alpine pastures, and buying cheese from farms. Days are long, the plateau verdant, herds grazing. The site attracts many visitors in July–August. In winter (December to March), the plateau transforms into a renowned Nordic skiing domain (cross-country skiing, snowshoeing). The atmosphere is peaceful, landscapes snow-covered, the Monument takes on a special dimension. Spring (April–May) offers variable conditions (residual snow possible) and risk of rockfall on the road from XQV0Z. Autumn (September–October) combines calm, soft light and possible early snow at altitude.
The Gourmet

The Grand Montoir passage on XQV0Z is airy and steep but secured by cables, chains and metal handrails. It can alarm those sensitive to heights. If unsure, ascend and descend via the Petit Montoir (longer but without exposed sections). Avoid the Grand Montoir in wet weather, snow or ice.
The Grand Parc d'XQV0Z (medieval theme park) is right next door and lets you extend the family day after the heritage trail.
The Grand Parc d'XQV0Z and the Parc des Dronières are nearby - ideal for a family day in the Genevois.
The Grand Tour

The Grands Montets is a winter ski destination (December–early May). It is completely closed throughout summer 2026 due to the new 3S cable car construction. Full reopening — including access to the 3,275 m summit — is planned for December 2026 according to Compagnie du XQV0Z. For summer 2026, visitors wanting a view over the Argentière glacier should consider the XQV1Z or Montenvers instead.

The Grands Montets is included in the Chamonix Le Pass: adult day pass 59.20–74 € depending on the period. An AlpiSki AGM ski touring ticket is also available at 36.80 € for adults (desk purchase only). Verified 28 April 2026.
The Grands Montets offers one of the only direct views of the Drus (3,754 m) and Aiguille Verte (4,122 m) from the slopes in XQV0Z, from the Lognan sector.
The Grands Montets ski area is being fully rebuilt after the 2018 fire. New Renzo Piano 3S cable car expected December 2026. Winter access via Plan Joran gondola (skiers only).
The Granges de Servette Museum is open from 29 June to 31 August, every day 3 pm to 7 pm. Occasional openings during the Heritage Days in September. Verified 10 June 2026.
The Granges de Servette Museum presents a permanent contemporary-art collection (the Lyon school since 1958) and more than 1,000 antique tools of traditional crafts.
The Grotte de Balme in XQV0Z: iconic natural cave in the Arve valley, a playground for cavers. Guided outings with a qualified instructor recommended.
The Grotte de Balme in XQV0Z: natural cave in the Arve valley, guided caving recommended. Seasonal access.

The Grotte de Balme is well-suited to a first taste of caving for anyone wanting to discover this discipline that combines exploration, geological observation and physical commitment. Several guide offices in the Arve and XQV0Z valleys offer half-day or full-day guided outings, with gear provided (oversuit, helmet with headlamp, harness depending on the route). Outings are usually open to participants from age 10-12, in good physical condition and not claustrophobic; some sections may involve narrow passages or short abseils.
The Herbularius garden at the Château de Fonbonne in XQV0Z is freely accessible, especially during the growing season (spring to autumn). Check ville-evian.fr. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The Herbularius garden at the Château de Fonbonne is laid out in raised beds enclosed by chestnut wattle, with a central water feature and a bower — faithful medieval aesthetics.

The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne mainly features medieval-inspired aromatic and medicinal plants: sage, rosemary, fennel, horseradish, lemon balm, arranged in raised beds around a water feature.
The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne offers a carefully composed photo setting: chestnut wattle, water feature, bower flowering with climbing roses.
The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne offers a free stroll among medieval-inspired medicinal and aromatic plants, in free access.

The Heritage Guides of the Pays d'art et d'histoire run themed walks ("Castles and lords of the Genevois") that include XQV0Z alongside other châtellenies. Free walk around the village; the ruins themselves are visible from marked paths.

The Heritage Trail showcases small built heritage (fountains, ovens, washhouses, wayside crosses), village chapels including the Saint-Jacques chapel in Charly (bell tower listed as a Historic Monument in 1975) and the history of the Mont Sion granite cutters.
The Hermit's Overhang

The Jaillet Gondola opens summer 2026: 4 July–30 August. Exact hours to confirm on lesportesdumontblanc.fr — 2025 reference: 9:15 am–5:45 pm. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026 — subject to change.
The Jaillet Gondola: Based on 2025 prices: adult return €19, child 5–14 €14.50, adult single €11.50, day pass €22.50. Under 5 free. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026.
The Jardins de l'Europe are open continuously, 24 hours a day, year-round. The park is not enclosed and never closes. Verified 10 June 2026.

The Jardins de l'Europe can be visited year-round. Spring offers flowering, summer the shade of the great tree canopy, and autumn (October-November) a display of colours thanks to the Virginia tulip tree and ginkgo biloba.
The LPO regularly organizes maintenance work parties (mowing orchid dunes, removing invasive plants, maintaining edges and ponds) open to volunteers. Contact: haute-savoie[a]lpo.fr.
The Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail is practicable from May to October depending on snow cover, with free access year-round once the road is cleared. In winter, the area is used for snowshoeing.
The Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail starts at the lake car park, above the village of XQV0Z, in the Vallée d'XQV1Z, at about 1,170 m altitude.

The Lac des Plagnes Interpretive Trail tells 20,000 years of geological history of the Vallée d'XQV0Z: great glaciers, slow retreat, peat bog formation, and the story of an intriguing erratic boulder left on site.
