The family loop of the Smugglers' Audio Trail is 4 km with about 200 m of climb, walked in around 2 hrs 30 with listening stops.
The family loop starts at
The famous Pont des Amours, spanning the Vassé canal, starts from the gardens and offers the postcard shot of XQV0Z with a view of the bay.
The farm is home to
The farm is open
The fee is a time-based pass: once inside, you play unlimited on all available machines for the chosen duration (1h, 2h or free evening). No extra tokens. Prices from ~€12-20/person.
The flight is rescheduled if conditions are unsafe (strong wind, rain, storms). The pilot decides the previous evening or the same morning. Flights are usually rescheduled rather than refunded.
The format suits families with primary-school children, school outings and nature clubs. The trail is doable in any season but spring and early summer are richest for wildlife observation.

The fortress originally belonged to the Ternier family, an influential lineage that sometimes rivalled the Counts of Geneva. The castle was then ceded to the Counts and became a comital residence and administrative seat — hence the name "Comtal". Not far away stood another fortification,
The fossil sand dunes, created by wind on the lake shores until the 18th century, constitute a rare geological curiosity. Marked trail through the woods.
The foundry has cast
The full Boucles du Coteau network in XQV0Z totals about 5.5 km and 200 m of positive elevation. The terrain is mostly shaded woodland, with no technical difficulty.

The full collection counts over 600 objects, mostly 17th-18th c.; about fifty are permanently shown: polychrome wood sculptures (saints, Virgins, angels), silverware (chalices, ciboria), and liturgical ornaments. Reduced price; opening hours vary with the season.
The game master follows the game via camera and mic and can provide hints on request or if you're stuck for too long. The aim is for you to fully enjoy the experience, not to remain stuck.
The game master follows the game via camera and mic and can provide hints on request or if you're stuck. The aim is for you to fully enjoy the experience.
The game session in the room lasts 60 minutes, plus around 15 minutes of briefing before and debriefing/souvenir photo after. Plan 1h30 on site.
The games bar (pool, foosball, darts, board games) and the craft-beer selection make it easy to extend the evening on site.
The garden displays more than
The garden evolves across the season (April to October): roses in spring/summer, autumn colours at the end of the season.
The garden has no formal ban, but dogs must be leashed to respect the plants and other visitors.

The garden is divided into two sections: the first includes an alpine meadow (a nod to the mountainous environment), a woodland of shade plants, a Renaissance-inspired weaving pattern, and a hedge cloister. The second section, below, houses the chambers of greenery and the five sensory gardens. Each visitor receives an explanatory map at the entrance, and a discovery quiz is offered to children to learn while having fun. Free guided tours are organized during the season (schedule on the official website). XQV0Z village itself is ranked among France's

The garden is open from April through early October, with annual closure from November through mid-April. Spring (April–May) offers apple tree blossoms, tree peonies, akebia, and cornflowers. Summer (June–August) is peak season: high visitor numbers, all flower beds in full bloom, fragrances of roses and aromatic plants at their peak. Autumn (September–early October) is quieter, with changing colors (crape myrtle, autumn foliage, asters). The visit is pleasant throughout the season, but gardeners recommend mornings to enjoy the dew and fragrances. On weekdays outside school holidays, the garden is less crowded.
The garden is open mid-April to mid-October,

The garden occasionally organizes themed guided tours (gardening techniques, medicinal plants, garden history), a spring plant market, and children's workshops in summer. Program and dates on the official website or by contacting the team. The garden regularly hosts television programs (France 5 "Silence, ça pousse") and photographers for features.
The garden offers close views of the lake, with the estate opening onto the water. An exceptional setting for a contemplative walk.
The gardens extend the Pâquier and the Champ de Mars: a several-kilometre running or walking loop along the lake, flat and accessible.
The gondola serves the Portes du XQV0Z ski area in winter. Meg'Bus shuttle from XQV1Z centre. Ski pass not included.
The greenway leads to the
The guided tour lasts about 1h30 to 2 hours, plus boat rides (~10 min each way). Plan around 2h30 on site.
The guided tour of XQV0Z lasts approximately 40 minutes. The exterior waterfall circuit takes 20 to 30 minutes. With the mill exhibition and time on-site, plan 1.5 to 2 hours for the entire site.
The guided tour of the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm lasts about 1 hr 30, covering the cuzna, the pèle, the cowshed, the hayloft and the outbuildings; bread-oven event included in summer.
The guided tour of the XQV0Z takes approximately 45 minutes. Allow 1–2 hours in total including exploration of the gardens, terraces and vegetable garden, and a break at the gift shop or food truck.
The gym offers
The gym welcomes climbers of all levels, from the curious first-timer to the experienced climber prepping for outdoor crags.
The heart silhouette created by the division of waters is particularly photogenic in spring and after heavy rain, when flow is high and the shape clearly visible.
The high-stakes room of the Casino of Saint-Julien includes 2 blackjack tables, 3 traditional English roulette wheels, 4 Texas Hold'em poker tables and 15 electronic English roulette wheels.
The highlight of the visit is the
The hike from XQV0Z or from the Ajon alpine pasture crosses

The hike to XQV0Z is moderately difficult. The final cliff-side section is exposed, secured by barriers and chains, but the ground can be slippery. Good hiking boots mandatory. Children must be held. Not recommended during rain.

The historic cinema of XQV0Z nestled in the valley, XQV1Z combines arthouse and repertory. Arthouse films, classics and mountain-themed sessions (Mountain Film Festival) sit alongside mainstream releases.

The historical trail of XQV0Z takes 1 hour to walk. A complete visit (monument, trail, exhibition, Maquis Memory building) takes approximately 2 hours. For sports activities (cross-country skiing, snowshoeing, mountain hiking), allow half a day to a full day.

The history of the pass is closely linked to the tourist development of XQV0Z: the opening of the road in 1902 marked the beginning of the resort's expansion. Today, the XQV1Z is a must for mountain lovers, hikers and touring cyclists. The alpine depression where the chapel stands is framed by the cornices of the Etale and the spectacular opening of the Porte des Aravis to the north.
The iconic summit of the Parmelan plateau, a vast limestone balcony at 1,832 m offering panoramic views over the XQV0Z basin, the Aravis and the XQV1Z massif.

The ideal period runs from May to October. In May, witness the transhumance of XQV0Z cattle, a festive event. Summer offers stable weather and verdant alpine pastures. In winter, access is possible on snowshoes for experienced hikers.
The inaugural exhibition
The inner courtyard is the marvel of the XQV0Z:
The instructor takes photos and videos during the outing, sent digitally afterwards. Perfect souvenir to share.
The interpretation trail around the XQV0Z takes about one hour. Geopark panels along the route tell the story of the lake's formation and the submerged hamlets.
The journey is made in

The jump is open to anyone in good health, weighing between ~45 and 110 kg depending on the operator. Excluded: pregnant women, cardiac issues, serious back problems, recent surgery. If unsure, consult your doctor.

The karting is located at the XQV0Z leisure center, by the lake between XQV1Z and Chamonix. Exceptional natural environment to complete your day: swimming, picnic, hiking. 20 min from Chamonix, 15 min from XQV2Z and Saint-Gervais.

The keep is open from mid-June to end of August, an ideal period to enjoy long days and clear views of the surrounding mountains. Outside this summer season, the keep is accessible during the European Heritage Days (September) and can be visited by request for groups through the Association of Friends of Viuz-Faverges. The climb of 145 steps is more pleasant in dry weather; wear suitable shoes and avoid very hot or stormy days. The castle courtyard offers a shaded setting for a rest before or after the climb.

The keep visit is organised by the Association of Friends of Viuz-Faverges and the Heritage Guides of Savoie Mont Blanc, who accompany visitors and tell the history of the castle, its successive owners since 1112, and the site's strategic role through the centuries. The castle itself, private property since 2017, hosts events and accommodation, while the keep and courtyard remain open to the public for heritage visits.

The kennel operates on the Plaine-Joux plateau, in XQV0Z (Pays du XQV1Z), at around 1,300 m altitude. Access by car via the D43 + route des Plagnes from XQV2Z (10-15 min). Parking at the Plaine-Joux station. Snow equipment mandatory in winter.
The killing blow came around

The lake has a supervised swimming zone in summer (July–August, 1–7 pm), with shallow areas cordoned off for young children. Around the lake, several leisure providers offer paid activities: tree-top course, equestrian centre, Big Air Bag, inflatable water structures (Le Splash), pumptrack, trampolines, kayaking, paddleboarding, and beach volleyball. A free children's play area (slides, swings, wooden trail course) is accessible to all.

The lake is a natural mountain lake formed by an 18th-century landslide that dammed the Dranse de XQV0Z valley. Hemmed in between impressive limestone cliffs and a forest of conifers, it is famous for its emerald waters, earning it the nickname

The lake is accessible year-round. High season runs from June to August: the beach is supervised in July–August (12 noon–6 pm), activity providers open daily, parking becomes paid. May–June and September are quieter periods, ideal for walks and water activities without crowds. In autumn and winter, the site becomes a popular place for peaceful strolls with spectacular views of snow-capped XQV0Z. The fitness trail and lake loop remain passable year-round except during heavy snowfall.
The lake is home to alpine newts, dragonflies and numerous plant species. Regeneration zones dedicated to filtration, separate from swimming area.
The lake is the starting point for an

The lake offers exceptional views of the XQV0Z massif and the Fiz range. Accessible year-round, it provides a relaxing setting in summer and winter alike (walks along the snow-covered lake). A 3-star campsite and snack restaurant are on-site. The site also hosts aquabike courses during the summer season.
The lake offers remarkable reflections of surrounding peaks, notably Aiguille de Charlanon. Morning light is particularly beautiful in July-August.
The lake stands apart from conventional swimming facilities through its

The lakefront was redesigned during winter 2025-2026 (municipal order no. 46/2026), with new lawn, redrawn paths and modernised facilities, jointly funded by the village and the Grand XQV0Z authority.
The largest alpine cirque with 500-700m cliffs and 30+ waterfalls in spring. Marked trails, paid parking, free access to XQV0Z.
The largest developed lake in the XQV0Z region, located at 547 m elevation with sandy beach, supervised swimming zone in summer and panoramic views of the XQV1Z massif.
The largest developed lake in the XQV0Z region: sandy beach, supervised swimming in summer, water and land activities in a green setting facing the massif.
The largest free beach on Lake XQV0Z: 400 m of grass and sand, summer lifeguards, free showers and toilets. Albigny district, XQV1Z.
The largest leisure park in XQV0Z country: 30 hectares of nature at the foot of the Contamines Nature Reserve, with mountain lakes, free swimming beach and over 20 activities for the whole family.
The largest leisure park in XQV0Z country: 30 hectares, 2 lakes, free swimming, 20+ sports and relaxation activities. Free access all season in XQV1Z.
The largest public beach on Lake XQV0Z, featuring 400 meters of grassy shoreline facing the mountains. Lifeguard-supervised in summer, very popular, full amenities.
The last 200 m to the summit include
The legendary Grands Montets ski area (3,275 m) above Argentière is being completely rebuilt after the 2018 fire. The new Renzo Piano 3S cable car is scheduled for December 2026.

The leisure base is accessible year-round. Summer (May to September) is peak season: supervised swimming in July–August, open-air pool open, all activities available, animated terraces. Spring and autumn are quieter, ideal for walks, fishing and land-based activities. In winter, the site is regularly snow-covered and the lakes frozen: the base becomes a starting point for snowshoe walks and cross-country skiing along the Giffre. Playgrounds and sports fields remain accessible when snow-free. Espace Le Bois aux Dames hosts cultural events year-round.
The limestone massif offers many equipped climbing routes from beginner to advanced. Topos available from guide offices.
The local club and ESF offer lessons. Ask directly at the rink.
The location is convenient for an evening in XQV0Z:

The loop around the Lac des Plagnes is easy and flat (30-45 min). The option that climbs to the Cubourré plateau and the erratic boulder is more demanding (2 to 3 hrs round trip) with elevation through the forest alongside the waterfall.
The loop's main feature is that it is
The main
The main XQV0Z measures 60 meters in height. There are actually two waterfalls at the site: a smaller one the trail passes beneath, and the main 60 m cascade at the canyon's end.
The main gondola of the XQV0Z Bike Park: 5-minute ride from the village to the Chavannes plateau (1,482 m), gateway to the Portes du Soleil ski and MTB network.
The main loop at XQV0Z is 4.5 km along the Dranse. Shorter loops (1 to 2 km) are possible by taking intermediate bridges.

The main season is July and August (daily 10:00–18:00). In June, the park opens weekends and public holidays only. Prefer mornings in July–August to avoid crowds. Beunaz Beach is quieter in June and early July. In very hot weather, combine the treetop course in the morning and swimming in the afternoon. Check the weather before visiting: strong winds or storms may cause the activity to close for safety reasons.

The main season runs December to March, when snow cover on Plaine-Joux allows sledding under the best conditions. Peak season (Christmas and February holidays) is in very high demand — book very early. Outside the snow season (April to November), cani-hiking and wheeled formats take over on the plateau's forest paths. Weather and snow may force a reschedule: keep some flexibility in your trip.

The main season runs December to March-April depending on Portes du Soleil snow cover. Christmas and February holidays are in very high demand — book very early. Shoulder periods (early December, late March) often offer more flexibility and quieter conditions. Off-season, cani-hiking and wheeled formats remain possible, on request. Weather, snow or thaws may force a reschedule: allow some flexibility in your trip.
The marsh hosts numerous waterfowl species: Eurasian reed warblers, grey herons, black kites. A purpose-built observatory allows discreet observation, especially in spring and at dawn.

The marsh offers beautiful photographic opportunities: golden reedbeds in autumn, dragonflies in flight, morning mists and water reflections. Light is particularly beautiful early morning and late afternoon.

The maze is composed of hedgerows and espalier apple trees that define chambers of greenery. Each thematic space showcases a collection of plants chosen for their sensory, medicinal, or edible properties: 25 varieties of mint, 25 of sage, 50 of fragrant pelargoniums, alpine plants, heirloom vegetables, espalier fruit trees, and old roses. Over 1,500 plant varieties are cultivated by a passionate team of gardeners led since 2009 by Matthieu Constans, head gardener. The garden welcomes approximately 40,000 visitors per year.
The minimum age is generally 10, provided you're accompanied by an adult depending on the room. Each room shows its difficulty level and minimum recommended age on the site.
The minimum age is generally 10, with an adult depending on the room. Each room shows its difficulty level and minimum recommended age on the site.
The minimum height for the main course at XQV0Z is 1.35 m. The black loops are optional and reserved for experienced participants.

The most common starting point for XQV0Z is Petit Port in XQV1Z-le-Vieux, accessible by car (10 min from the centre) or by bus line 20. The trail starts on Avenue de Chavoire, just after the bakery, at the Bois Jettaz sign.
The most iconic access is the
The multi-activity park includes several options adapted to all heights and levels:
The municipal beach adjoins the park: direct access on foot to the sunbathing lawns, the solarium and supervised swimming in season.
The municipal museum
The museum also features a
The museum also offers two
The museum has heavily modernised its offer in recent years with a strong family focus:

The museum is aimed at families with children from age 6-7 (curious about history and natural sciences), school groups (by booking), local archaeology enthusiasts and visitors staying in XQV0Z or around the lake. The visit is relatively short (45 min-1h30 depending on interest), ideal alongside a lakeside walk, a meal in a XQV1Z restaurant or a visit to the XQV2Z château and the Maison des oiseaux.
The museum is housed in XQV0Z's former tobacco manufactory, whose industrial history (tobacco, Tefal) is widely covered in the collections.
The museum is in XQV0Z (74140), on the shore of Lake Geneva, 15 minutes from XQV1Z and 40 minutes from Geneva. Exact address to confirm on ville-sciez.fr.
The museum is open
The museum is suited to families with children from age 6-7 (curious about history and natural sciences). For younger kids, the visit can be shorter.

The museum is the life's work of Philippe Piccot, who ran the Cinéma du Chablais in XQV0Z and saved and restored every Buisse-Bottazzi model: projection-cabin machines, lab equipment and accessories. Open every Tuesday in July-August, 2:30-5:30 pm; free guided tours.
The museum sits beneath the striking timber frame of an 1842 farmhouse, an architectural treasure of the Bornes Plateau. A sight in its own right.

The musher occasionally offers discovery evenings, moonlit rides in peak season and birthday packages for children. Watching local sled-dog races (Grande Odyssée Savoie XQV0Z, in the area) is sometimes proposed. Programme and dates posted on the official website and the kennel's social media.
The musher often photographs the ride; souvenirs are shared at the end (depending on package). Capture the moment.
The name 'Bellegarde' comes from Janus Bellegarde, syndic and secretary of the syndic of Thonon in the 16th century, who lived in the tower. The château today houses
The network is organised in three concentric loops marked with the classic hiking colour code:
The new
The new Nid d'Aigle platform and Col de Voza provide access to mountain bike trails. Ride the train up and bike down toward XQV0Z (separate ski lift pass required).
The offer is structured in two formats:
The offering centres on
The offering covers all
The offering varies with season and age. In winter, when snow is sufficient, the musher offers

The office (contact point) is in central XQV0Z. From Geneva (~45 km): A41 exit XQV1Z-Nord — 40 min. Outings usually start at meeting points in the massifs (Bauges, Aravis, XQV2Z) — carpool or personal vehicle depending on outing.
The office is also a valuable information hub

The on-site restaurant is a traditional brasserie, well suited to family evenings combining a meal with a few games. A bar and lounge area completes the offer, providing a space to wait between games or to round off the evening.
The only large natural sand beach on Lake XQV0Z, equipped with shaded lawns, diving board and accessible facilities. Blue Flag certified since 2010.
The only natural fine sand beach on the French shore of Lake Geneva, with a gentle slope extending several tens of metres and shallow waters reaching 25°C in summer.
The only place with aquariums in XQV0Z, at the Port de Rives. 2 Lake Geneva aquariums, fishermen's huts. Adult €3.50, free under 18 and every Sunday.
The only underground gallery open to the public in XQV0Z. A 40-minute guided tour 10° below ground and a spectacular 40-meter-high waterfall.
The operator may occasionally take part in regional balloon gatherings (Chablais local festivals). Private flights for birthdays, proposals, hen/stag. Gift vouchers year-round.
The operator supplies full equipment:
The origin of the name

The original cable cars (Lognan I and II) were destroyed by fire in August 2018. A new 3S cable car designed by Renzo Piano has been under construction since 2024, linking Argentière to the 3,275 m summit. The Plan Joran gondola provides interim winter access.
The outdoor
The outdoor park welcomes kids from around age 5-6 (depending on the workshop), bouldering from age 4 with family. Classes from age 6-7.

The park also hosts facilities managed by operators: the Centre Nautique de l'Espace Bernard Pellarin (heated open-air pool open May to late August), an adventure tree-climbing course (Tactiq Aventure), tennis courts and a fishing lake (permit required). The animal enclosure allows free observation of deer, stags, does and mouflons, making the park a popular family destination in the Genevois region.
The park celebrates its
The park connects with the redesigned town hall square — a landscape programme of 2010-2013 (ADP Dubois) that structured XQV0Z's civic heart.
The park distinguishes itself through its

The park features several emblematic species of local Alpine fauna: red deer, hinds, roe deer, wild boar, chamois, marmots (depending on season), ibex and other Alpine forest animals. Visibility varies by time of day and season.
The park has
The park includes a
The park includes the

The park is aimed at families with children, walkers and hikers, schools and holiday centres. It's an ideal visit to discover Alpine fauna in a few hours, in a natural and soothing setting. Educational panels explain the species, their way of life and their role in the ecosystem. The walk around the enclosures is easy (forest tracks) and suitable for all-terrain pushchairs or young children.
The park is best enjoyed April to October, with marked seasonal highlights. The
The park is divided into three zones: the
The park is divided into two worlds: a
The park is in the Semnoz forest, above XQV0Z (74000), accessible by the Semnoz road (D241) 10-15 minutes from central XQV1Z by car. Parking nearby.

The park is open April to October, but the best period to enjoy activities fully is June to September, when swimming is open and all providers are active. In July–August, heavy weekend crowds — prefer mornings or weekdays. In June and September, quieter weather, ideal to enjoy nature without crowds. The park closes in winter when the resort focuses on alpine skiing.
The park is open from
The park is open roughly May to October,

The park is open year-round with hours varying by season. High season (April to August) offers the widest time slots with evening openings until 7:30 pm on weekends. In winter, the park operates Wednesdays and weekends with daily opening during school holidays. Spring and autumn are ideal periods to enjoy the forest with less crowding. Allow 1 to 2h30 depending on the course chosen. Avoid heavy rain or strong wind days. Online booking required, possible until the last minute.
The park is operated by
The park is split into two zones: a

The park is the official local recommendation under the 'Indian Forest XQV0Z-Les Aravis XQV1Z' label. The Indian Forest network has no other site in the XQV2Z department, so this XQV3Z park is the reference for the area, in a mid-mountain setting overlooking the Aravis.
The park occasionally hosts community sports events (running races, family activities) organised by XQV0Z Town Hall. Check the Tourist Office calendar for dates.
The park offers fine lawns overlooking XQV0Z: a family picnic with views of the Pointe de Marcelly and the Aravis range.

The park offers four types of courses suited to all sizes and ages: the Grand Course (from 1m35, approximately 2h30) with Tarzan swing and return zip-lines of 200 m; the Junior Course (from 1m20, approximately 2h) with 5 loops including a zip-line course; the Children's Course (from 1 m, approximately 1h) with 4 loops; and the Acro'Net (all ages, 1 to 2h) with giant balls, ball pit, trampolines and slide. All courses are equipped with a continuous safety line system developed by Koala Equipment and designed by Altus, guaranteeing maximum safety while allowing autonomous progression once the briefing is completed.

The park offers two marked circuits: an easy route (approximately 1 hour) suitable for all-terrain strollers, and a "fitness" route to explore every corner. New for 2026: an immersive sensory route, "The Secret Atmospheres of Merlet" (5 minutes). Free activities are offered in July–August (Antlers and Horns, The Mountain and Its Secrets, The Predators), as well as exhibition rooms and video screenings. The park is committed to an eco-responsible approach: local electricity production (solar panels and micro-hydroelectric power station), selective sorting and zero waste.

The park provides harness, absorber lanyard, helmet and Smart Belay carabiners. Bring: flexible clothing, long sleeves, long trousers (harness friction), closed-toe shoes/trainers, hair tie for long hair.
The park sits on the site of the former château of Count Joachim de la Grange (18th c.). Interpretive panels cover the seigneurial history of the XQV0Z.

The park welcomes children from around 4-6 years depending on the zone, with a dedicated little-jumpers area. Teens and adults enjoy free-jump, dodge ball and trampoline basket. Check the minimum age on just-jump.fr.

The park welcomes children from around age 3 (dedicated toddlers' area) through to adults, as families or with friends. Sessions typically last 1 hour, on fixed slots, with non-slip socks compulsory (sold on site). Children's birthday parties are a specialty: all-inclusive package with private room, cake and drinks. The park also offers group packages, stag/hen, business seminars and school groups outside school hours. Systematic briefing by the Jump Coaches.

The park welcomes families, groups of friends, schools and businesses. Access is from age 5 or so, with dedicated slots for the youngest (pre-school) in the morning on some days. Birthdays are a major part of the offer: 1 hour of jumping + private room for cake and drinks subject to availability. The park also hosts stag/hen parties, business seminars (team-building with dodgeball or ninja) and themed sessions (neon nights, music).
The park's main attraction is

The pass has been climbed six times by the Tour de France. Two routes to choose from: via XQV0Z (13 km, 6.5% average, sections at 10-11%) or via XQV1Z (18 km, 4.6% average, more regular). Splendid views of the Aravis range.

The pass is crossed by the D909 departmental road, opened in 1902, today a stage of the Route des Grandes Alpes® linking XQV0Z to Nice. On site: the Sainte-Anne chapel, restaurants, souvenir shops, alpine farms selling Reblochon cheese directly, parking areas, and hiking trailheads. On a clear day, open view of the XQV1Z massif. The pass also welcomes cyclists and bikers who tackle this legendary ascent.

The path along the Dranse at XQV0Z is walkable from around 1 May to 19 November, outside the snow season. In winter the upper section is snowy and used for cross-country skiing or snowshoeing depending on conditions.

The path along the Dranse at XQV0Z starts at the free car park in the Très-les-Pierres hamlet. At the finish, there is a car park in the centre of XQV1Z. Bal'Aulps valley shuttle bus available in season.
The path along the Dranse is around 16 km linear between the Très-les-Pierres hamlet at XQV0Z and the village of XQV1Z. Up to 20 km depending on the variant. Allow 4 hrs on foot, 1 hr 30 by bike.
The path along the banks of the Arve from XQV0Z to Arthaz can be walked year-round. Prefer March to November (outside floods). In summer, the shade of the woods and the river's coolness are welcome.
The path along the banks of the Arve runs through rich riparian forest: grey heron, dipper, kingfisher, coots, sometimes beavers. Pioneer plants and species typical of riverbanks.
The path is officially designated a mountain-bike route by Savoie Mont Blanc Tourisme. Rideable on hybrid and mountain bikes, as an out-and-back or 14.5 km loop.
The path passes near the Jardin des Iris in XQV0Z, a haven of perennials with spring flowering (April-May). Free access.
The path runs through the XQV0Z valley, home of the AOP XQV1Z cheese. Dairy farms and the Maison du Fromage can be visited.
The path stays wide and gently sloped, suiting the whole family;
The permanent collection, built up since 1958, gathers contemporary paintings and sculptures where the
The permanent exhibition

The plateau offers a preserved natural setting, both wild and welcoming, where forest meets pastureland and where deer, hinds and mouflons are regularly spotted in the Grande Jeanne park. XQV0Z regularly features in regional sporting competitions, including the XQV1Z Maxi Race, and welcomes thousands of walkers, athletes and families each year seeking fresh air and exceptional panoramas.

The platform offers one of the most spectacular panoramas in the XQV0Z region: a sweeping view 800 m above the lake, a 180° panorama from the Bauges massif in the west to Salève and the Jura in the north, with Mont Blanc, the Aravis and XQV1Znette visible to the east. The viewpoint sits on the ridgeline between Mont Baron (1,299 m, summit 10 minutes above the platform) and Mont Veyrier (1,291 m, 30 minutes further north via the ridge path). Together they form a continuous balcony overlooking Lake XQV2Z, highly prized by hikers, photographers and bivouac campers. An orientation table is installed on the platform. The site is also renowned for its spectacular sunsets over the lake.

The playground spans one hectare of forest and features around twenty fun workshops: tree courses, zip-lines, net maze, giant trampoline, tree cabins, net sea, serpent swing, and a new aerial course over 1.20 m high (reserved for older children). A Mini-Kids area welcomes 2–3 year-olds with age-appropriate games. An organic eco-snack (crêpes, savoury galettes, ice cream, local drinks) completes the offer on site.

The price for XQV0Z varies by course: Grand Course adult (16+) €26, youth (under 16) €21, Junior Course €20, Children's Course €16, Acro'Net €16, Orienteering course €6. Group rate from 8 people. Verified May 14, 2026.
The price is around €25-35 per person, with a discount from 4 players. Group packages and offers for stag/hen or business are available. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
The price is around €25-35 per person, with a discount from 4 players. Group packages and offers for stag/hen or business available. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
The price of the Notre Histoire museum in XQV0Z is €3.70 full price, €3 reduced. FREE for everyone on Wednesdays and free all year for under-22s and students. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The price of the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in XQV0Z is not published on the official sources consulted. Contact Philippe Piccot directly on +33 (0)4 50 94 11 79 for the current price.
The price of the Sacred Art Museum of XQV0Z is not published on official sources. Contact the Tourist Office on +33 (0)4 50 47 76 08 for the current price.
The price of the Savoyard Pottery Museum at XQV0Z is not published on the official sources. Please call +33 (0)4 50 62 01 90.
The programme at XQV0Z is refreshed every week, generally on Wednesday. Check AlloCiné or the local press for the weekly schedule.
The programme at XQV0Z is refreshed every week, generally on Wednesday. Check the official site (XQV1Z or AlloCiné for the weekly schedule.
The programme changes every week — check the official site or AlloCiné for current films and showtimes.
The programme features
The pèle of the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm is the living room heated by the stove — the heart of the traditional Savoyard house, where the family would gather.
The quieter north-facing side of XQV0Z: 5-minute gondola to the Mont-Chéry summit (1,535 m), 360° views of Mont Blanc and Lac XQV1Z, hiking and MTB with a wilder feel.
The recent renovation added
The remains of the Château de XQV0Z are visible 24/7, free access year-round. Daytime is preferable to read the arrow slits and the curtain wall properly. Checked on 10 June 2026.

The remains of the Château de XQV0Z can be visited year-round, but spring and summer remain the most pleasant seasons — the village of XQV1Z (820 m altitude) can be covered in snow in winter. Autumn offers beautiful light on the medieval stones.

The restaurant and the panoramic terrace are a strong point of the venue, with classic brasserie cuisine and a particularly enjoyable view of the XQV0Z range at sunset. The bowling lanes, pool tables and arcade games make the complex a one-stop evening venue, combining game, drink and meal under one roof.
The rink is a key sporting facility of the city, hosting individual sessions, school groups and clubs throughout the year. The Friday

The rink is aimed at families with children from age 4-5, groups of friends, school groups and sports clubs. Beginners can take a lesson with an instructor (ice school or private), while experienced skaters enjoy free skating or join the local hockey club training. Disco-on-ice and themed nights run during winter school holidays, and some evening sessions are open in high season.

The rink welcomes the XQV0Z Hockey Club for home games, as well as figure skating galas and regional championships, with a packed events calendar from December to March. Public sessions alternate with sports activities, making the rink a key family destination of the resort.

The road is closed in winter, usually from mid-November to early May depending on snow cover. In 2026 the col reopened on 6 May at 2pm. Access is free year-round during the open season, and a free shuttle bus runs to the col from XQV0Z from 11 July to 21 August.
The road to the pass winds through forest for most of the route, then opens to panoramic views in the final kilometres:
The rooms at XQV0Z generally host 2 to 6 players per room, with an ideal team of 4 to move easily in the décor. For larger groups, several rooms can be booked in parallel.
The rooms cover varied worlds:
The rooms generally host 2 to 6 players, with an ideal team of 4. For larger groups, several rooms can be booked in parallel.
The route
The route accepts dogs on leads, with no risky livestock over most of the trail.
The route alternates wooded natural stretches, urban sections (XQV0Z, the Jardin des Iris, the Grand Casino) and farmland. Wide views of

The route finishes along a rocky ridge, a fun and aesthetic section, before descending via a hiking trail.

The route is waymarked in yellow (PDIPR); a map is available at the tourist information office. Dogs must be kept on a lead because of the grazing herds.
The route leaves the village centre and crosses the
The route lends itself well to snowshoeing in winter: gentle gradient, waymarking maintained (depending on snow cover).

The route mostly follows the hillsides above the lake (300–900 m altitude), offering panoramic balcony views over
The route offers three emergency exits allowing you to adapt the itinerary to your level and fitness on the day.

The route via XQV0Z (18 km at 4.6%) is more accessible for intermediate cyclists. The route via XQV1Z is significantly harder (sections at 10% for 3 km after the village), reserved for experienced cyclists.
The route was developed in two phases: the
The routes are passable on snowshoes in winter depending on weather conditions and snow cover (information at the Foyer des Moises).
The ruins of the Château de XQV0Z are open 24/7 year-round. Guided tours by the Heritage Guides are by reservation. Checked on 10 June 2026.
The schedule at XQV0Z changes every week, usually on Wednesday. Check the official site (XQV1Z or AlloCiné for the weekly schedule.

The school offers a wide range of activities:
The season runs April to October,

The season runs April to September depending on Dranse flow. Booking is mandatory, online or by phone, with weather confirmation the day before. Prices range from around €45 for classic descents to €60-65 for sporty formats (2026 prices to confirm). XQV0Z supplies all technical equipment: wetsuit, neoprene socks, helmet, lifejacket, paddle/fins. Bring swimwear, towel and worn-out trainers or river shoes. Swimming is mandatory.

The season runs from April to October. Conditions vary by weather: calm flights in the morning (ideal first time), thermals in late afternoon (sensations). In bad weather flights are postponed or cancelled.
The season runs from April to October. May-June offer iris and water-lily bloom, September-October the beautiful autumn light. Avoid peak summer for a more peaceful visit.

The season runs from April to late October, with peak from June to September. Spring (May-June): dry rock after rains, cool but pleasant temperatures, moderate attendance. Summer (July-August): very early starts recommended (from 8 am) as the wall faces full south — it gets very hot from 11 am. September-October: optimal conditions (mild temperatures, reduced attendance, golden light on XQV0Z). Always avoid storm days and after heavy rain (slippery limestone).

The season runs from June to late September depending on water levels and weather. Prices range from around €55-80 for a half-day to €85-110 for a full day (2026 indicative prices, to confirm on the official site). Booking is via escape-canyon.com or by phone, ideally several days ahead in peak season. The meeting point is usually in XQV0Z or near the chosen canyon, communicated when you book. Plan extra driving time to the canyon itself.

The season runs from late March to mid-November, with peak attendance July-August (daily opening). Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) offer a calmer experience with changing forest colours. Wednesdays, weekends and school holidays are preferable outside July-August. The park is forest-based: wear appropriate clothing (covering clothes, closed shoes). Avoid shorts and short sleeves (harness friction). No running water on site: bring your own water bottle.

The season runs roughly from April to October, with optimal conditions in the morning (calm flights, ideal for first-timers) or late afternoon (thermals for those who love sensations). Booking is essential. Plan 2-3h on site (meeting at take-off, briefing, flight, return transport). Conditions vary, so your slot may be moved or rescheduled; keep some flexibility in your programme.

The season usually runs from June to late September depending on river flow. Prices are around €55-80 for a half-day and €85-110 for a full day (2026 indicative prices, to confirm on the official site). Booking is via neo-canyon.com or by phone; very strongly recommended several days ahead in July and August. The meeting point is usually in XQV0Z or close to the chosen canyon, communicated when you book.

The self-guided farm visit is free, no booking required, year-round Monday to Saturday. Workshops, donkey walks, escape game and stays are paid — rates on lafermedupetitmont.fr.
The sensation is powerful: heights reach up to
The setting is the garden's signature feature: an uninterrupted view of the

The seven trails are graded by level: a children's trail (from age 3), then beginner, intermediate, sporty and expert routes. A safety briefing at the start is compulsory. Spectacular ziplines stretch up to 400 m above the thermal park, with sweeping views of the XQV0Z range, a unique setting for treetop adventures.
The single price for the Museum of Regional Art and Folklore at XQV0Z is €5. Free for children under 6. Verified 10 June 2026.
The single price for the Nature Museum at XQV0Z is €6 per person, free for under-2s. Groups €12/person with activity. Family: 3rd person onwards €10. Verified 10 June 2026.
The single rate at the Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z is €4 per person. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The site also includes a

The site also offers an orienteering course (from age 7, search for 20 markers in the forest) and a treasure hunt (from age 3) for the youngest. XQV0Z welcomes families, school groups, corporate committees, birthday parties, bachelor and bachelorette parties with adapted packages. Sportswear and closed shoes covering heel and toes are mandatory for safety reasons. Online booking is required on the official website, with the possibility to book until the last minute. The park stands out for its excellent value for money and professional on-site supervision.
The site can be privatised for weddings, seminars or receptions, in a unique setting in XQV0Z.

The site can only be visited on a guided tour during the season (usually April to October), reached from Évian harbour by a small shuttle boat that hugs the shore — a very enjoyable approach that is part of the experience. The tour, led by a naturalist guide, lasts about 1h30 to 2 hours and alternates botanical, ecological and historical commentary on the villa and the gardens. Children are welcome: the tone is accessible and the paths suit child-height discovery. Daily capacity is limited, so booking is strongly recommended in July-August.
The site comprises the remains of two castle enclosures, an
The site features
The site features a fine sandy beach with supervised swimming in July–August (depth zones
The site has earth paths and requires boarding a boat. Wheelchair access is partial: we recommend contacting the Évian Tourist Office to prepare your visit.
The site has held the

The site hosts cultural events throughout the year: medieval historical reenactments, live performances, temporary exhibitions (art, photography, heritage). European Heritage Days in September with free activities and guided tours. The site is part of the Chablais geological route inaugurated during Heritage Days.
The site hosts throughout the year historical reenactments, live performances and temporary exhibitions organized by the municipality and local heritage associations.
The site is a
The site is a major memorial place of the XQV0Z Resistance, linked to the Saxel maquis and the Vallée Verte STO refusers.

The site is accessible (disabled parking, adapted toilets). The Alpine Coaster requires being able to get on/off the sled and operate the hand brake — assistance possible but a minimum level of mobility is required. Call +33 4 50 53 08 97 to discuss your situation.

The site is accessible and flyable year-round. In summer (May to October), flight conditions are optimal: strong thermals in afternoon, high numbers of pilots and spectators, pleasant plateau temperature. In winter (December to April), flights also take place, but the ski station is active and the site quieter. Spring and autumn offer stable conditions with fewer crowds. Best flight hours: afternoon (thermals), inadvisable in morning and in strong westerly winds. The site is heavily visited on weekends from June to September: arrive early or visit on weekdays to avoid crowding at takeoff.
The site is accessible by marked paths from the Ternier hamlet. Walls and earthworks visible; free access.

The site is accessible year-round, but the best period extends from April to October to fully enjoy the panorama and favourable weather conditions. In summer (July-August), the site benefits from ideal light in the late afternoon to admire Lake XQV0Z and the Alps. Possibility of intense heat with no shade on the plateau. Winter and spring offer quieter visits and clear views on fine days, but beware of ice on trails and ruins in winter. The site organizes regular cultural events: medieval reenactments, photo exhibitions, live shows — check the XQV1Z municipality agenda or the Chablais Tourist Office.

The site is accessible year-round, but the experience varies by season. Spring (April–May) offers the most powerful flow thanks to snowmelt, with a very impressive waterfall but glacial water. Summer (June–September) is the ideal period for guided canyoning and swimming in the downstream pools, though the water remains very cold. Autumn (October) offers colorful forest scenery and fewer visitors. In winter (December–March), the waterfall may freeze partially or completely, creating spectacular scenery appreciated by snowshoe hikers and ice climbing enthusiasts. Winter access is practicable but requires appropriate equipment (crampons recommended in freezing conditions). The on-site restaurant closes in low season — check before visiting.

The site is bordered by restaurants, brasseries and water sports schools (sailing, diving, boat rentals). A kiosk and automatic restrooms are on-site. Winter saunas on the waterfront complete the leisure offerings year-round.
The site is freely accessible on foot via a trail starting between XQV0Z station and the Fontaine car park; a
The site is frequented by canyoning groups in summer. Supervised activity only, specialist equipment required. Do not venture alone on descent areas.

The site is highly popular with the general public who come to admire the ballet of colorful sails against the exceptional panorama. Flights are practiced in thermals in the afternoon, with landing at Chedde or Marlioz in the Arve valley, 760 meters below. The takeoff zone is equipped with an FFVL (French Free Flight Federation) information panel, a wind sleeve, and a webcam viewable online.
The site is labelled Jardin remarquable by the French Ministry of Culture: official recognition of the landscape, botanical and educational quality.

The site is now laid out as an interpretive trail signposted from the Avoriaz tourist office, passing between the Malinka and Saskia buildings down to the old works. Orientation table; free year-round access.
The site is one of 23 geosites in the Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark. Interpretation panels on the geological history of the hilltop, from the molasse sea to alpine glaciers.

The site is open April to September only. High season (July-August) offers the longest hours (9am–6pm) and more visitors, particularly for zip-lines. April, May, June, and September offer reduced hours (10am–5pm) but fewer visitors, ideal for a quieter cave visit. Underground temperature remains constant at 10°C year-round — bring a sweater or fleece even in mid-summer. The waterfall is most impressive in spring (April-May) when snowmelt generously feeds the Saint-Ruph torrent. In September, flow is lower but the setting remains magnificent. On rainy days, the waterfall gains power and the natural site takes on a mysteriously atmospheric and particularly photogenic quality.

The site is protected: the shorelines have been classified to preserve their natural character and historical connection to the House of Savoy. Swimming is free and unsupervised. The beaches are connected by maintained trails that run along the shoreline. One beach, marked with signs, is reserved for naturist practice.

The site is rich in history: a marshland island in the Middle Ages, the "Clos Lombard" was given to the Visitation order in 1644 by a Lombard banking family, became national property at the Revolution, then bought back by the town between 1835 and 1843. Free 24/7.
The site is visited only on a guided tour (~1h30-2h) led by a specialist guide. Botanical, ecological and historical commentary accessible to all.

The site occupies the location of a mountain hamlet inhabited year-round until the 1900s, before becoming a seasonal pasture. The park was created in 1967–1968 by Philippe Cachat, a Chamonix native passionate about mountains and an airplane pilot, with Bambi, a tamed chamois who became the mascot. Today managed by the Cachat family (6 employees in season), the park combines observation of alpine wildlife, an exceptional panorama of the XQV0Z massif and discovery of mountain heritage through its old alpine chalets. An altiport (Altiport Philippe-Cachat) occupies the park's center.

The site of the ruins of the Château de XQV0Z is freely accessible. For a guided tour, book with the Guides du Patrimoine Savoie Mont Blanc, who offer tours of the village and ruins year-round by appointment.
The site offers
The site offers a rare combination of facilities: diving boards at

The site offers a wide range of activities: supervised swimming in the lake from early July to early September (12pm–6pm), a children's playground, sports pitches (football, basketball, beach volley, pétanque), fishing, lakeside walks, stand-up paddle and a discovery trail. Paying facilities are also available: an open-air swimming pool with slides and three basins, tennis and padel courts, an adventure course and paragliding.
The site operates on two levels: it's a

The site presents remarkable geological interest with its famous S-shaped rock fold, characteristic of Jurassic limestone series of the subalpine nappes. Classified as a natural site since 1991, it offers complete amenities: free parking, picnic areas, and starting points for hiking and mountain biking trails.

The site regularly hosts summer events: children's shows, open-air concerts, trout-fishing contests. La Covagne bar-restaurant on the lake shore. Public toilets, benches, and picnic tables. Mountain-bike wash area. Located in the Grand Massif domain, Lac Bleu is a landing point for paragliders. XQV0Z is served by the intercommunal Haut-Giffre Tourisme office.

The site stands out for its varied facilities: more than 1.2 km of zip-lines spread across all courses (including a 40 m zip-line dedicated to 3-6 year-olds), an airbag for free-fall jumps of 3 to 7 meters onto a giant cushion, a unique human catapult in XQV0Z propelling participants over 12 meters, and two outdoor escape games around the lake combining riddles, observation and teamwork. The park welcomes families, school groups, birthday parties and corporate events in a wooded environment on the lake shore.
The site was redeveloped in

The site's main draw is the outdoor balneoform pool (open June-September) — the main reason to come in summer. The Spa is the winter option par excellence. Daily access from €25-32 depending on the offer.
The site's second sensory trail, walked barefoot to experience different materials (straw, water, bark, wood chips).

The ski area is accessible in winter (6 Dec 2025–3 May 2026) via the Plan Joran gondola (skiers only). It is closed from 4 May to 30 November 2026 for the new 3S cable car works. Full reopening is planned for December 2026.
The slot floor has
The spa is adults-only; depending on the slot, minimum age is 14 or 16. It is not suitable for families with young children. Details on the official website.
The spa is designed around the view: large windows and panoramic terraces facing the Aiguilles and the glacier.
The spirit is firmly family-oriented:
The standout feature is that the château is
The start is in the centre of the village: you can combine it with a stroll through XQV0Z, its ice rink, shops and tourist office.

The summer park is open April to end of August, with a gradual increase in services: April and May with reduced hours (weekends and public holidays only in May), then peak season from early June to end of August with extended hours and all shows. The best months to visit are
The summit offers an exceptional circular view of Lake Geneva, the peaks of the Chablais (Cornettes de Bise, Dent d'Oche, Roc d'Enfer), the Mont Blanc massif in clear weather, and the Swiss Jura.
The summit offers one of the widest views over the XQV0Z basin — ideal late in the day for golden light on the lake and Bauges massif.
The temporary exhibition
The threat was taken seriously by
The three escape-game scenarios cover different themes — scientific exploration, fantasy, mystery — and are designed for teams of
The tone of the tour is suitable for children from age ~6-7. A great way to introduce children to nature and wetland conservation.

The torrent feeding the cascade originates at Pointe d'Areu (2,468 m), a bastion of the Bornes massif. After a 5.5-kilometer course, it breaks through Jurassic limestone cliffs to form the XQV0Z before reaching the plain. Downstream, the torrent bed tumbles down the slope over 200 meters, scattered with large rocky blocks, creating a succession of small secondary waterfalls difficult to spot. The torrent water is captured upstream of the cascade and treated at a microfilter station to supply part of the municipality of XQV1Z; this installation is underground and has not degraded the classified site.
The traditional gaming room at XQV0Z Grand Casino offers English Roulette tables, open in the evening.
The trail climbs via the Tête des Annes and the Col de l'Oulettaz to the
The trail crosses the main communes of the Pays du XQV0Z:

The trail first leads to the most spectacular part of the waterfall, then descends steeply towards the Chinaillon torrent where you discover a wild gorge. The terrain can be muddy and slippery — good hiking boots are essential. This easy and refreshing walk, ideal for families, also allows you to discover old Chinaillon with its typical alpine chalets and panoramic views over the Aravis range.
The trail follows the Chéran river, an emblematic spot for fly-fishers, and offers a green setting right in the heart of the village.

The trail is accessible from April to October without particular difficulty. Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) offer the best conditions: the waterfall has strong flow after rainfall, and the potholes are clearly visible. In summer, the shaded trail remains pleasant even in hot weather, but water flow may be lower during dry periods. In winter, access can be difficult depending on snowfall. Avoid periods of heavy rain: the trail becomes very slippery and stream crossings can be risky.
The trail is one of the few in XQV0Z to take in

The trail is passable from April to November, except in snowy conditions. In spring (April–May), after rain or snowmelt, the waterfall reveals itself in all its power with spectacular flow. In summer, prefer a visit early morning or late afternoon to enjoy the peace and coolness in the shade of the beech forest, especially during peak season at the resort. In autumn (October), the flamboyant colours of the forest add different charm to the walk. Avoid snowy periods (December to March) when the trail may be impassable. Check with the Tourist Office before departure.

The trail is passable from April to October. Summer (June–September) is the busiest period, with peak crowds on sunny weekends — the viewpoint is one of the most popular in the XQV0Z basin. Spring (April–May) and autumn (September–October) offer lower visitor numbers and soft photographic light. In winter, the terrain becomes slippery and icy; weathered rocks can be dangerous in damp conditions. Sunsets in late afternoon (17:00–20:00 depending on season) attract many bivouac campers. The view is clear year-round, but fog may obscure the lake during winter thermal inversions.
The trail leads to the
The trail leads to the pink marble quarry of XQV0Z, a relic of local quarrying that tells the geological history of the Chablais.
The trail lies within the Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark and offers an introduction to the geology of the Northern Alps.
The trail runs alongside a mountain river; keep an eye on children but enjoy the sound of the water.

The trail runs through the commune of XQV0Z (74350), in the Monts du Genevois, halfway between XQV1Z and XQV2Z. It is short and flat, accessible to children and occasional walkers alike, in shaded woodland ideal in summer. At the heart of the trail, a stop is offered by the
The trail starts at the Virage du Tenne, at the end of the Route du Petit-XQV0Z (2 km from the village). It crosses a

The trail to XQV0Z is rated difficult (400 m elevation gain from Planpraz, scree passages, mountain trail). It is suitable for children experienced in hiking (from approximately 8-10 years old) and in good physical condition. Not accessible with a stroller.

The treetop course at XQV0Z is accessible from age 2 with the Little Kid course (up to 105 cm), under adult supervision. From 105 cm, children access the Kid courses independently. From 135 cm, access to Indiana Park courses (blue, red, black).

The usual starting point of the Heritage Trail is at the Charly chapel (Saint-Jacques chapel, 15th century), in the heart of the village of Charly in XQV0Z. You can also join it from Jussy or Saint-Symphorien.
The valley with its meadows offers an ideal setting for a packed lunch. Respect nature: take your waste with you, no fire outside authorized areas.

The venue includes a traditional restaurant open at lunch and dinner, ideal for family or group evenings combining a meal with several games of bowling. The cuisine is classic brasserie style, designed for everyday dining and group bookings.

The venue is fully accessible (wheelchair-friendly, audio description on some shows). Plan at least 1h30 on site, up to half a day with planetarium and workshops. Tickets are around €5-7, free under age 6, with a family pass and discounts for Grand XQV0Z residents. Booking advised for the planetarium and holiday workshops. The Passerelles district also offers riverside walks along the Thiou — perfect before or after the visit.
The venue offers

The venue offers outdoor games on forested fields with obstacles, plus possibly urban-style scenarios. Usual scenarios include capture the flag, elimination, zone defence, protect the president, base attack/defence. A typical game lasts 10-15 minutes followed by a debrief, with several games totalling 1h30 to 2h of actual play. Pricing is generally degressive depending on number of paintballs (packages of 100, 200, 300 balls) and players.
The venue stands apart from other gyms thanks to its
The very gentle profile — inherited from the railway — makes it one of the most

The via ferrata hosts no organised events: it is a free-access route integrated into the Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve. Chamonix and XQV0Z guide offices occasionally run collective introduction sessions (summer school holidays) or discovery days combining Bérard with another XQV1Z via. The Reserve Visitor Centre in XQV2Z also offers educational nature activities in July-August.
The via is graded AD+ (Fairly Difficult +): fun yet sporty. It requires good fitness for the steep 20-min approach and proper exposure management. Suitable for accompanied beginners without vertigo.

The via is graded AD+ (Fairly Difficult +): not very physically demanding, but the starting wall and aerial passages require managing exposure. Suitable for accompanied beginners with prior introduction to verticality.
The village also preserves a rural church and chapels typical of Savoyard architecture.
The village car park is nearby and free. The garden is 2 minutes' walk behind the church.
The village centre is the start of several hiking trails through the Brevon and col de Tré-l'Aigle — the garden often serves as a starting or finishing point.

The village of XQV0Z is pedestrian-only. Paid parking is available approximately 300 meters from the XQV1Z. During peak season (July–August), it is recommended to arrive early in the morning to find parking easily.
The village of XQV0Z, its abbey and the Maison du Fromage are 10 min away by car to extend the day.
The visit

The visit circuit includes the remains of the abbatial church, former cellars, gatehouse, medicinal herb garden, medieval vegetable garden and vegetated cloister. An augmented reality application allows visitors to discover the abbey reconstructed in 3D as it was between the 16th and 18th centuries, using a virtual reality headset or digital tablet.
The visit combines well with a lakeside walk, XQV0Z beach, XQV1Z château, the Maison des oiseaux (raptor demonstrations) or a meal at a lakeside restaurant.

The visit highlights the patient gestures of breeders, cheesemakers and master ripeners of the XQV0Z Valley through images, sounds, hands-on stations and reconstructions. Visitors discover the local XQV1Z cow (brown-ringed eyes), her rich milk and the cheese's signature.
The visit is
The visit is laid out in
The visit lasts about 45 minutes to 1h30 depending on your interest and the possible presence of a guide. The museum is modest in size, making it an ideal stop alongside other activities.
The visit offers a permanent trail on the
The visit to the Old Ski Museum at XQV0Z must be booked by SMS on +33 (0)7 62 21 88 68, mentioning "Musée du Ski", at most 2 days before the visit. Verified on 10 June 2026.
The visit unfolds over
The walk is short (30-45 minutes), taken at your own pace through the beds and around the
The walk starts at the entrance to XQV0Z village, at the foot of the
The walk takes
The walking route along the banks of the Arve from XQV0Z to Arthaz is 8.9 km one way, around 2 hours of walking. A 14.5 km loop is possible by taking the other bank on the return.
The walkway runs from the ticket office downstream up to the

The water park features a 25 m swimming pool, a 500 m² balneoform pool (indoor and outdoor by season) heated to 33°, a current river, a fun paddling pool and 9 slides. Climbing is the main feature on the sport side, with one of the largest indoor walls in the Geneva area: 1,500 m² of climbing surface.

The water sports centre at Espace Bernard Pellarin is open from 18 May to 30 August 2026. Public opening hours (outside school group periods in May-June): Monday to Friday 11:15–13:30 and 16:30–19:00, Wednesday and weekends extended hours. Verified on the CCPC website on 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.
The waterfall and its potholes offer excellent photographic opportunities, especially in spring and autumn when water flow is high. The site is particularly photogenic after rainfall.

The waterfall can be visited year-round. Its flow is most impressive in spring (April–May) during snowmelt. In summer, the flow decreases but the site becomes a popular swimming destination with its natural pool and shaded forest areas. Autumn offers a peaceful atmosphere with seasonal colors. The site can be crowded on weekends and during school holidays.

The waterfall consists of several water falls that flow into potholes known as the "Chartreux potholes", naturally carved into the rock. At the base of the waterfall, a Chartreux cross is engraved in the stone, marking the former boundary of Carthusian monk territory. The trail follows the Diomaz stream through the forest, with several stream crossings via footbridges or at fords.

The waterfall is fed by the Arpenaz torrent, also called Nant d'Arpenaz or La Laiteuse, which descends from the Platé massif from an altitude of 1,850 m and travels 4 km before joining the Arve. The name "Arpenaz" comes from the Celtic prefix "Ar" (flowing water) and the suffix "pen" (rocky head).
The waterfall transforms into an ice climbing site in winter. Practice reserved for experienced and guided climbers. Winter access is difficult due to snow cover.
The waymarking for the alpine-pasture trail uses a dedicated logo, 'l'Alpagiste', painted on the signposts — easy to follow for walkers.

To the south, the Philibert d'Orlye promenade runs along the lake shore to Menthon harbour. Views extend to the Semnoz mountain range stretching across the water, Leschaux pass and Entrevernes mountain. During high season, the beach is supervised by lifeguards. Today only
Today only a few wall sections of the Ruins of the castle of XQV0Z remain, along with a memorial monument erected in memory of the young victims of the Christmas 1943 massacre.

Today only a single fragment of the enclosure wall survives, leaning against the back of the presbytery in the village centre. Pierced with arrow slits and built of rough-cut stone, this short stretch of wall is the only physical reminder of the medieval fortress of XQV0Z and of the dramatic episode in XQV1Z–Savoy relations that preceded the final union of the region under Savoy.
Today only the base of the south tower and a wall section pierced by four arrow slits remain of the Saint-Michel castle, lost in the forest above the Le Lac hamlet in XQV0Z.

Together with Albigny Beach, XQV0Z is one of only two entirely free beaches in XQV1Z. It benefits from a prime location near the water sports center, tennis courts, the Brise Glace concert hall, and the Marquisats site, which houses the superior art and design school (ESAAA). To the north lies XQV2Z's water sports base where canoes, kayaks, sailboats, and divers operate — the launch zone is off-limits for swimming.

What distinguishes Plaine-Joux from other free flight sites in XQV0Z is its unique combination of accessibility (road to takeoff), stable aerological conditions thanks to its south-southeast orientation, and one of the most beautiful alpine views from the launch area. Several professional paragliding schools are located directly on-site, ensuring optimal supervision for beginners and experienced pilots seeking to improve cross-country skills.

What remains in XQV0Z is a curtain wall set against the parish rectory, on which arrow slits can still be made out. It is the last visible vestige of the 92 m-long fortress built in 1304 by Hugh of XQV1Z, dismantled after the 1564 fire to be used as a stone quarry.

What sets Indiana'Ventures apart from many Chablais treetop parks is its unique setting beside a natural temperate mountain lake at 900 m altitude. Unlike purely forest parks, families here can alternate morning climbing with afternoon swimming without changing location. The continuous safety line system, on-site professional height safety staff, and graduated offering (from Little Kid to black course) welcome the whole family, from 2-year-old toddlers to adults seeking thrills. The address '1000 route de la plage' reflects the site's multi-activity vocation: lake, beach, forest and leisure coexist harmoniously.

XQV0Z can be visited freely on site without reservation for individual visitors. Tickets are purchased at the reception. Group booking is required at 04 50 04 52 63.
XQV0Z can be visited year-round with two distinct faces.
XQV0Z can be visited year-round, with different seasonal interests.

XQV0Z can be visited year-round. June to September offers active pastures and clear trails. December to March is ideal for skiing. October delights with autumn colours. Avoid April-May transition period unless you enjoy late snow.

XQV0Z can be visited year-round. Winter (December to March) is the high season for the cross-country domain: 29 km of skiing trails, snowshoeing, sled dogs, sledging. Snow reserves allow early-season opening and late closing. Nordic nights (Thursday evenings, 10 km lit 6 pm–8:30 pm, €5 per person) attract experienced skiers. Spring (April–May) is a transition period: the historical trail reopens early April with the Maquis Memory building, but snow often persists through May on higher ground. Summer (June–September) offers the best conditions for hiking, mountain biking, wildlife watching and discovering the historical heritage. Alpine pastures are in flower, high-altitude restaurants open, views are clear. Autumn (October–November) is quieter: the Maquis Memory building closes end September, but the plateau remains accessible. Autumn colours of coniferous forests and low-angle light appeal to photographers. The plateau is frequented year-round by locals for endurance sports (trail, cycling) and remains a place of reflection and remembrance in all seasons.

XQV0Z can be visited year-round. Winter (December–March) for cross-country skiing and snowshoeing; summer (June–September) for hiking, mountain biking, discovering the historical heritage and observing alpine fauna within the Natura 2000 site.

XQV0Z in XQV1Z: 750 m² entirely dedicated to bouldering, kids' area from age 4, bar and snack.
XQV0Z in XQV1Z: multi-level arena, 15-20 min games, birthdays, stag/hen parties. 15 min from Geneva.

XQV0Z is 10 minutes walk from the old town along the lake. By bus, take line 6, 'Marquisats' stop directly across from the beach. By bike, use the cycle path alongside the beach.
XQV0Z is 5-10 min from Évian and Thonon, reachable by car (free parking) or CCPEVA bus. The Lake Geneva greenway runs nearby.

XQV0Z is a challenging and exposed hike, reserved for experienced hikers accustomed to mountain terrain. The final section, after the refuge, is particularly technical with scree and steep poorly-marked passages. Beginners can hike to the XQV1Z refuge (2,002 m) to enjoy the panorama without continuing to the summit.

XQV0Z is a family business created in 1994 by the Fontaine family. Christian, David and Steeve provide educational supervision and share their tips to become a real driver. Friendly atmosphere and personalized welcome.
XQV0Z is a free public space
XQV0Z is a multiplex in XQV1Z, XQV2Z: multiplexe Megarama à XQV3Z, modern equipment (immersive sound, digital laser projection), OV/dubbed shows depending on programme. Programme refreshed weekly.

XQV0Z is a multiplex in XQV1Z, XQV2Z: multiplexe au centre d'XQV3Z (environ 8 salles), modern equipment (immersive sound, digital laser projection), OV/dubbed shows depending on programme. Programme refreshed weekly.
XQV0Z is a multiplex in the heart of XQV1Z with several digital, surround-sound auditoriums. Chablais multiplex — mainstream, art-house, OV, family

XQV0Z is a next-generation multiplex right next to the Swiss border, with a large IMAX auditorium, a 4DX room (motion seats + effects), Dolby Atmos screen
